source.subject: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)
Attachments: 2026-01-15-controlled-failover-metrics-snapshot.pdf, gameday-action-items-2026-01-16.csv

Team — sharing a concise leadership readout from Thursday’s controlled regional failover (us-east → eu-west) and what we’re doing next.

TL;DR
- The core automation path worked end-to-end (signal → controller decision → routing policy flip → traffic shift → stabilization).
- Customer impact was limited but not zero: brief elevated 5xx/timeouts for a small slice of Hosted traffic + streaming disconnects for some clients that don’t handle redirects cleanly.
- We found several “must-fix before wider rollout” items (anti-flap tuning, dashboard annotations, streaming reconnect guidance, and capacity headroom guardrails).

What we tested
- Scenario: planned impairment simulation in us-east and controlled failover to eu-west for a scoped set of routes/models and a limited tenant allowlist.
- Objective: validate (1) detection + decisioning speed, (2) safe automation/rollback behavior, (3) observability + on-call usability, and (4) Dedicated protection (no unexpected pool drain).

Results against success criteria (high level)
- Detection + failover time:
  - First corroborated impairment signal observed: ~T+75s from impairment start.
  - Policy flip executed: ~T+2m40s.
  - Traffic settled in eu-west: ~T+4–5m (client retry/backoff dominated tail).
- Error/latency impact:
  - Hosted API: short spike in 5xx/timeouts during the shift window; elevated tail latency in eu-west for ~12–18 minutes as retries + warm-up converged.
  - Dedicated: no unplanned customer pool drain; no sustained SLO-relevant impact observed.
- Safety/guardrails:
  - No uncontrolled oscillation, but we did see an automated rollback trigger earlier than intended due to hold-down/threshold interaction (details below).

Customer impact / support signals
- One customer reported transient 5xx/timeouts during the planned window (SUP-25310).
- Streaming disconnects were reported by a couple customers whose clients did not gracefully reconnect on redirect (SUP-25322).
- Follow-on ticket on elevated tail latency in eu-west immediately post-shift (SUP-25341) — correlates with headroom + retry storm.

What went well
- On-call coordination and go/no-go discipline were strong (checklist completion and rollback readiness were clear).
- Region health aggregation + policy engine evaluation produced explainable decisions (we could point to which signals tripped).
- Dedicated isolation worked as expected (Hosted shift did not inadvertently trigger Dedicated drain behaviors).

Issues found (key)
1) Observability gap: dashboards lacked consistent automated annotations for: impairment start, policy flip, rollback evaluation points. This made timeline reconstruction slower than it should be.
2) Anti-flap / rollback tuning: hold-down timer and corroboration windows were too aggressive in one segment, resulting in an early rollback evaluation while the region was still stabilizing.
3) Tail latency in eu-west after shift: headroom assumptions were optimistic for the specific routes/models in scope; combined with client retry behavior this caused a brief tail spike.
4) Streaming client behavior: redirect/reconnect handling still causes user-visible disconnects for some SDK versions / client patterns.

Decisions / next steps
- We are NOT expanding automation scope until the above items are closed and we pass a smaller-blast-radius rehearsal.
- We will schedule Game Day #2 with tighter allowlist + synthetic load shaping, and explicitly measure: failover time, retry amplification, and “time to steady-state p99”.

Action items (short list; full tracker linked)
- Add automation-driven dashboard annotations + incident timeline hooks (Owner: Sean / Observability; target: end of Jan)
- Retune rollback/hold-down thresholds and add “stabilization grace” logic (Owner: Rafael + Routing; target: end of Jan)
- Capacity guardrail: require minimum eu-west headroom for routes in allowlist before executing flip (Owner: Irene + Fleet; target: early Feb)
- Streaming: finalize redirect-safe reconnect behavior + publish SDK guidance (Owner: Priya + Runtime/Gateway; target: early Feb)
- Comms: align Support macros + status-page language for planned tests (Owner: Sean; target: before next test)

Links
- Results page (metrics + timeline): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Controlled+Failover+Test+Results+-+2026-01-15
- Draft postmortem (focus on regressions + prevention): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Postmortem%3A+Controlled+Failover+Test+Regression
- Action item tracker (ENG-2426): https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2426/post-gameday-action-items

Asks
1) Approval to hold Game Day #2 in early Feb (exact date TBD) once the above gating items are closed.
2) Confirmation that we should proactively notify the same set of enterprise accounts as last time (Support has the list), or expand/contract based on risk appetite.

Thanks to everyone who helped make the test controlled and safe — we learned the right things without a large blast radius.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:03 AM Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

This is the right framing.

Two adds:
- Let’s treat “dashboard annotations” as a hard gate for the next test. We lost time during the live window correlating graphs to events.
- Support comms: I’d prefer we keep proactive notifications limited to the same cohort (largest Hosted + Dedicated accounts + anyone with data residency questions) until streaming reconnect guidance is fully out.

I’ll sync with Rafael on gating criteria and get a proposed Feb window on the calendar once ENG-2426 is green.

— Sean
Sean Gallagher
Director, Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Thanks, Ava.

A couple questions before I approve another production test window:
1) Were there any indications we violated (or came close to violating) the multi-region availability SLO budget during the test window?
2) What is our plan to ensure the next test doesn’t create a retry amplification event in the target region? (I want this explicitly called out in go/no-go.)

If we can answer those clearly and keep blast radius limited, I’m supportive of early Feb.

— Neha
Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Answers inline.

1) SLO/error budget proximity
- We did not materially burn the multi-region availability SLO budget for the month.
- The spike was brief and scoped; the rolling burn-rate alerts triggered as expected, but did not cross paging thresholds for sustained windows.
- We’ll include the exact numbers in the postmortem once the annotation gap is fixed (so we can line up the windows precisely), but this was within the planned “acceptable test impact” bounds.

2) Preventing retry amplification in the target region (explicit go/no-go)
We’re adding three concrete gates before we run Game Day #2:
- Capacity/headroom gate: require minimum eu-west headroom for the exact route/model allowlist (not just aggregate regional headroom). Irene’s team is helping define thresholds.
- Gateway/backoff gate: validate retry budgets and Retry-After behavior in staging under redirect (Priya’s team + Peter’s circuit breaker changes), with a target “max retry amplification” metric.
- Synthetic load rehearsal: replay a shaped load pattern with perf canaries enabled and confirm p99 stabilization time is within target before touching prod.

If those three are green, we’ll propose an early Feb date with an even smaller allowlist than 1/15.

I’ll also make sure the go/no-go doc includes an explicit rollback trigger tied to retry amplification (not just error burn).

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference

title: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)
Attachments: 2026-01-15-controlled-failover-metrics-snapshot.pdf, gameday-action-items-2026-01-16.csv

Team — sharing a concise leadership readout from Thursday’s controlled regional failover (us-east → eu-west) and what we’re doing next.

TL;DR
- The core automation path worked end-to-end (signal → controller decision → routing policy flip → traffic shift → stabilization).
- Customer impact was limited but not zero: brief elevated 5xx/timeouts for a small slice of Hosted traffic + streaming disconnects for some clients that don’t handle redirects cleanly.
- We found several “must-fix before wider rollout” items (anti-flap tuning, dashboard annotations, streaming reconnect guidance, and capacity headroom guardrails).

What we tested
- Scenario: planned impairment simulation in us-east and controlled failover to eu-west for a scoped set of routes/models and a limited tenant allowlist.
- Objective: validate (1) detection + decisioning speed, (2) safe automation/rollback behavior, (3) observability + on-call usability, and (4) Dedicated protection (no unexpected pool drain).

Results against success criteria (high level)
- Detection + failover time:
  - First corroborated impairment signal observed: ~T+75s from impairment start.
  - Policy flip executed: ~T+2m40s.
  - Traffic settled in eu-west: ~T+4–5m (client retry/backoff dominated tail).
- Error/latency impact:
  - Hosted API: short spike in 5xx/timeouts during the shift window; elevated tail latency in eu-west for ~12–18 minutes as retries + warm-up converged.
  - Dedicated: no unplanned customer pool drain; no sustained SLO-relevant impact observed.
- Safety/guardrails:
  - No uncontrolled oscillation, but we did see an automated rollback trigger earlier than intended due to hold-down/threshold interaction (details below).

Customer impact / support signals
- One customer reported transient 5xx/timeouts during the planned window (SUP-25310).
- Streaming disconnects were reported by a couple customers whose clients did not gracefully reconnect on redirect (SUP-25322).
- Follow-on ticket on elevated tail latency in eu-west immediately post-shift (SUP-25341) — correlates with headroom + retry storm.

What went well
- On-call coordination and go/no-go discipline were strong (checklist completion and rollback readiness were clear).
- Region health aggregation + policy engine evaluation produced explainable decisions (we could point to which signals tripped).
- Dedicated isolation worked as expected (Hosted shift did not inadvertently trigger Dedicated drain behaviors).

Issues found (key)
1) Observability gap: dashboards lacked consistent automated annotations for: impairment start, policy flip, rollback evaluation points. This made timeline reconstruction slower than it should be.
2) Anti-flap / rollback tuning: hold-down timer and corroboration windows were too aggressive in one segment, resulting in an early rollback evaluation while the region was still stabilizing.
3) Tail latency in eu-west after shift: headroom assumptions were optimistic for the specific routes/models in scope; combined with client retry behavior this caused a brief tail spike.
4) Streaming client behavior: redirect/reconnect handling still causes user-visible disconnects for some SDK versions / client patterns.

Decisions / next steps
- We are NOT expanding automation scope until the above items are closed and we pass a smaller-blast-radius rehearsal.
- We will schedule Game Day #2 with tighter allowlist + synthetic load shaping, and explicitly measure: failover time, retry amplification, and “time to steady-state p99”.

Action items (short list; full tracker linked)
- Add automation-driven dashboard annotations + incident timeline hooks (Owner: Sean / Observability; target: end of Jan)
- Retune rollback/hold-down thresholds and add “stabilization grace” logic (Owner: Rafael + Routing; target: end of Jan)
- Capacity guardrail: require minimum eu-west headroom for routes in allowlist before executing flip (Owner: Irene + Fleet; target: early Feb)
- Streaming: finalize redirect-safe reconnect behavior + publish SDK guidance (Owner: Priya + Runtime/Gateway; target: early Feb)
- Comms: align Support macros + status-page language for planned tests (Owner: Sean; target: before next test)

Links
- Results page (metrics + timeline): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Controlled+Failover+Test+Results+-+2026-01-15
- Draft postmortem (focus on regressions + prevention): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Postmortem%3A+Controlled+Failover+Test+Regression
- Action item tracker (ENG-2426): https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2426/post-gameday-action-items

Asks
1) Approval to hold Game Day #2 in early Feb (exact date TBD) once the above gating items are closed.
2) Confirmation that we should proactively notify the same set of enterprise accounts as last time (Support has the list), or expand/contract based on risk appetite.

Thanks to everyone who helped make the test controlled and safe — we learned the right things without a large blast radius.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:03 AM Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

This is the right framing.

Two adds:
- Let’s treat “dashboard annotations” as a hard gate for the next test. We lost time during the live window correlating graphs to events.
- Support comms: I’d prefer we keep proactive notifications limited to the same cohort (largest Hosted + Dedicated accounts + anyone with data residency questions) until streaming reconnect guidance is fully out.

I’ll sync with Rafael on gating criteria and get a proposed Feb window on the calendar once ENG-2426 is green.

— Sean
Sean Gallagher
Director, Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Thanks, Ava.

A couple questions before I approve another production test window:
1) Were there any indications we violated (or came close to violating) the multi-region availability SLO budget during the test window?
2) What is our plan to ensure the next test doesn’t create a retry amplification event in the target region? (I want this explicitly called out in go/no-go.)

If we can answer those clearly and keep blast radius limited, I’m supportive of early Feb.

— Neha
Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Answers inline.

1) SLO/error budget proximity
- We did not materially burn the multi-region availability SLO budget for the month.
- The spike was brief and scoped; the rolling burn-rate alerts triggered as expected, but did not cross paging thresholds for sustained windows.
- We’ll include the exact numbers in the postmortem once the annotation gap is fixed (so we can line up the windows precisely), but this was within the planned “acceptable test impact” bounds.

2) Preventing retry amplification in the target region (explicit go/no-go)
We’re adding three concrete gates before we run Game Day #2:
- Capacity/headroom gate: require minimum eu-west headroom for the exact route/model allowlist (not just aggregate regional headroom). Irene’s team is helping define thresholds.
- Gateway/backoff gate: validate retry budgets and Retry-After behavior in staging under redirect (Priya’s team + Peter’s circuit breaker changes), with a target “max retry amplification” metric.
- Synthetic load rehearsal: replay a shaped load pattern with perf canaries enabled and confirm p99 stabilization time is within target before touching prod.

If those three are green, we’ll propose an early Feb date with an even smaller allowlist than 1/15.

I’ll also make sure the go/no-go doc includes an explicit rollback trigger tied to retry amplification (not just error burn).

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Intermittent timeouts & 5xxs from Hightower - us-west egress instability
messages:
From: Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Hi Rafael,

We started seeing a spike in 5xx responses and timeouts from Redwood's generate endpoint around 06:30 PST this morning. Impacting live checkout inference: pages 10–20% error rate and lots of slow responses (~3–6s increased latency). We haven't changed our client code.

Sample request IDs and timestamps attached in the JSON (customer-logs-sample.json). The errors are mainly 524/connection-timeout and occasional 502s. The traffic is routed to the us-west model pool.

Can you confirm if anything is happening on your side (peering, NAT exhaustion, proxy resets, or DNS changes)? This is causing checkout slowdowns for a subset of sessions. We're on a renewal discussion so this is urgent.

Thanks,
Amy Liu
Sr. SRE, Hightower Retail

Attachments: customer-logs-sample.json (snippet)
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 09:28:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Amy —

Thanks for the report and the logs. I've looped in Ben (SRE) and Evelyn (infra engineering) for immediate investigation and Marissa for customer ops/exec visibility.

First steps from our side:
- Ben & Evelyn: please capture a traceroute/ping from our us-west egress to the Hightower NAT and check for packet loss and path anomalies.
- We'll check recent peering announcements, route flaps, and DNS TTL changes for the customer domain and Redwood egress.
- Can you paste 2–3 example request IDs and exact UTC timestamps (to the second) from the sample file? That will help us correlate.

If helpful we can stand up a short bridge (20–30m) to work through traces — happy to do that now.

Rafael
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference | rafael@redwood.com
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Team — quick initial findings from the us-west egress plane and the traces Amy provided:

- I ran parallel traceroutes and a mtr to the customer's NAT IPs. We're seeing intermittent path shifts and elevated RTTs (~80–120ms spikes) with ~3–6% transient packet loss coincident with the error windows.
- The path shift correlates with an intermediate VPC peering hop in us-west-2 where BGP picture shows an upstream flap for ~60s at 06:29–06:30 PST.
- DNS: noticed short TTL churning on the customer's lb host (their infrastructure team made a routing announcement earlier today and TTLs went from 60 to 10s for a small subset). That likely increased DNS lookups and exposed our egress proxies to rapid cache churn.
- Proxy/failover: Our connection logs show repeated TCP RSTs between our proxy cluster and the customer's NAT during the same windows. That fits a combined peering + DNS churn scenario.

Attached: path-trace-20260115.txt and a pcap snippet (egress-pcap-jan15.pcap) — contains SYN/RST bursts and duplicated IPs in the handshake phase.

Short mitigation options (recommendation first):
1) Fail traffic for Hightower account to our us-east region for the next 60 minutes while we validate peering stability (quickest to reduce customer impact).
2) Increase proxy connection timeout and enable sticky DNS caching for this account for 30 minutes to blunt TTL-induced churn.
3) Coordinate with Hightower to confirm they will not roll additional DNS changes for the next hour and share their routing change details.

If we want to avoid full region failover, option 2 (sticky DNS + proxy delay) is lower risk and can be rolled immediately.

Ben
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference

Attachments: path-trace-20260115.txt, egress-pcap-jan15.pcap
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 11:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Team & Amy —

Thanks for the rapid triage. Rafael, please own communications to the Hightower execs while we work the mitigation. Ben: prioritize sticky-DNS + proxy timeout change first and confirm any customer-visible impact (cold caches, possible brief re-auth). Evelyn: please prepare a rollback plan in case the proxy tweak unexpectedly increases tail latencies.

Amy — I want to acknowledge this is high priority. I've looped our customer success director offline and will escalate to our COO if the region failover is required. We'll also prepare a credit proposal if required after we stabilize.

Proposed next steps (customer-facing):
- Immediate: Ben applies sticky-DNS + increase proxy timeout (target: within 15m).
- 30m: monitor for error rate drop to <1% and latency to baseline.
- 60m: if unstable, we will shift Hightower traffic to us-east and continue investigation; we will coordinate the DNS change freeze with your infra team.

I'll send an exec note once we have the 15m signal.

Marissa Cole
VP, Customer Operations | Redwood Inference
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>, Kevin Zhou <kevin.z@hightowerretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west) — mitigation update

Amy / Kevin —

Quick update following the mitigation work:
- We applied sticky-DNS and increased proxy connection timeouts for the Hightower account at 10:20 PST. Error rate dropped from ~12% to ~0.8% across monitored routes within 18 minutes. Latency returned to baseline for 95% of requests.
- We did not need to fail traffic to us-east. Ben's team will keep the temporary DNS caching and proxy timeouts for another 90 minutes and then revert to normal after validation.
- Root cause: combination of a transient BGP flap on an upstream peer (causing path oscillation) plus an aggressive customer-side DNS TTL change that increased cache churn on our egress proxies. The TCP RST bursts in the attached pcap indicate repeated half-open handshakes during the flap.

Next steps / ask from Hightower:
1) Please confirm you will hold DNS/routing changes for 2 hours so we can revert and verify stable behavior.
2) Share any peering/routing notes from your infra team for the 06:20–06:40 PST window (this helps our postmortem).
3) We'll schedule a 30-minute postmortem tomorrow to review learnings and discuss a more robust mitigation (e.g., per-account egress steering or longer TTL pinning for checkout routes).

As a goodwill gesture we will credit X% of the impacted usage during the outage window — Marissa will follow up on the credit/contract side.

Thanks for the patience and for sending the log samples — that sped identification.

Rafael
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: path-trace-20260115.txt, customer-logs-sample.json, egress-pcap-jan15.pcap

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Team — quick initial findings from the us-west egress plane and the traces Amy provided:

- I ran parallel traceroutes and a mtr to the customer's NAT IPs. We're seeing intermittent path shifts and elevated RTTs (~80–120ms spikes) with ~3–6% transient packet loss coincident with the error windows.
- The path shift correlates with an intermediate VPC peering hop in us-west-2 where BGP picture shows an upstream flap for ~60s at 06:29–06:30 PST.
- DNS: noticed short TTL churning on the customer's lb host (their infrastructure team made a routing announcement earlier today and TTLs went from 60 to 10s for a small subset). That likely increased DNS lookups and exposed our egress proxies to rapid cache churn.
- Proxy/failover: Our connection logs show repeated TCP RSTs between our proxy cluster and the customer's NAT during the same windows. That fits a combined peering + DNS churn scenario.

Attached: path-trace-20260115.txt and a pcap snippet (egress-pcap-jan15.pcap) — contains SYN/RST bursts and duplicated IPs in the handshake phase.

Short mitigation options (recommendation first):
1) Fail traffic for Hightower account to our us-east region for the next 60 minutes while we validate peering stability (quickest to reduce customer impact).
2) Increase proxy connection timeout and enable sticky DNS caching for this account for 30 minutes to blunt TTL-induced churn.
3) Coordinate with Hightower to confirm they will not roll additional DNS changes for the next hour and share their routing change details.

If we want to avoid full region failover, option 2 (sticky DNS + proxy delay) is lower risk and can be rolled immediately.

Ben
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference

Attachments: path-trace-20260115.txt, egress-pcap-jan15.pcap


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 11:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Team & Amy —

Thanks for the rapid triage. Rafael, please own communications to the Hightower execs while we work the mitigation. Ben: prioritize sticky-DNS + proxy timeout change first and confirm any customer-visible impact (cold caches, possible brief re-auth). Evelyn: please prepare a rollback plan in case the proxy tweak unexpectedly increases tail latencies.

Amy — I want to acknowledge this is high priority. I've looped our customer success director offline and will escalate to our COO if the region failover is required. We'll also prepare a credit proposal if required after we stabilize.

Proposed next steps (customer-facing):
- Immediate: Ben applies sticky-DNS + increase proxy timeout (target: within 15m).
- 30m: monitor for error rate drop to <1% and latency to baseline.
- 60m: if unstable, we will shift Hightower traffic to us-east and continue investigation; we will coordinate the DNS change freeze with your infra team.

I'll send an exec note once we have the 15m signal.

Marissa Cole
VP, Customer Operations | Redwood Inference


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>, Kevin Zhou <kevin.z@hightowerretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west) — mitigation update

Amy / Kevin —

Quick update following the mitigation work:
- We applied sticky-DNS and increased proxy connection timeouts for the Hightower account at 10:20 PST. Error rate dropped from ~12% to ~0.8% across monitored routes within 18 minutes. Latency returned to baseline for 95% of requests.
- We did not need to fail traffic to us-east. Ben's team will keep the temporary DNS caching and proxy timeouts for another 90 minutes and then revert to normal after validation.
- Root cause: combination of a transient BGP flap on an upstream peer (causing path oscillation) plus an aggressive customer-side DNS TTL change that increased cache churn on our egress proxies. The TCP RST bursts in the attached pcap indicate repeated half-open handshakes during the flap.

Next steps / ask from Hightower:
1) Please confirm you will hold DNS/routing changes for 2 hours so we can revert and verify stable behavior.
2) Share any peering/routing notes from your infra team for the 06:20–06:40 PST window (this helps our postmortem).
3) We'll schedule a 30-minute postmortem tomorrow to review learnings and discuss a more robust mitigation (e.g., per-account egress steering or longer TTL pinning for checkout routes).

As a goodwill gesture we will credit X% of the impacted usage during the outage window — Marissa will follow up on the credit/contract side.

Thanks for the patience and for sending the log samples — that sped identification.

Rafael
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: path-trace-20260115.txt, customer-logs-sample.json, egress-pcap-jan15.pcap
text:
From: Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Hi Rafael,

We started seeing a spike in 5xx responses and timeouts from Redwood's generate endpoint around 06:30 PST this morning. Impacting live checkout inference: pages 10–20% error rate and lots of slow responses (~3–6s increased latency). We haven't changed our client code.

Sample request IDs and timestamps attached in the JSON (customer-logs-sample.json). The errors are mainly 524/connection-timeout and occasional 502s. The traffic is routed to the us-west model pool.

Can you confirm if anything is happening on your side (peering, NAT exhaustion, proxy resets, or DNS changes)? This is causing checkout slowdowns for a subset of sessions. We're on a renewal discussion so this is urgent.

Thanks,
Amy Liu
Sr. SRE, Hightower Retail

Attachments: customer-logs-sample.json (snippet)

Rafael

messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Laura Chen <laura@healthyops.com>
Cc: Ajay Varma <procurement@healthyops.com>
Date: Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 09:15 AM -0800
Subject: HealthyOps — Handoff & implementation context (SOW + MOP attached)

Hi team,

Happy Monday — quick handoff for HealthyOps (closing notes below). I’m looping CS + implementation to kick off planning.

Short context:
- ARR: ~$420k (3-year term signed 2026-01-10)
- Go-live target: Mar 31, 2026 (pilot -> prod by end of Q1)
- Primary contacts: Laura Chen (Platform), Ajay Varma (procurement)
- Key requirements: private VPC deployment (US), SAML SSO, SOC2 evidence requested, data retention 12 months, high-throughput embedding workloads (expected ~80–120M tokens/mo during pilot ramp)

Attached: Signed_SOW_HealthyOps_20260110.pdf, Implementation_MOP_v1.xlsx

Asks for CS/Implementation: please confirm owner assignments below and propose 2x kickoff windows (week of Jan 19). Also flag any blockers (legal, security, infra) we should escalate.

Recommended owners (sales-side):
- Primary Implementation Lead: Karthik Iyer
- Onboarding PM: Vanessa Ortiz
- CS Exec Sponsor: Monica Patel
- Escalation / Ops: Tessa Morgan

Rafael
Rafael Mendes | Account Executive, Enterprise 
Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

--
On attachments: Implementation_MOP_v1.xlsx contains a tentative week-by-week sprint for pilot. If you want, I can convert to a shared Google Sheet once owners confirm.

From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Chen <laura@healthyops.com>, Ajay Varma <procurement@healthyops.com>
Date: Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 10:03 AM -0800
Subject: Re: HealthyOps — Handoff & implementation context (SOW + MOP attached)

Thanks Rafael — welcome, HealthyOps team. 

I’ll take CS sponsor and coordinate the kickoff. Karthik and Vanessa — please confirm you can take the assignments; I’ll draft a kickoff agenda and intake spreadsheet.

Minimum intake items I need before we lock a technical kickoff:
1) Sandbox credentials (or a temporary API token + test dataset)
2) SAML IdP metadata or contact for SSO setup (IdP vendor + admin contact)
3) Confirmation of data residency region (you listed US — please confirm east/west)
4) Expected concurrency and peak QPS during pilot (approximate)
5) Any compliance evidence beyond SOC2 (PCI, HIPAA notes if applicable)

Proposed kickoff windows (PT):
- Wed Jan 21, 10:00–11:00am PT
- Thu Jan 22, 9:00–10:00am PT

If either works for Laura/Ajay I’ll send a calendar invite and a short agenda (30 min intro + 30 min tech deep-dive). Rafael — I have a couple of onboarding credit questions (one-time courtesy ask from sales) — can we sync briefly?

Regards,
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
monica@redwood.com

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 9:15 AM Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com> wrote:
> [quoted message omitted]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Laura Chen <laura@healthyops.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 08:26 AM -0800
Subject: Re: HealthyOps — Handoff & implementation context (SOW + MOP attached)

Monica — confirmed, I’ll be primary impl lead. Quick technical checklist and asks so we can scope the kickoff properly: 

Technical intake (high priority):
- Sandbox API endpoint + credentials (token or key). If you prefer, we can spin a test tenant on our hosted infra for integration tests.
- A representative sample dataset (JSON lines) and schema for embedding tasks — we need to validate tokenization and sequence length distributions.
- SAML IdP metadata (or a test IdP) and test user that can be used during the kickoff.
- Network constraints for private VPC: will HealthyOps provide VPN/IP allowlist, or do they want us to stand up an outbound peering?
- Expected peak concurrent requests and average sequence length so I can recommend node sizing / batch profiles.

Pre-kickoff ops ask to Tessa: we may need an initial burst reservation (Dedicated pool) for pilot to avoid cold-start throttles. Can you flag availability and lead time for a temporary reservation (2–4 weeks)?

I’ll draft a short tech-runbook and include a preliminary test plan. Assuming we get sandbox creds and sample data by EOD Jan 14, we can run pre-kickoff validations and hit the Jan 21 window.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer | Implementation Lead, Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 10:03 AM Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com> wrote:
> [quoted message omitted]

From: Laura Chen <laura@healthyops.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Cc: Ajay Varma <procurement@healthyops.com>, James Park <james@healthyops.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 15, 2026 at 11:22 AM -0800
Subject: Re: HealthyOps — Handoff & implementation context (SOW + MOP attached)

Thanks all — appreciate the clear handoff. A couple of items from our side:

1) Sandbox access — attached file "sandbox_credentials.txt" contains a temporary token + endpoint for initial validation (expires in 14 days).
2) Security evidence — attaching our security questionnaire (we had a few specific questions about KMS and audit logs). James Park (cc’d) is our security lead and will work with your security/infra team if needed.
3) SAML: we use Okta; I’ll share IdP metadata during the kickoff.
4) Procurement: Ajay is on copy; we expect final procurement sign-off within 72 hours for the onboarding fee.

Proposed kickoff: Jan 21, 10:00am PT works for our engineering and platform leads. I’ve blocked calendar time tentatively — please send an invite.

Laura Chen
Head of Platform, HealthyOps
laura@healthyops.com | +1 (617) 555-0147

Attachments: sandbox_credentials.txt (token + endpoint), healthyops_security_questionnaire.pdf

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Chen <laura@healthyops.com>, Ajay Varma <procurement@healthyops.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 15, 2026 at 02:02 PM -0800
Subject: Re: HealthyOps — Handoff & implementation context (SOW + MOP attached)

Great — Jan 21, 10:00am PT confirmed. Monica, please send the invite with the agenda and the shared intake sheet.

Quick sales-side note: we discussed a one-time 10% onboarding credit during contract negotiations as a courtesy for faster time-to-value. Can you confirm if that’s within CS/finance guidelines or if Tessa should escalate to SalesOps? If we expect procurement to hold onboarding beyond 5 business days, I’d like us to apply the credit proactively to keep momentum.

If anything urgent comes up before the kickoff (sandbox errors, missing artifacts), ping me directly.

Thanks all — excited to get HealthyOps into pilot.

Rafael
Rafael Mendes | Account Executive, Enterprise
Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.com

On Thu, Jan 15, 2026 at 11:22 AM Laura Chen <laura@healthyops.com> wrote:
> [quoted message omitted]

Subject: HealthyOps — Handoff & implementation context (SOW + MOP attached)

Hi team,

Happy Monday — quick handoff for HealthyOps (closing notes below). I’m looping CS + implementation to kick off planning.

Short context:
- ARR: ~$420k (3-year term signed 2026-01-10)
- Go-live target: Mar 31, 2026 (pilot -> prod by end of Q1)
- Primary contacts: Laura Chen (Platform), Ajay Varma (procurement)
- Key requirements: private VPC deployment (US), SAML SSO, SOC2 evidence requested, data retention 12 months, high-throughput embedding workloads (expected ~80–120M tokens/mo during pilot ramp)

Attached: Signed_SOW_HealthyOps_20260110.pdf, Implementation_MOP_v1.xlsx

Asks for CS/Implementation: please confirm owner assignments below and propose 2x kickoff windows (week of Jan 19). Also flag any blockers (legal, security, infra) we should escalate.

Recommended owners (sales-side):
- Primary Implementation Lead: Karthik Iyer
- Onboarding PM: Vanessa Ortiz
- CS Exec Sponsor: Monica Patel
- Escalation / Ops: Tessa Morgan

Rafael
Rafael Mendes | Account Executive, Enterprise 
Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

--
On attachments: Implementation_MOP_v1.xlsx contains a tentative week-by-week sprint for pilot. If you want, I can convert to a shared Google Sheet once owners confirm.

I’ll take CS sponsor and coordinate the kickoff. Karthik and Vanessa — please confirm you can take the assignments; I’ll draft a kickoff agenda and intake spreadsheet.

Minimum intake items I need before we lock a technical kickoff:
1) Sandbox credentials (or a temporary API token + test dataset)
2) SAML IdP metadata or contact for SSO setup (IdP vendor + admin contact)
3) Confirmation of data residency region (you listed US — please confirm east/west)
4) Expected concurrency and peak QPS during pilot (approximate)
5) Any compliance evidence beyond SOC2 (PCI, HIPAA notes if applicable)

Proposed kickoff windows (PT):
- Wed Jan 21, 10:00–11:00am PT
- Thu Jan 22, 9:00–10:00am PT

If either works for Laura/Ajay I’ll send a calendar invite and a short agenda (30 min intro + 30 min tech deep-dive). Rafael — I have a couple of onboarding credit questions (one-time courtesy ask from sales) — can we sync briefly?

Regards,

Technical intake (high priority):
- Sandbox API endpoint + credentials (token or key). If you prefer, we can spin a test tenant on our hosted infra for integration tests.
- A representative sample dataset (JSON lines) and schema for embedding tasks — we need to validate tokenization and sequence length distributions.
- SAML IdP metadata (or a test IdP) and test user that can be used during the kickoff.
- Network constraints for private VPC: will HealthyOps provide VPN/IP allowlist, or do they want us to stand up an outbound peering?
- Expected peak concurrent requests and average sequence length so I can recommend node sizing / batch profiles.

Pre-kickoff ops ask to Tessa: we may need an initial burst reservation (Dedicated pool) for pilot to avoid cold-start throttles. Can you flag availability and lead time for a temporary reservation (2–4 weeks)?

I’ll draft a short tech-runbook and include a preliminary test plan. Assuming we get sandbox creds and sample data by EOD Jan 14, we can run pre-kickoff validations and hit the Jan 21 window.

Subject: Subprocessor roster + temporary addition notice

Hi Rafael,

Quick one — procurement and security are collecting a final subprocessor inventory as part of VeloxPay's vendor refresh. Can you please:  

- Share your current subprocessor roster (names, country of processing, purpose, contract/SLA link)
- Confirm any temporary/short-term processors added in the last 90 days and the justification

We received a note from an internal product team that a caching partner (third-party edge-cache) was briefly used during a pilot in December — we need confirmation and any mitigation.

Could you get that over by EOD Tuesday (13 Jan)? If you expect any redlines on the DPA addendum we plan to issue, flag them early.

Thanks,
Samira
Samira Khan
Head of Vendor Risk — VeloxPay

---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@veloxpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 09:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Subprocessor roster + temporary addition notice

Hi Samira — thanks for the heads up. We're compiling the roster and a short summary of the December pilot.

Preliminary items attached: redwood_subprocessor_roster_20260111.xlsx (master roster) and a short DPA addendum draft we've used with similar customers. High level:  
- No permanent subprocessor additions in the last 90 days.
- There was a short pilot (20–22 Dec) that used a third‑party edge cache (internal name: EdgeSwift) to validate regional cache hit rates. It was deployed to a test slice and did not see customer traffic beyond synthetic tests.

I'll loop in Kimberly for encryption / KMS attestation and Marissa for contract changes. Expect a consolidated response + attestation by EOD Tuesday.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Director, Trust & Compliance
Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: redwood_subprocessor_roster_20260111.xlsx (xlsx), dpa_addendum_draft_for_veloxpay_20260112.docx (docx)

To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 11:27:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Subprocessor roster + temporary addition notice

Rafael / team — quick attestation from infra security:  

- KMS/HSM usage: All secrets for the EdgeSwift pilot were stubbed; no production KMS keys were provisioned to the pilot slice.
- Logs & telemetry: Pilot telemetry were routed to a segregated test telemetry bucket (retention 7d) and are excluded from customer logs.
- Data residency: EdgeSwift test instances were in us-west-2 and exclusively processed synthetic requests.

I've attached a short snapshot we normally provide to customers: kms_attestation_snapshot_20260112.pdf. If VeloxPay requires a signed attestation we can escalate to Legal.

-Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Sr. Manager, Infrastructure Security
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: kms_attestation_snapshot_20260112.pdf (pdf)

Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, procurement@veloxpay.com
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 08:09:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Subprocessor roster + temporary addition notice

Hi all — looping in Legal ops context and confirmation on contract posture:  

- EdgeSwift was engaged under a limited pilot MSA addendum (pilot-only terms). We did not sign a new permanent SOW.
- No changes to our published DPA or subprocessor schedule are needed for production. For transparency we're offering to include EdgeSwift in the subprocessor appendix as "pilot-only, non-production" if VeloxPay would like the line item explicitly called out.
- If VeloxPay requests an executed addendum (DocuSign), please confirm scope: "pilot-only visibility" vs "production inclusion".

Rafael — finalizing a slightly redacted roster (remove internal dev contact emails) and will send the final packet in ~90 minutes.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Head of Privacy Programs
Redwood Inference
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@veloxpay.com>, VeloxPay Security <security@veloxpay.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 16:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Subprocessor roster + temporary addition notice

Samira — thanks for the patience. Attached is the finalized packet:  

1) redwood_subprocessor_roster_20260111.xlsx — roster with processing country, purpose, and links to master agreements (we redacted internal-only emails).
2) kms_attestation_snapshot_20260112.pdf — infra attestation for the EdgeSwift pilot.
3) dpa_addendum_draft_for_veloxpay_20260112.docx — suggested language if you prefer pilot-only line items in the DPA appendix.

Summary: no production subprocessor additions; EdgeSwift was pilot-only, processed synthetic data in us-west-2, and did not see customer traffic. If VeloxPay wants EdgeSwift enumerated in the DPA appendix as a pilot-only processor we can provide a short addendum and route it via DocuSign (link below). Otherwise, the roster above reflects the current production roster.

DocuSign (optional addendum): https://www.docusign.net/veloxpay-dpa-addendum-12345

If you want a signed technical attestation, please let us know and we'll have Legal review the attestation PDF.

Director, Trust & Compliance
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Review: customer comms for planned regional failover test (Jan 15) + who to notify
source.body:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen_phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Review: customer comms for planned regional failover test (Jan 15) + who to notify

Team,

Ahead of Thursday’s controlled failover game day (us-east -> eu-west) we need final eyes on the customer comms draft and a decision on which enterprise accounts get proactive outreach vs status page only.

Asks:
1) Please review the attached draft (email + FAQ). Redlines or “don’t say this” are welcome.
2) Reply with the list of enterprise accounts you believe we should proactively notify (CS-owned outreach), and which should get status page only.

Working assumptions (please challenge):
- This is a resiliency test with explicit rollback criteria and on-call approval hooks.
- We will NOT promise zero impact; we will message “no expected downtime” but acknowledge possibility of brief elevated error/latency.
- Dedicated customers: we’ll include a sentence on safeguards (pool drain gates / no cross-tenant mixing) without over-explaining.

Proposed send time: Wed 1/14 10:00 PT to selected accounts. Status page note at start of window.

Draft copy highlights:
- Window: Thu 1/15 10:00–10:30 PT
- What customers might see: short-lived elevated 5xx/timeouts, redirects, streaming reconnects
- What to do: enable retries with backoff; for streaming, reconnect on 307/308; use idempotency keys
- How we’ll communicate: status page updates every 10 min during the window

Attachments:
- Redwood_Planned_Failover_Test_Customer_Email_v3.docx
- Failover_Test_FAQ_v1.md
- Enterprise_Notify_List_2026-01-13.csv (initial cut from CS + SRE)

Thanks,
Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Manager (Failover Automation)
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 10:01 AM Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Owen Phillips <owen_phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 10:01 AM
Subject: Re: Review: customer comms for planned regional failover test (Jan 15) + who to notify

Edits / callouts:
- Strong +1 on avoiding “no customer impact.” Suggest we say: “We do not expect downtime, but you may observe brief increases in error rate and tail latency during traffic steering.”
- Recommend removing the word “incident” anywhere in the planned notice. Customers interpret that as we’re already degraded.
- In the FAQ, the line “failover will redirect traffic automatically” should be “may redirect traffic” (avoid sounding like we’re forcing all customers to move).

Notify list (proactive outreach):
1) Atlas Payments (procurement asked specifically about auditability + HA; they’ll appreciate the heads up)
2) Northstar Health (regulated + data residency questions; they need explicit boundary language)
3) Kestrel Financial (Dedicated, high QPS, historically sensitive to timeouts)
4) Workbench AI (Hosted, heavy streaming usage; they opened tickets on disconnects before)

Status page only:
- Most mid-market; anyone below ~2% of regional traffic / no dedicated SLA addendum.

Also: please include a support contact line that routes to on-call liaison during the window (we can staff it). We got burned last time when questions came into general support and took 45+ minutes to route.

-dev

—
Dev Patel
Support Escalation Owner
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 10:26 AM Owen Phillips <owen_phillips@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Owen Phillips <owen_phillips@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 10:26 AM
Subject: Re: Review: customer comms for planned regional failover test (Jan 15) + who to notify

A few wording tweaks from the CS side:
- Replace “failover test” in subject line with “resiliency test” (less scary).
- Suggest we define the blast radius explicitly: “limited to a subset of routes/models in us-east with controlled ramp.” (We can keep it high-level.)
- Add a line clarifying that customers don’t need to take action unless they see errors; otherwise it reads like required work.

Proactive notify: agree with Dev’s four.
Add:
- Ironclad Legal (Dedicated + strict SLA language; they will ask if this affects credits)

One request: can we add a single sentence about audit trail availability? We’ve had multiple enterprise accounts ask “how will you prove what happened.” Something like: “We record an internal timeline of routing changes and validation checks; your account team can provide a summary on request.”

-Owen

—
Owen Phillips
Support Incident Liaison
Redwood Inference

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen_phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 11:08 AM
Subject: Re: Review: customer comms for planned regional failover test (Jan 15) + who to notify

Dedicated-specific feedback:
- Please avoid any phrasing that implies we’ll “move dedicated tenants across regions” as a default. Some Dedicated contracts assume region pinning.
- For Dedicated, I’d prefer:
  “Dedicated capacity includes safeguards to prevent cascading overload during regional impairments (e.g., controlled drain and traffic shaping).”
  No mention of drain automation unless asked.

Accounts to proactively notify (Dedicated):
- Atlas Payments (even if still in procurement, they’re tracking us closely)
- Kestrel Financial
- Ironclad Legal

Northstar Health: yes, but we should add a data residency note:
- “For customers with regional restrictions, routing policies will respect configured residency boundaries.”

Also: If we’re linking to a status page template, ensure it distinguishes “planned resiliency test” vs “service degradation.”

Lauren

—
Lauren Bishop
Dedicated Product Lead
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 12:17 PM Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen_phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 12:17 PM
Subject: Re: Review: customer comms for planned regional failover test (Jan 15) + who to notify

Hosted API feedback:
- For the “What you might see” section, call out redirects explicitly (307/308) and recommend honoring Location + reusing auth headers.
- Suggest we remove “use idempotency keys” unless we also include a link to docs. Otherwise we’ll get a bunch of “how do I do that?” tickets. Alternate: “If your client supports idempotency keys, enable them for retries.”

Proactive notify (Hosted-heavy):
- Workbench AI (streaming)
- LatticeCRM (very latency-sensitive, has custom alerting wired to tail latency)

If we want a safe default promise, I’d go with: “We are running this test to reduce the impact of future regional impairments.”

Nikhil

—
Nikhil Sharma
Hosted API Product Lead
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 2:38 PM Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:

Decisions / guidance:

1) Proactive outreach: keep it tight and high-signal. Notify:
- Atlas Payments
- Northstar Health
- Kestrel Financial
- Ironclad Legal
- Workbench AI
- LatticeCRM

Everyone else: status page only.

2) Tone: This is a “planned resiliency test” with customer benefit framing. Do not imply we anticipate an incident.

3) Wording to include (approved):
- “We do not expect downtime; however, you may observe brief increases in error rate and tail latency while traffic steering occurs.”
- “For customers with regional restrictions, routing policies will respect configured residency boundaries.”

4) Wording to avoid:
- “Zero impact,” “seamless,” or anything that reads like a guarantee.

5) Escalation: Add a line: “If you experience elevated errors during the window, reply to this email and your account team will engage our on-call reliability lead.” (Do not publish a direct pager number.)

Neha

Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability

title: Review: customer comms for planned regional failover test (Jan 15) + who to notify
preview:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen_phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Review: customer comms for planned regional failover test (Jan 15) + who to notify

Team,

Ahead of Thursday’s controlled failover game day (us-east -> eu-west) we need final eyes on the customer comms draft and a decision on which enterprise accounts get proactive outreach vs status page only.

Asks:
1) Please review the attached draft (email + FAQ). Redlines or “don’t say this” are welcome.
2) Reply with the list of enterprise accounts you believe we should proactively notify (CS-owned outreach), and which should get status page only.

Working assumptions (please challenge):
- This is a resiliency test with explicit rollback criteria and on-call approval hooks.
- We will NOT promise zero impact; we will message “no expected downtime” but acknowledge possibility of brief elevated error/latency.
- Dedicated customers: we’ll include a sentence on safeguards (pool drain gates / no cross-tenant mixing) without over-explaining.

Proposed send time: Wed 1/14 10:00 PT to selected accounts. Status page note at start of window.

Draft copy highlights:
- Window: Thu 1/15 10:00–10:30 PT
- What customers might see: short-lived elevated 5xx/timeouts, redirects, streaming reconnects
- What to do: enable retries with backoff; for streaming, reconnect on 307/308; use idempotency keys
- How we’ll communicate: status page updates every 10 min during the window

Attachments:
- Redwood_Planned_Failover_Test_Customer_Email_v3.docx
- Failover_Test_FAQ_v1.md
- Enterprise_Notify_List_2026-01-13.csv (initial cut from CS + SRE)

Thanks,
Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Manager (Failover Automation)
Redwood Inference

Rafael/Talia: please finalize v4 today and route to CS for Wednesday send. Ensure INT-1142 is in good standing with change approval.

Neha

—
Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Linda Park <linda.park@acme-corp.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: security@acme-corp.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: SIG follow-up: encryption specifics (in transit / at rest / KMS)

Hi Rafael,

Thanks for sending the completed SIG spreadsheet earlier. Our infosec team reviewed the answers and flagged a few follow-ups specifically around encryption and key management before we can close the intake ticket:

- Clarify exactly which TLS versions and cipher suites you accept for platform API endpoints (we need to ensure TLS 1.2+ and AEAD ciphers).
- For data at rest, is envelope encryption used? Who manages DEKs and KEKs?
- Do you offer HSM-backed keys or BYOK for enterprise/private deployments? Any details on how you rotate keys (frequency, automation, and rewrap strategy)?
- Evidence request: architecture diagram showing KMS placement, and your key-rotation policy document.

Can you respond with short, concrete answers and attach the diagram and policy? We are on a tight timeline for our vendor security review (deadline: 2026-01-20).

Thanks,
Linda Park
Senior InfoSec Analyst
Acme Corp

--
Note: please attach only redacted diagrams if there is sensitive internal detail.

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Linda Park <linda.park@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 11:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up: encryption specifics (in transit / at rest / KMS)

Linda — thanks for the clear list. See answers below; I attached a short PDF with the encryption section filled out plus an architecture diagram and our key rotation policy (stubs attached): redwood_sig_encryption_answers.pdf, redwood_kms_arch_diagram.png, kms-rotation-policy_v1.2.pdf.

Quick answers (summary):
- In transit: public API endpoints enforce TLS 1.2 minimum, TLS 1.3 preferred. We publish a cipher-suite policy; active connections use AEAD ciphers (AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305). Mutual TLS is available for Dedicated and Private deployments.

- At rest: we use envelope encryption. Customer data stored in object stores and persistent volumes is encrypted with data encryption keys (DEKs). DEKs are themselves encrypted (wrapped) with KMS-managed key encryption keys (KEKs).

- KMS / HSM: For our hosted environment we use AWS KMS with customer-managed CMKs that are HSM-backed (AWS KMS w/ FIPS 140-2 validated modules). For Private (VPC/on-prem) deployments we support BYOK (imported keys into a vendor KMS or customer-managed HSM) and bring-your-own-HSM options.

- Key rotation: automated rotation of DEKs is performed transparently on a rolling basis; KEKs (CMKs) are rotated at least annually by default (365 days) and can be configured to automatic rotation where supported. Rotation is done via re-wrapping DEKs with the new KEK version; full re-encryption of large volumes is avoided by rotating wrapping keys rather than re-encrypting every object (envelope pattern). For customers who require forced re-encryption, we provide a controlled rekey workflow with staging, verification, and fallback.

- Access control / audit: all KMS operations require least-privilege IAM roles with separation of duties. KMS access is audited; we retain key usage logs in our immutable audit pipeline and can provide exports for SOC 2 evidence.

Evidence attached:
- redwood_sig_encryption_answers.pdf (answers exported from SIG tab for encryption)
- redwood_kms_arch_diagram.png (redacted placement diagram showing KMS and HSM boundaries)
- kms-rotation-policy_v1.2.pdf (internal policy excerpt that describes cadence and emergency rotation process)

If you need any of the attachments in a different format or a version redacted further, happy to produce one. Priya (security) and Neha (engineering) are looped in for any deeper technical follow-ups.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
rafael_mendes@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0132

Attachment stubs: redwood_sig_encryption_answers.pdf (PDF, 42KB), redwood_kms_arch_diagram.png (PNG, 210KB), kms-rotation-policy_v1.2.pdf (PDF, 85KB)

From: Linda Park <linda.park@acme-corp.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: security@acme-corp.com
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 08:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up: encryption specifics (in transit / at rest / KMS)

Rafael — thanks for the quick reply and the attachments. A couple of clarifying questions from our crypto reviewer:

1) When you say KEKs are rotated at least annually, do you support more frequent rotation (e.g., 90 days) for high-security customers? Is the rotation automated or manual for BYOK keys in Private deployments?

2) For the HSM-backed CMKs you referenced, can you confirm the vendor/certification level (e.g., AWS CloudHSM / FIPS 140-2 Level 3, or AWS KMS backed by Nitro with FIPS modules)? We need the exact wording for our risk assessment.

3) During rotation, what is the expected impact window for requests that need the previous KEK version? Is there an automatic fallback to previous key versions while rewrap completes?

If you can answer those three items, we should be able to close the encryption section.

Thanks — appreciate the help.
Linda

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Linda Park <linda.park@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 12:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up: encryption specifics (in transit / at rest / KMS)

Linda — answers inline below. Apologies for the dense reply; trying to be explicit for your reviewer.

1) Rotation cadence: yes — we support customer-configurable KEK rotation frequencies. Default is 365 days, but for enterprise customers we can configure rotation down to 90 days. For Hosted (Redwood-managed) CMKs, rotation is automated and handled by KMS with zero-downtime rewrap of DEKs. For Private/BYO/Imported keys: automation depends on the customer's KMS/HSM capability. If the customer imports keys into a supported HSM with automation APIs, we can wire up the rotation job; otherwise rotation of imported keys is a manual process guided by our ops runbook and executed by the customer's key administrators. We document the difference in the policy (see section 3.2 of kms-rotation-policy_v1.2.pdf).

2) HSM vendor / certification: for our hosted offering we rely on AWS KMS which uses FIPS 140-2 validated HSMs (AWS-managed FIPS modules). For Dedicated we can use customer-controlled CMKs in the same AWS account/partition. For Private on-prem deployments, customers can use AWS CloudHSM or any HSM that supports PKCS#11 import (we commonly see Thales and Utimaco). Exact phrasing you can use in your assessment: "Redwood uses HSM-backed keys via AWS KMS (FIPS 140-2 validated modules). For on-prem/private deployments, Redwood supports customer-managed HSMs (e.g., Thales, Utimaco) with PKCS#11 integration."

3) Impact window and fallback: rotation is implemented as a wrap/unwrap (re-wrapping) operation on DEKs; the runtime consults key metadata and supports key versioning. During a KEK rotation we retain the previous KEK version for a configurable grace period (default 30 days) to ensure in-flight requests and older ciphertexts can still be decrypted. The service automatically attempts decryption using the latest KEK and falls back to previous versions if needed. If a customer requests immediate strict enforcement (no fallback), that can be configured but may require coordination for re-encrypting archived objects.

If you want exact excerpts for your report, Priya can share a short extract from our SOC 2 control narrative and the redacted architecture diagram. Priya / Neha — can you drop a one-paragraph control excerpt we can share with Acme?

Thanks,
Rafael

--
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_kms_arch_diagram.png (redacted diagram), kms-rotation-policy_v1.2.pdf (policy excerpt)

source.subject: Routing + Control Plane readiness review (timeline to enable dry-run in prod)
source.body:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Routing + Control Plane readiness review (timeline to enable dry-run in prod)

Team,

Ahead of next week’s controlled failover window, can we do a quick async readiness pass for routing + control plane so we can enable failover controller *dry-run* safely in prod (no policy flips) and start collecting “would-have-done” evidence?

Target:
- Dry-run enabled in prod by Wed 1/13 (scoped: Hosted API, limited routes/tenants)
- First production game day remains Thu 1/15 (with explicit go/no-go)

I put a checklist + current PR status in this doc:
- Attachment: Routing_ControlPlane_Readiness_Checklist_v4 (Google Doc link)

Here’s the condensed list so we can reply-all with green/yellow/red:

1) Policy evaluation determinism + signal priority
- Confirm deterministic ordering and tie-break behavior (no “random” flips under noisy signals)
- Required: signal priority matches ADR-042 (reachability > 5xx burn > tail latency burn > capacity)
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19812-policy-engine-signal-priority (status?)

2) Failover controller dry-run and auditability
- Dry-run emits recommended actions + evidence signals and writes structured audit event (who/what/why)
- No writes to routing policy stores in dry-run mode
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19814-failover-controller-dry-run (status?)
  - redwood/pr-19824-failover-event-audit-log (status?)

3) Rollback/anti-flap guardrails (even if dry-run)
- We need the config schema + enforcement ready so when we later flip from dry-run -> execute, we’re not “re-learning” safety constraints
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19815-routing-policy-rollback-safety
  - redwood/pr-19833-failover-controller-rate-limits

4) Control plane / routing dependencies
- Region health service endpoint availability + TTL / caching to avoid stampedes during partial outages
- Routing reads region health in a bounded way (timeouts/fallback)
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19810-add-region-health-aggregator-service
  - redwood/pr-19825-region-health-cache-ttl

5) Observability + alerting to support dry-run validation
- Metrics for: dry-run recommendations, trigger source, failover_reason (low-cardinality), confidence/corroboration count
- Burn-rate rules exist but are tuned to avoid paging noise during the dry-run period
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19816-add-multi-region-slo-metrics
  - redwood/pr-19818-alerts-failover-burn-rate
  - observability-pack/pr-907-failover-dashboards-and-alert-rules

6) Gateway + client behavior considerations (so dry-run evidence matches reality)
- Timeouts/retry budgets tuned to avoid masking regional impairment / amplifying spikes
- PR:
  - redwood/pr-19820-api-gateway-fallback-timeout-tuning

7) Infra / DNS routing prerequisites (for later execution / fast detection)
- Confirm DNS TTL plan + rollout guardrails are approved; no change required for dry-run but want us aligned
- PR:
  - redwood-terraform/pr-1442-dns-ttl-and-failover-routing-updates

If each of you can reply with:
- ✅ ready / ⚠️ at risk / 🛑 blocked
- what is needed + date you expect to clear

…we can lock the Wed enablement plan and avoid a last-minute scramble.

Thanks,
Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
Program Operations (Reliability)
Redwood Inference



From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Routing + Control Plane readiness review (timeline to enable dry-run in prod)

✅ from SRE side *for dry-run enablement* assuming the scope stays “Hosted API / limited tenants” and we have two items in place:

1) Dashboards annotated by the controller (even in dry-run)
- We need an explicit marker when a dry-run recommendation would have triggered.

Here’s the condensed list so we can reply-all with green/yellow/red:

1) Policy evaluation determinism + signal priority
- Confirm deterministic ordering and tie-break behavior (no “random” flips under noisy signals)
- Required: signal priority matches ADR-042 (reachability > 5xx burn > tail latency burn > capacity)
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19812-policy-engine-signal-priority (status?)

2) Failover controller dry-run and auditability
- Dry-run emits recommended actions + evidence signals and writes structured audit event (who/what/why)
- No writes to routing policy stores in dry-run mode
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19814-failover-controller-dry-run (status?)
  - redwood/pr-19824-failover-event-audit-log (status?)

3) Rollback/anti-flap guardrails (even if dry-run)
- We need the config schema + enforcement ready so when we later flip from dry-run -> execute, we’re not “re-learning” safety constraints
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19815-routing-policy-rollback-safety
  - redwood/pr-19833-failover-controller-rate-limits

4) Control plane / routing dependencies
- Region health service endpoint availability + TTL / caching to avoid stampedes during partial outages
- Routing reads region health in a bounded way (timeouts/fallback)
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19810-add-region-health-aggregator-service
  - redwood/pr-19825-region-health-cache-ttl

5) Observability + alerting to support dry-run validation
- Metrics for: dry-run recommendations, trigger source, failover_reason (low-cardinality), confidence/corroboration count
- Burn-rate rules exist but are tuned to avoid paging noise during the dry-run period
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19816-add-multi-region-slo-metrics
  - redwood/pr-19818-alerts-failover-burn-rate
  - observability-pack/pr-907-failover-dashboards-and-alert-rules

- PR:
  - redwood/pr-19820-api-gateway-fallback-timeout-tuning

- Confirm DNS TTL plan + rollout guardrails are approved; no change required for dry-run but want us aligned
- PR:
  - redwood-terraform/pr-1442-dns-ttl-and-failover-routing-updates

If each of you can reply with:
- ✅ ready / ⚠️ at risk / 🛑 blocked
- what is needed + date you expect to clear

…we can lock the Wed enablement plan and avoid a last-minute scramble.

Thanks,
Priya

Redwood Inference
