source.subject: Owner mapping dispute: capacity-related incident subtypes (Routing vs Fleet) — decision needed for v1
related_links: Linear: ENG-4825 (control-plane owner mapping config and audit)
Linear: ENG-4823 (add incident type to alert payload and pager routing)
Linear: ENG-4824 (instrument routing fallback reasons for taxonomy)
source.thread_body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Owner mapping dispute: capacity-related incident subtypes (Routing vs Fleet) — decision needed for v1

Sean,

We need a tiebreak decision today to unblock the v1 “incident type -> primary owner” mapping and the control-plane config (ENG-4825).

Dispute: several capacity-adjacent subtypes currently oscillate between Routing (Smart Routing) and Fleet (Infra/Fleet) as the primary owner, depending on who is on-call and what the symptom looked like.

Where it’s showing up:
- Incidents that start as “fallback storms” / elevated 5xx/429 but underlying cause is GPU pool saturation or regional capacity exhaustion.
- Incidents where router admission control sheds load (429) because the local pool is full, but routing is “doing the right thing” per policy.

Two proposed options:
A) Own by symptom: anything involving fallback/admission control defaults to Routing.
B) Own by controllable lever / root cause: capacity scarcity -> Fleet; policy/selection bugs -> Routing.

SRE recommendation is B, but Routing is concerned about being paged for “capacity incidents” and losing fast mitigation loops.

Concrete subtypes in question (from the draft sheet):
- CAPACITY > Regional capacity exhaustion
- CAPACITY > Provisioning lag / scale-up stalled
- CAPACITY > Pool saturation (GPU/utilization)
- ROUTING/FALLBACK > Excessive fallbacks due to capacity signals

Proposed owner mapping if we choose B:
- CAPACITY.* => Primary: Fleet; Secondary/Consulted: Routing (for traffic steering, safe-mode, policy constraints)
- ROUTING/FALLBACK.* => Primary: Routing; Consulted: Fleet (if capacity constraints are suspected)

Decision we need:
1) Which team is primary owner for the “capacity-driven fallback storm” subtype?
2) Can we document a single decision rule for incident commanders to pick the right primary type during an incident?

Attaching the disputed rows + the current draft mapping.

Attachment(s):
- Ownership-Mapping-v1_Disputed-Capacity-Subtypes_2026-01-09.xlsx (spreadsheet)
- incident-taxonomy-v1-owner-mapping-snippet.png (image)

Thanks,
Ava
—
Ava Chen
Platform Operations (Reliability Program)
Redwood Inference


From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 9:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Owner mapping dispute: capacity-related incident subtypes (Routing vs Fleet) — decision needed for v1

+1 on needing a crisp rule, but Routing can’t end up as “consulted” on anything that is manifested as router-triggered 429/5xx.

In the last two incidents, the fastest mitigation was:
- tighten routing policy / safe-mode to reduce thrash
- force regional steering
Fleet changes (adding capacity) were slower.

If we go with B, I’d like:
- a dedicated subtype under ROUTING/FALLBACK that captures “capacity-driven fallback storms” and keeps Routing as primary
OR
- a hard rule that when fallback_reason=capacity AND traffic steering / safe-mode is required, Routing is primary.

Otherwise we’ll lose the first 30 minutes.

Mina


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 10:05 AM PST
Subject: Re: Owner mapping dispute: capacity-related incident subtypes (Routing vs Fleet) — decision needed for v1

Fleet POV: primary ownership should map to who can remove the constraint.

If the router is falling back because the region/pool is out of headroom, that’s capacity. Routing mitigations can reduce blast radius, but they don’t fix the underlying shortage.

If the router is falling back incorrectly (bad health signal, bad policy rollout, wrong compatibility matrix), that’s Routing.

I’m fine with Routing being auto-paged/consulted for CAPACITY incidents (because steering matters), but the primary owner should be Fleet for CAPACITY.*

Vanessa


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Owner mapping dispute: capacity-related incident subtypes (Routing vs Fleet) — decision needed for v1

Decision: choose option B (own by controllable boundary / root cause). We’ll treat “capacity-driven fallback storms” as a CAPACITY incident for primary ownership.

Rationale:
- Primary owner must align to the team accountable for preventing recurrence (forecasting, provisioning, pool sizing, regional capacity posture).
- Routing absolutely remains critical for mitigation; we’ll formalize it as required secondary/consulted for CAPACITY incidents.

To address Mina’s first-30-minutes concern:
- Paging/escalation rules: CAPACITY incidents should page Fleet as primary AND also notify Routing immediately when fallback/admission control is involved.
- Classification rule for ICs (write this into the decision tree):
  1) If headroom is constrained (pool saturation, provisioning backlog, regional exhaustion) OR fallback_reason=capacity is dominant -> classify as CAPACITY.* (primary Fleet).
  2) If headroom exists but router is falling back / shedding due to misconfig, bad rollout, policy bug, or incorrect health signals -> classify as ROUTING/FALLBACK.* (primary Routing).
  3) If unclear in first 15 minutes -> set CAPACITY.* with “contributing_systems=Routing” and revisit once signals confirm.

Action items (please assign owners + dates):
- Update the owner mapping table and control-plane config to reflect this (ENG-4825).
- Ensure incident-bot can set a secondary/escalation target (Routing) when CAPACITY incident includes fallback/admission symptoms (ENG-4823).

Sean


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 11:28 AM PST
Subject: Re: Owner mapping dispute: capacity-related incident subtypes (Routing vs Fleet) — decision needed for v1

Thanks Sean — captured.

I’ll implement the decision in the v1 mapping + decision tree as follows:

Final mapping (v1):
- CAPACITY > Regional capacity exhaustion — Primary: Fleet; Secondary: Routing; Consulted: SRE
- CAPACITY > Provisioning lag / scale-up stalled — Primary: Fleet; Secondary: SRE; Consulted: Routing
- CAPACITY > Pool saturation (GPU/utilization) — Primary: Fleet; Secondary: Routing; Consulted: SRE
- ROUTING/FALLBACK > Fallback misbehavior (policy/health/compat bugs) — Primary: Routing; Secondary: SRE; Consulted: Fleet

IC decision rule (one-liner for the bot + policy page):
- “If the constraint is headroom (saturation/backlog/exhaustion) -> CAPACITY (Fleet owns). If headroom exists but fallbacks/shedding are incorrect -> ROUTING/FALLBACK (Routing owns). If unknown -> CAPACITY + tag Routing as contributing system, revisit within 30 minutes.”

Follow-ups (next week):
1) Ava: update Confluence owner mapping + decision tree language and link it from the incident classification policy. ETA Tue 1/13.
2) Rafael: confirm escalation rule wording for CAPACITY incidents (Fleet primary + Routing notified) and align with incident-bot closure gating. ETA Wed 1/14.
3) Mina/Vanessa: provide 2–3 concrete examples each for the taxonomy page (what to pick, what not to pick). ETA Wed 1/14.
4) Routing team: ensure fallback_reason enums land + are visible in the incident channel bot summary (ENG-4824). Tracking separately.

If anyone thinks we need a dedicated “Capacity-driven fallback storm” subtype name for readability, we can add it as an alias in the UI copy while keeping the underlying type low-cardinality (CAPACITY > saturation/exhaustion) for metrics.

Ava
—
Ava Chen
Platform Operations (Reliability Program)
Redwood Inference

Linear: ENG-4824 (instrument routing fallback reasons for taxonomy)

source.subject: Request: SOC 2 Type II report, bridge letter & renewal runway for upcoming expansion
source.messages:
From: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@acme-health.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Acme Health Procurement <procurement@acme-health.com>
Date: Fri, 9 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: SOC 2 - upcoming renewal & bridge request for expansion

Hi Dev,

We're getting close to our vendor renewal / expansion window and procurement/security asked me to gather the latest SOC 2 Type II report and any applicable bridge letters. Can you confirm: 1) the audit period covered by your most recent Type II, 2) whether a bridge letter is available that extends coverage into our renewal date (we're targeting Mar 31, 2026), and 3) any distribution controls (NDA / minimum access steps) we need to follow to receive the report?

We may be adding capacity across two new regions as part of this renewal, so procurement wanted to confirm timelines for evidence delivery so they can clear the contract.

Thanks,
Paul Nguyen
Security Procurement
Acme Health
paul.nguyen@acme-health.com

---

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@acme-health.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 9 Jan 2026 12:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SOC 2 - upcoming renewal & bridge request for expansion

Hi Paul — thanks for the heads up. Short answers up front: we can provide the latest SOC 2 Type II report and a temporary bridge letter that covers until 2026-03-31; distribution requires a signed NDA or an access request via DocuSign (link below). Details: 

- SOC 2 Type II report: attached (redwood_soc2_type_ii_2024.pdf). Audit period covered: 2024-07-01 through 2025-06-30.
- Bridge letter: attached as redwood_soc2_bridge_q1_2026.pdf. This is an interim letter prepared by our security team that attests no material changes since the end of the last audit period; it is time-limited and valid through 2026-03-31.
- Distribution controls: we require either (a) a signed Redwood mutual NDA or (b) an executed one-way access agreement and an explicit distribution approval from your security/compliance team. We can expedite via DocuSign: https://docusign.com/ABC-REDWOOD-SOC2-ACCESS

Attached files: redwood_soc2_type_ii_2024.pdf (redacted PII), redwood_soc2_bridge_q1_2026.pdf, redwood_distribution_guidance.txt (short checklist). If Procurement prefers, we can also grant time-limited secure view access to the report via our compliance portal and log viewer access.

Looping in Laura from Security and Tessa from Sales Ops to confirm the distribution route and any additional evidence you might need for the expansion (e.g., encryption key management details, SOC 2 control narratives).

Next steps for us: please confirm the recipient list for report distribution and whether Acme will sign the mutual NDA or use our one-way access agreement. If you want the portal link instead, let us know and we'll send an invite that logs viewer IPs and timestamps.

Best,
Dev Patel
Senior Account Executive — Enterprise
Redwood Inference
dev.patel@redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood_soc2_type_ii_2024.pdf (attachment stub), redwood_soc2_bridge_q1_2026.pdf (attachment stub)

---

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@acme-health.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 9 Jan 2026 13:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SOC 2 - upcoming renewal & bridge request for expansion

Hi Paul / Dev — quick note from security: the attached Type II report has standard redactions for customer-controlled PII and some vendor-specific configuration details. We can provide an expanded evidence packet (control narratives, KMS attestation, SLA excerpts) on a case-by-case basis — please list specific artifacts Acme needs.

Distribution: we prefer DocuSign for audit trail. If Acme sends a procurement email thread confirming the allowed recipients (names + emails), Security will approve distribution within 24 business hours.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Director, Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

---

From: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@acme-health.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Acme Health Procurement <procurement@acme-health.com>
Date: Fri, 9 Jan 2026 14:50:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SOC 2 - upcoming renewal & bridge request for expansion

Thanks Dev and Laura — that works. Procurement is fine signing the mutual NDA; I need to confirm two recipients for distribution: 
- Sarah Kim (Head of Security) <sarah.kim@acme-health.com>
- Contracts intake <contracts@acme-health.com>

I'll get the OK from Contracts and send the signed NDA today. Could you also confirm whether the bridge letter covers changes related to multi-region deployment (we're planning a us-west + eu-west expansion)? Any notes on regional controls would be helpful for our InfoSec review.

Best,
Paul

---

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@acme-health.com>, Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@acme-health.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 9 Jan 2026 15:28:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SOC 2 - upcoming renewal & bridge request for expansion

Great — thanks Paul. Confirming: bridge letter explicitly states no material changes since the audit through 2026-03-31 and includes an excerpt on our deployment governance that calls out regional isolation practices (VPC segmentation, customer pinning, and region-specific key management). For the EU region we have additional controls documented in our control narrative; I'll have Rafael (Engineering Security) add a short region controls addendum if you want it with the report.

I'll route the DocuSign link to Contracts once Paul confirms the final recipient emails. Rafael — can you prepare a 1-page addendum for eu-west/us-west controls and hit reply-all when ready?

Thanks — I’ll follow up with the DocuSign envelope and temporary secure-view link to the report once we have the signed NDA.

Best,
Dev
Senior Account Executive — Enterprise
Redwood Inference
dev.patel@redwood.ai

Attachments referenced: redwood_soc2_type_ii_2024.pdf, redwood_soc2_bridge_q1_2026.pdf, redwood_distribution_guidance.txt

source.subject: Go/No-Go criteria + rollback authority for controlled regional failover (Q1 game day)
source.body:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: Go/No-Go criteria + rollback authority for controlled regional failover (Q1 game day)

Team —

Before we schedule the production controlled failover window next week, I want us aligned on explicit go/no-go criteria, who can pull the stop, and what “rollback” means operationally.

Please review the proposed criteria below and reply with edits today. Goal is to lock by EOD so we can approve the change request and finalize comms.

Proposed GO criteria (production game day):
1) Signal quality + trigger safety
- Region Health aggregator is feeding routing with fresh data (no gaps > 60s) in prod.
- Failover controller in dry-run has produced consistent recommendations for 72h (no oscillation, no missing-signal crashes).
- Burn alerts for 5xx + tail latency are enabled with tuned thresholds (no paging noise spikes from baseline).

2) Guardrails
- Policy flip rate limiting enabled (max flips per route per hour per tier) + hold-down timers set.
- Approval hooks enabled for production execution (manual ack required) for this first test.

3) Capacity + overload protection
- Destination region headroom meets “safe to failover” threshold for the selected routes/models (Hosted + Dedicated). Irene/Victor sign-off on capacity + networking readiness.
- Gateway retry budgets/timeouts updated + overload responses (Retry-After) deployed.

4) Observability + operational readiness
- Dashboard panels are complete: reachability probes, 5xx burn, latency burn, capacity headroom, policy flips, and annotations.
- Runbook updated with exact steps for enable/disable, rollback, and customer checks.

Proposed NO-GO criteria (any one = postpone):
- Missing health signal streams in prod OR controller crashes when any signal is missing.
- Capacity headroom below threshold in the target region for the selected blast radius.
- DNS/traffic steering changes not fully rolled out (or provider caveats not mitigated).
- Unable to demonstrate rollback within 5 minutes in staging rehearsal.

Rollback authority + escalation:
- Immediate stop authority: SRE on-call lead (Rafael) can halt automation and revert policy flips without additional approvals.
- Technical escalation chain (24/7): Rafael (SRE) → Sean (Reliability Program) → Irene (Infra) / Priya (Routing/Control Plane) depending on issue.
- Executive escalation: Neha (me) as final call on continuing vs aborting the test.

Definitions (so we don’t argue mid-event):
- “Rollback” = revert routing policies to pre-test state AND disable automation loop (controller) AND place region in hold-down if signals are noisy.
- “Abort” = rollback + stop further steps + comms per playbook (internal + status page decision).

Attachments:
- ControlledFailover-GoNoGo-Criteria-v3.docx
- ProductionChangeApproval-INT-1142-summary.pdf

Thanks — please confirm (1) you agree, (2) any redlines, (3) who is primary/backup for the live approval ack.

Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 9:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Go/No-Go criteria + rollback authority for controlled regional failover (Q1 game day)

Agree with the structure and authority callouts.

Two edits from my side:
- Add an explicit GO item that incident-bot posts automation decisions into the incident channel with links to the dashboards (so the timeline is not manual). We missed this in the last rehearsal and it slowed triage.
- On NO-GO: if we still have unresolved alert routing/paging noise from the new burn rules, postpone. I’d phrase as “pager load stable for 48h after final tuning.”

For live approval ack (manual hook), my preference:
- Primary: Rafael (on-call lead)
- Backup: Sean (me)

Sean Gallagher
Director, SRE
Redwood Inference

---

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Go/No-Go criteria + rollback authority for controlled regional failover (Q1 game day)

+1 overall.

Infra/network specific asks to bake into GO:
- DNS TTL changes: confirm fully rolled out and we’ve validated actual resolver behavior from 3 external vantage points (not just internal). We’ve seen sticky caching despite TTL.

title: Go/No-Go criteria + rollback authority for controlled regional failover (Q1 game day)
preview:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: Go/No-Go criteria + rollback authority for controlled regional failover (Q1 game day)

Team —

Before we schedule the production controlled failover window next week, I want us aligned on explicit go/no-go criteria, who can pull the stop, and what “rollback” means operationally.

Please review the proposed criteria below and reply with edits today. Goal is to lock by EOD so we can approve the change request and finalize comms.

Proposed GO criteria (production game day):
1) Signal quality + trigger safety
- Region Health aggregator is feeding routing with fresh data (no gaps > 60s) in prod.
- Failover controller in dry-run has produced consistent recommendations for 72h (no oscillation, no missing-signal crashes).
- Burn alerts for 5xx + tail latency are enabled with tuned thresholds (no paging noise spikes from baseline).

2) Guardrails
- Policy flip rate limiting enabled (max flips per route per hour per tier) + hold-down timers set.
- Approval hooks enabled for production execution (manual ack required) for this first test.

3) Capacity + overload protection
- Destination region headroom meets “safe to failover” threshold for the selected routes/models (Hosted + Dedicated). Irene/Victor sign-off on capacity + networking readiness.
- Gateway retry budgets/timeouts updated + overload responses (Retry-After) deployed.

4) Observability + operational readiness
- Dashboard panels are complete: reachability probes, 5xx burn, latency burn, capacity headroom, policy flips, and annotations.
- Runbook updated with exact steps for enable/disable, rollback, and customer checks.

Proposed NO-GO criteria (any one = postpone):
- Missing health signal streams in prod OR controller crashes when any signal is missing.
- Capacity headroom below threshold in the target region for the selected blast radius.
- DNS/traffic steering changes not fully rolled out (or provider caveats not mitigated).
- Unable to demonstrate rollback within 5 minutes in staging rehearsal.

Rollback authority + escalation:
- Immediate stop authority: SRE on-call lead (Rafael) can halt automation and revert policy flips without additional approvals.
- Technical escalation chain (24/7): Rafael (SRE) → Sean (Reliability Program) → Irene (Infra) / Priya (Routing/Control Plane) depending on issue.
- Executive escalation: Neha (me) as final call on continuing vs aborting the test.

Definitions (so we don’t argue mid-event):
- “Rollback” = revert routing policies to pre-test state AND disable automation loop (controller) AND place region in hold-down if signals are noisy.
- “Abort” = rollback + stop further steps + comms per playbook (internal + status page decision).

Attachments:
- ControlledFailover-GoNoGo-Criteria-v3.docx
- ProductionChangeApproval-INT-1142-summary.pdf

Thanks — please confirm (1) you agree, (2) any redlines, (3) who is primary/backup for the live approval ack.

Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference

- Primary: Rafael (on-call lead)

Sean Gallagher

source.subject: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16
source.body:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Dec 11, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16

Sean / Rafael — requesting leadership approval to publish and enforce the remediation SLA policy + escalation rules as part of the Reliability Program.

This is the "time-to-mitigate" and "time-to-fix" expectations that will be:
- visible in incident tooling (incident-bot + dashboards)
- used for breach alerts (Slack + paging for Sev0/Sev1)
- referenced by CS for Dedicated/Private post-incident reviews

Asks:
1) Approve the v1 matrix and carve-outs (attached).
2) Approve escalation triggers (attached).
3) Confirm we can begin enforcement starting Tue 12/16 for new incidents (classification required; SLA timers on).

What’s in v1 (high level):
- Hosted: tighter time-to-mitigate for Sev0/Sev1, time-to-fix aligned to "restore + prevent recurrence" guidance (not full RCAs).
- Dedicated: separate expectations due to fixed capacity + customer-specific topology; includes explicit "customer-caused" and "cloud provider outage" pause criteria.
- Private: acknowledges customer-managed networking; SLA applies to Redwood-controlled components, plus defined handoff to Private Deployments + Networking.

Carve-outs / pause conditions (explicitly documented):
- Confirmed cloud provider regional outage affecting control plane or fleet provisioning.
- Customer-caused misconfiguration confirmed (e.g., private DNS/TLS changes) — timers can pause once documented and communicated.
- Force majeure / security incident dual-tracking (security owns response; reliability SLA timer can be paused with Security Ops acknowledgement).

Risks / operational impact (see risk notes attachment):
- Expect +10–15% paging for first 2 weeks due to breach alerts and "unknown" classifications.
- Requires on-call discipline: mitigation updates + owner confirmation in incident-bot.
- Dedicated/Private comms: CS needs clear language to avoid over-promising "time-to-fix" when infra dependencies exist.

Proposed decision deadline: EOD Fri 12/12 so we can keep the 12/16 rollout.

Attachments:
- Remediation_SLA_Matrix_v1.0.pdf
- Escalation_Policy_v1.0.docx
- SLA_Risk_Notes_v1.0.txt

Links:
- Confluence draft: incident-ownership-mapping-and-remediation-sla-matrix
- Process update draft: incident-classification-policy
- Linear tracking: PM-912 (tier alignment), ENG-4829 (timers + breach alerts)

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
VP, Product Operations
Redwood Inference


---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Dec 11, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16

Adding SRE context + what we’ll operationalize.

What changes for on-call:
- Incident commander must set tier + severity + incident_type within 15 min of declaring (or choose "Unknown" + reason).

Enforcement scope:
- New incidents only starting 12/16.

title: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16
preview:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Dec 11, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16

Sean / Rafael — requesting leadership approval to publish and enforce the remediation SLA policy + escalation rules as part of the Reliability Program.

This is the "time-to-mitigate" and "time-to-fix" expectations that will be:
- visible in incident tooling (incident-bot + dashboards)
- used for breach alerts (Slack + paging for Sev0/Sev1)
- referenced by CS for Dedicated/Private post-incident reviews

Asks:
1) Approve the v1 matrix and carve-outs (attached).
2) Approve escalation triggers (attached).
3) Confirm we can begin enforcement starting Tue 12/16 for new incidents (classification required; SLA timers on).

What’s in v1 (high level):
- Hosted: tighter time-to-mitigate for Sev0/Sev1, time-to-fix aligned to "restore + prevent recurrence" guidance (not full RCAs).
- Dedicated: separate expectations due to fixed capacity + customer-specific topology; includes explicit "customer-caused" and "cloud provider outage" pause criteria.
- Private: acknowledges customer-managed networking; SLA applies to Redwood-controlled components, plus defined handoff to Private Deployments + Networking.

Carve-outs / pause conditions (explicitly documented):
- Confirmed cloud provider regional outage affecting control plane or fleet provisioning.
- Customer-caused misconfiguration confirmed (e.g., private DNS/TLS changes) — timers can pause once documented and communicated.
- Force majeure / security incident dual-tracking (security owns response; reliability SLA timer can be paused with Security Ops acknowledgement).

Risks / operational impact (see risk notes attachment):
- Expect +10–15% paging for first 2 weeks due to breach alerts and "unknown" classifications.
- Requires on-call discipline: mitigation updates + owner confirmation in incident-bot.
- Dedicated/Private comms: CS needs clear language to avoid over-promising "time-to-fix" when infra dependencies exist.

Proposed decision deadline: EOD Fri 12/12 so we can keep the 12/16 rollout.

Attachments:
- Remediation_SLA_Matrix_v1.0.pdf
- Escalation_Policy_v1.0.docx
- SLA_Risk_Notes_v1.0.txt

Links:
- Confluence draft: incident-ownership-mapping-and-remediation-sla-matrix
- Process update draft: incident-classification-policy
- Linear tracking: PM-912 (tier alignment), ENG-4829 (timers + breach alerts)

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
VP, Product Operations
Redwood Inference

Two notes:
1) We should avoid framing "time-to-fix" as a guarantee in customer-visible contexts. Internally we can keep it as an expectation / management target; for Hosted we can communicate "next update" + "mitigation" timelines instead.
2) The breach alerting should differentiate "missed mitigation" vs "missed fix" so we don’t create noise when mitigation was on time but the deeper fix is scheduled.

Otherwise yes from me.

Nikhil


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Dec 11, 2025 at 2:41 PM PT
Subject: Re: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16

Thanks. Directionally supportive.

Conditions for approval:
- We must keep the carve-outs tight and auditable. No "pause" without a written reason in the incident timeline.
- Dedicated/Private language must be vetted by CS (Rachel) so we don’t set expectations incorrectly.
- Confirm who is the final approver for pause/unpause (IC vs SRE manager-on-call) and document it.

Neha — please send updated matrix with Lauren/Nikhil tweaks incorporated. I’ll approve in-thread.

Sean Gallagher
Executive Sponsor, SRE


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Dec 12, 2025 at 9:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16

Incorporated requested updates (no change to the numeric targets, but clarified definitions/measurement):

Updates made:
- Dedicated: "Mitigation" explicitly includes graceful degradation (fallback model/region, queueing, controlled throttling) if it materially reduces customer impact.
- Dedicated Sev2 time-to-fix: clarified as "time to deliver Redwood-side fix"; customer maintenance window scheduling called out as a dependency that can extend final rollout.
- Hosted: clarified that "time-to-fix" is an internal remediation expectation; customer comms remain focused on mitigation + next update cadence.
- Breach alerting: separate timers for mitigation vs fix (ENG-4829 will alert differently).
- Pause/unpause authority: SRE Incident Commander can initiate pause; SRE manager-on-call must acknowledge for Sev0/Sev1 within 30 min. All pauses require a reason string + evidence link in timeline.

Re-attaching updated docs:
- Remediation_SLA_Matrix_v1.0.pdf (rev 12/12)
- Escalation_Policy_v1.0.docx (rev 12/12)

Rachel — please confirm CS language is acceptable for Dedicated/Private by noon; otherwise we’ll proceed with internal-only publication and hold CS templates until Monday.

Neha


---

From: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Dec 12, 2025 at 11:37 AM PT
Subject: Re: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16

Reviewed from CS/Enterprise comms POV.

Approved with one small wording request for the CS-facing snippet:
- Replace "time-to-fix" with "target remediation timeline" in customer summaries.
- When a pause is invoked, we should communicate "timeline adjusted due to external dependency" rather than "SLA paused".

Otherwise the matrix and escalation policy read clean.

Rachel Kim
Enterprise CS Programs


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Dec 12, 2025 at 4:37 PM PT
Subject: Re: Approval request: Remediation SLA policy + escalation rules (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) — v1 rollout 12/16

Approved.

Proceed with:
- Publishing policy + matrix in Confluence
- Tooling enforcement for new incidents starting 12/16
- Warn-mode for Dedicated/Private breach paging for week 1 as Rafael described

Please ensure we track:
- classification compliance rate
- breach rate by tier and incident type
- paging volume impact week-over-week

Rafael/Talia: bring early readouts to staff meeting week of 1/5.

Sean

source.subject: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)
source.body:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Sean — requesting SRE + exec approval to enable the mixed-workload scheduler prototype behind flag in a *limited* production canary (Hosted API, us-east-1).

Why now
- We have repeat internal + customer escalations where mixed short/long prompts inflate p95/TTFT (HOL blocking during long prefill).
- The runtime team has a prototype (two-queue short/long + aging + token budget guardrails) that shows consistent p95 improvement on mixed distributions without material throughput loss in our benchmark suite.

What we’re proposing (scope + blast radius)
- Region/pool: Hosted API us-east-1, pool: hosted-canary (not general).
- Traffic: 1% -> 5% (same pool) over ~3 hours if metrics stay within guardrails.
- Duration: 24 hours at 5% (or revert sooner if drift).
- Feature gating:
  - Flag: scheduler.mixed_workload.enabled=true (pool-scoped)
  - Policy knobs pinned (no auto-tuning) for the canary window.

Success criteria (must hit)
- Mixed workload p95 latency (end-to-end) improves >= 15% vs baseline (comparable traffic band), measured on canary + perf-canary.
- No p95 regression > 5% on steady-state short-only workload.
- No error-rate increase beyond normal variance; no sustained queue starvation counters.

Guardrails / rollback triggers (aligned to SRE doc)
- Rollback immediately if any of:
  - p95 (Hosted API chat) +10% for > 10 minutes vs same-hour baseline
  - p99 +15% for > 10 minutes
  - starvation_long_counter increments continuously for > 2 minutes (indicates long queue not making progress)
  - elevated OOM/compute errors attributable to batch construction changes
- Rollback options:
  1) Flip flag off (fast, no deploy) — expected to drain within minutes.
  2) Revert knobs to baseline (if only parameter-induced).
  3) Runtime rollback to 1.17.3 in hosted-canary (Connor has the release playbook ready).

Known risks (and mitigations)
- Risk: long-queue starvation under sustained short traffic
  - Mitigation: aging + min dispatch share + regression test merged (PR #24905).
- Risk: kernel selection instability when alternating short/long sequences
  - Mitigation: prefill/decode separation hooks + capped token budget per iteration; monitored via kernel_thrash metric + Nsight spot checks in staging.
- Risk: throughput dip due to policy overhead
  - Mitigation: overhead measured <1% CPU in staging; monitor tokens/sec and batch utilization.

Links / artifacts
- Results summary (attached): mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf
- Canary plan draft (attached): canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx
- Confluence design + ADR:
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SERVE/Mixed-workload+scheduler+prototype+-+Design
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-Short-Long-Prompt-Scheduling-Policy
- Perf-canary dashboard:
  - https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-mixed/mixed-workload-perf-canary?var-runtime=1.18.0-rc3

Request
- Sean: please reply with SRE sign-off (or required edits) so Connor can schedule the window today.
- If approved, we’ll do the ramp during US business hours with Rafael as ops point and Aisha watching SLO + rollback triggers.

Thanks,
Mateo
Executive Sponsor, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachment: mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf (application/pdf)
Attachment: canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

A couple asks before I sign off:

1) Can you confirm the change is *pool-scoped only* (no spillover to other hosted pools via shared config)?
2) What’s our exact dashboard + alert set for queue starvation + per-class waiting time? I don’t want this to be “we’ll eyeball it.”
3) In the results summary: what’s the worst-case scenario you saw (any regressions on long-only or long-spike mixes)?

If we can nail those three, I’m comfortable with a 1% -> 5% ramp with a tight rollback.

-Sean

---

From: Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:36 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Responding inline:

> 1) confirm pool-scoped only
Yes. The flag + knobs are resolved at pool config level (runtime config service), and we’re only enabling for pool=hosted-canary in us-east-1. No global default change. (Config diff is attached in the canary plan doc; Rafael can also paste the exact key path if helpful.)

> 2) dashboard + alert set
Allison has the dashboard spec + alerts wired. We’ll be monitoring:
- scheduler_queue_depth{class=short|long}
- scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class=short|long}
- scheduler_starvation_events_total{class=long}
- scheduler_dispatch_share{class=short|long}
- plus end-to-end p95/p99 and TTFT.

> 3) worst-case scenario seen
In “long-spike” synthetic (10 minutes of 70% long-context), we saw:
- Mixed p95 improved still (less HOL for short),
- Long-class p95 increased ~3–6% depending on SKU, but no starvation due to aging + min-share.
We did see one starvation corner case early (continuous short flood); that’s what PR #24905 addresses, and it’s in the 1.18.0-rc3 build proposed.

Relevant PRs:
- Two-queue policy: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24844
- Metrics: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24888
- Starvation fix: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24905

-Lena

---

From: Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 11:05 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Dashboard is live for hosted-canary / us-east-1:
https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/sched-class/scheduler-class-metrics?var-region=us-east-1&var-pool=hosted-canary

Alerts (PagerDuty: Runtime-SRE)
- “SchedulerLongClassStarvation”
  - fires if scheduler_starvation_events_total{class="long"} increases AND scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class="long"} > 20000ms for 2m
- “SchedulerClassSkewTooHigh”
  - fires if dispatch_share{class="short"} > 0.95 for 5m (indicates long not getting scheduled)
- “SchedulerQueueTimeRegressionShort”
  - fires if time_in_queue p95 short > baseline + 15% for 10m

These are documented in the dashboard “Readme” panel and mirror the Hosted API tail latency guardrails doc.

-Allison

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 11:42 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Approved from SRE side with the following conditions:
- Ramp stays within the guardrails described (1% -> 5% only; no additional pools without a follow-up).
- Aisha or Rafael is explicitly watching during the ramp window; if alerts fire, rollback is immediate (no “let’s wait it out”).
- Connor to confirm rollback path (flag off + runtime rollback) is ready before we start.

Thanks for putting the monitoring in place.

-Sean

---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 12:06 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Confirmed — we’ll follow those conditions.

Connor: please schedule the ramp for this afternoon (target start 2:00pm PT / 5:00pm ET) and reply-all with the exact change window + rollback checklist.

Rafael/Aisha: you’re on point for the ramp. If any of the three rollback triggers hit (p95/p99 drift, starvation, error-rate anomalies), roll back immediately and we’ll do the postmortem tomorrow.

Thanks all.

-Mateo

Why now
- We have repeat internal + customer escalations where mixed short/long prompts inflate p95/TTFT (HOL blocking during long prefill).
- The runtime team has a prototype (two-queue short/long + aging + token budget guardrails) that shows consistent p95 improvement on mixed distributions without material throughput loss in our benchmark suite.

What we’re proposing (scope + blast radius)
- Region/pool: Hosted API us-east-1, pool: hosted-canary (not general).
- Traffic: 1% -> 5% (same pool) over ~3 hours if metrics stay within guardrails.
- Duration: 24 hours at 5% (or revert sooner if drift).
- Feature gating:
  - Flag: scheduler.mixed_workload.enabled=true (pool-scoped)
  - Policy knobs pinned (no auto-tuning) for the canary window.

Success criteria (must hit)
- Mixed workload p95 latency (end-to-end) improves >= 15% vs baseline (comparable traffic band), measured on canary + perf-canary.
- No p95 regression > 5% on steady-state short-only workload.
- No error-rate increase beyond normal variance; no sustained queue starvation counters.

Guardrails / rollback triggers (aligned to SRE doc)
- Rollback immediately if any of:
  - p95 (Hosted API chat) +10% for > 10 minutes vs same-hour baseline
  - p99 +15% for > 10 minutes
  - starvation_long_counter increments continuously for > 2 minutes (indicates long queue not making progress)
  - elevated OOM/compute errors attributable to batch construction changes
- Rollback options:
  1) Flip flag off (fast, no deploy) — expected to drain within minutes.
  2) Revert knobs to baseline (if only parameter-induced).
  3) Runtime rollback to 1.17.3 in hosted-canary (Connor has the release playbook ready).

Known risks (and mitigations)
- Risk: long-queue starvation under sustained short traffic
  - Mitigation: aging + min dispatch share + regression test merged (PR #24905).
- Risk: kernel selection instability when alternating short/long sequences
  - Mitigation: prefill/decode separation hooks + capped token budget per iteration; monitored via kernel_thrash metric + Nsight spot checks in staging.
- Risk: throughput dip due to policy overhead
  - Mitigation: overhead measured <1% CPU in staging; monitor tokens/sec and batch utilization.

Links / artifacts
- Results summary (attached): mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf
- Canary plan draft (attached): canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx
- Confluence design + ADR:
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SERVE/Mixed-workload+scheduler+prototype+-+Design
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-Short-Long-Prompt-Scheduling-Policy
- Perf-canary dashboard:
  - https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-mixed/mixed-workload-perf-canary?var-runtime=1.18.0-rc3

Request
- Sean: please reply with SRE sign-off (or required edits) so Connor can schedule the window today.
- If approved, we’ll do the ramp during US business hours with Rafael as ops point and Aisha watching SLO + rollback triggers.

Thanks,
Mateo
Executive Sponsor, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachment: mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf (application/pdf)
Attachment: canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

A couple asks before I sign off:

1) Can you confirm the change is *pool-scoped only* (no spillover to other hosted pools via shared config)?
2) What’s our exact dashboard + alert set for queue starvation + per-class waiting time? I don’t want this to be “we’ll eyeball it.”
3) In the results summary: what’s the worst-case scenario you saw (any regressions on long-only or long-spike mixes)?

If we can nail those three, I’m comfortable with a 1% -> 5% ramp with a tight rollback.

-Sean

---

From: Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:36 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Responding inline:

> 1) confirm pool-scoped only
Yes. The flag + knobs are resolved at pool config level (runtime config service), and we’re only enabling for pool=hosted-canary in us-east-1. No global default change. (Config diff is attached in the canary plan doc; Rafael can also paste the exact key path if helpful.)

> 2) dashboard + alert set
Allison has the dashboard spec + alerts wired. We’ll be monitoring:
- scheduler_queue_depth{class=short|long}
- scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class=short|long}
- scheduler_starvation_events_total{class=long}
- scheduler_dispatch_share{class=short|long}
- plus end-to-end p95/p99 and TTFT.

> 3) worst-case scenario seen
In “long-spike” synthetic (10 minutes of 70% long-context), we saw:
- Mixed p95 improved still (less HOL for short),
- Long-class p95 increased ~3–6% depending on SKU, but no starvation due to aging + min-share.
We did see one starvation corner case early (continuous short flood); that’s what PR #24905 addresses, and it’s in the 1.18.0-rc3 build proposed.

Relevant PRs:
- Two-queue policy: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24844
- Metrics: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24888
- Starvation fix: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24905

-Lena

---

From: Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 11:05 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Dashboard is live for hosted-canary / us-east-1:
https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/sched-class/scheduler-class-metrics?var-region=us-east-1&var-pool=hosted-canary

Alerts (PagerDuty: Runtime-SRE)
- “SchedulerLongClassStarvation”
  - fires if scheduler_starvation_events_total{class="long"} increases AND scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class="long"} > 20000ms for 2m
- “SchedulerClassSkewTooHigh”
  - fires if dispatch_share{class="short"} > 0.95 for 5m (indicates long not getting scheduled)
- “SchedulerQueueTimeRegressionShort”
  - fires if time_in_queue p95 short > baseline + 15% for 10m

These are documented in the dashboard “Readme” panel and mirror the Hosted API tail latency guardrails doc.

-Allison

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 11:42 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Approved from SRE side with the following conditions:
- Ramp stays within the guardrails described (1% -> 5% only; no additional pools without a follow-up).
- Aisha or Rafael is explicitly watching during the ramp window; if alerts fire, rollback is immediate (no “let’s wait it out”).
- Connor to confirm rollback path (flag off + runtime rollback) is ready before we start.

Thanks for putting the monitoring in place.

-Sean

---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 12:06 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Confirmed — we’ll follow those conditions.

Connor: please schedule the ramp for this afternoon (target start 2:00pm PT / 5:00pm ET) and reply-all with the exact change window + rollback checklist.

Rafael/Aisha: you’re on point for the ramp. If any of the three rollback triggers hit (p95/p99 drift, starvation, error-rate anomalies), roll back immediately and we’ll do the postmortem tomorrow.

Thanks all.

-Mateo

source.subject: Usage health checkpoint — proposed Q1 capacity uplift & term note
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Emma Shaw <emma.shaw@cobalt.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Nov 2025 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Usage health checkpoint — proposed Q1 capacity uplift & term note

Hi Emma,

Quick check-in on Cobalt AI's inference usage as we head into Q1 planning. We've attached a short usage report and a 1-page ROI snapshot that summarize the token cadence and latency profile for the last 90 days. Highlights: 
- Monthly token consumption has moved from ~1.8M to ~3.2M over the last two months (spike around model rollout).
- Median p95 latency remains within SLA target on the hosted tier, but tail latency increases during concurrent batch inference windows.
- Prefix caching reduced repeat-call tokens by ~12%, but new prompt patterns are driving headroom needs.

Given the trend, we recommend a modest capacity uplift for Q1: a dedicated pool with burst credits to smooth day-of-week spikes and a capacity ladder that adjusts after month 2. Attached: 20251112-cobaltai-usage-report.csv and 20251112-q1-uplift-roi.pdf.

Can we schedule a 30-min sync this week to walk the numbers and align on a short pilot? Proposed times: Thu 11/13 10:30am PT or Fri 11/14 9:00am PT.

Thanks,
Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0183

Attachments: 20251112-cobaltai-usage-report.csv (csv), 20251112-q1-uplift-roi.pdf (pdf)
From: Emma Shaw <emma.shaw@cobalt.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Cobalt AI Platform Team <platform@cobalt.ai>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 10:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Usage health checkpoint — proposed Q1 capacity uplift & term note

Hi Rafael / Monica,

Thanks for the report — useful snapshot. The spike you saw coincides with our new conversational agent rollout (released 10/25). We expect baseline tokens to settle between 3.0M–3.5M/mo but anticipate short bursts up to ~6M tokens/day during peak campaigns. A few quick asks before we lock a pilot: 
1) Can you confirm SLA behavior when burst usage hits 4x baseline? We need guarantees that tail latency won’t impact customer-facing flows.
2) What pricing flexibility do we have for a committed uplift? If we sign for a 12-month laddered commitment, can we get burst credits and a mid-term true-up window?
3) Legal will want a summary of data retention and audit logs for Dedicated.

I can do Fri 11/14 9am PT — please send an invite.

—Emma
Emma Shaw
Head of Platform | Cobalt AI
emma.shaw@cobalt.ai

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Emma Shaw <emma.shaw@cobalt.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Nov 2025 08:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Usage health checkpoint — proposed Q1 capacity uplift & term note

Emma — great questions. Short answers and a proposed technical approach below.

1) SLA and burst behavior: Dedicated pools are isolated; we recommend a 1-node Dedicated pilot (NVIDIA A10x-equivalent) with automatic fast-failover to a warm spare. For observed bursts up to 4x baseline we maintain p95 under 600ms in our tests; tail latency above that is mitigated using a temporary routing to our nearest region cache. We will instrument an SLO dashboard for you during the pilot.

2) Pricing & commitment: Draft quote attached (20251114-cobaltai-dedicated-quote.pdf). Proposal: 12-month term with a capacity ladder (3.0M tokens/mo starting tier; step to 4.5M after month 3 if uplift confirmed), plus 15% burst credits applied to overage for the pilot period. We can include a 30-day mid-term true-up window at month 6.

3) Data retention/audit logs: Redwood Private/Dedicated supports 90/180/365-day retention options, and we can surface audit logs via your SIEM integration. Laura from Legal is looped for any contract/data language.

Next steps: I created a DocuSign quote link and a HubSpot draft (see links). Rafael — can you confirm the final uplift numbers so I can finalize the PDF for signatures?

Thanks,
Monica
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.com

Attachments: 20251114-cobaltai-dedicated-quote.pdf (pdf)
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Emma Shaw <emma.shaw@cobalt.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 Nov 2025 16:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Usage health checkpoint — proposed Q1 capacity uplift & term note

Emma — thanks for the sync earlier and for walking through peak profiles. Per our conversation, confirm we will: 
- Start a 2-month Dedicated pilot (1-node pool) beginning Monday 12/1.
- Provision an initial committed ladder: 3.0M tokens/mo (month 1–2), automatic step to 4.5M tokens/mo from month 3 if observed 30-day moving average exceeds threshold.
- Apply 15% burst credits for overage during the pilot and include a 30-day true-up at month 6.
- SLA: p95 latency guarantee of 600ms for the Dedicated pool with warm-spare failover and SLO dashboard access.

I've attached the final quote (20251120-cobaltai-final-quote-ds.pdf) and sent the DocuSign envelope (link above). Signing will finalize provisioning lead times. Laura — please flag any contract language changes for Emma's team; otherwise we'll proceed on the 12/1 provisioning timeline.

Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0183

Attachments: 20251120-cobaltai-final-quote-ds.pdf (pdf)

source.subject: CardinalCloud CIO pre-brief & sponsor demo coordination
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Alice Chen <alice.chen@cardinalcloud.com>, Bob Mitchell <bob.mitchell@cardinalcloud.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Nov 2025 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Intro + quick sponsor pre-brief for CardinalCloud pilot

Hi Alice / Bob —

Thanks again for the productive discovery last week. Per our conversation, we'd like to schedule a short sponsor pre-brief ahead of the demo so your CIO (and any VP-level sponsors) have context on the scope and success criteria. Goal is a focused 20–30 minute sync: 

- Confirm CIO priorities (security, latency targets, data residency)
