DRIs proposed are fine. Ensure changes to mapping have a clear review checklist (naming, region coverage, dashboards/alerts links, and Dedicated/Private sensitivity).

Neha
text:
From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Status page transparency upgrades — scope approval + measurement plan ask

Neha — sharing the updated brief for the Status Page Transparency Upgrades initiative for approval.

What changed since last review:
- Component taxonomy now explicitly splits by (product surface x region x capability) where it helps customers (e.g., Hosted API vs Streaming vs Embeddings, by US-East/US-West/EU-West).
- Automation approach is “assistive by default”: incident-bot drafts updates from incident state + metrics, Comms Lead/IC owns final publish.
- Added guardrails on language: no implied SLA/credits; avoid customer-specific capacity discussion; Dedicated/Private incidents handled with product-appropriate visibility.

Decisions we’re seeking from you:
1) Approve the taxonomy direction (granularity + naming)
2) Approve the automation scope (incident-bot drafting + statuspage API client, not fully autonomous state flips)
3) Confirm measurement expectations for week 7–8 (TTFU, accuracy audit, support deflection)

Attachments:
- StatusPage_Transparency_Upgrades_Project_Brief_v1.2.pdf
- Statuspage_Component_Taxonomy_v0.9.docx

If you’re aligned, we’ll start Week 1–2 design (taxonomy + signal mapping) immediately and keep you posted via a weekly rollout note.

Thanks,
Talia Benson
Program Manager, Reliability Initiatives
Redwood Inference


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 10:01 AM PT
Subject: Re: Status page transparency upgrades — scope approval + measurement plan ask

Approved on (1) and (2).

A few notes / constraints to hold the line on:
- We need more precision without turning the status page into an internal services diagram. Product-surface + region is the right customer mental model. Keep “capability” splits only where it’s a real, recurring failure mode (Streaming is a good example).
- Do not auto-publish component status changes off metrics alone in this phase. Drafts + prompts + validation steps are the correct direction.
- Dedicated / Private: we can’t imply anything that creates a contractual interpretation. Messaging should stay consistent with our public language and avoid customer-specific capacity/placement references.

Measurement plan: I want an explicit plan attached to the brief by end of week.
Please include:
- TTFU target (time-to-first-update) by severity (e.g., Sev0/Sev1) and how incident-bot reminders enforce it
- Accuracy audit method (how we detect mismatches between impacted routes/regions vs what we marked on the status page)
- Support impact: how we measure ticket deflection / reduction in “status page inaccurate” escalations
- Rollout gates: dogfood → limited public → full rollout, with rollback criteria

Also, please identify a single DRI for the component mapping source-of-truth (repo/config) and a review/approval path. I don’t want this drifting across Confluence + spreadsheets.

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 10:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: Status page transparency upgrades — scope approval + measurement plan ask

+1 to all of Neha’s points.

On mapping source-of-truth: SRE recommends we keep the component registry + mapping config in-repo with schema validation (PR review required) and treat Confluence as the human-readable reference. Rafael can own the operational process; Christine (implementer) will do the first pass on registry entries with runtime/observability review.

On accuracy audits: we can use the weekly job we discussed to compare incident tags (component_id/region/capability) against status updates and flag mismatches for follow-up.

Sean


---

From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 11:27 AM PT
Subject: Re: Status page transparency upgrades — scope approval + measurement plan ask

Thanks — we’ll incorporate the measurement plan into the brief and circulate by EOW.

Draft measurement plan (for alignment now):

1) TTFU targets (public status update)
- Sev0: first public update within 15 minutes of declaration (target), reminder at +10 min via incident-bot; escalation ping to IC + Comms Lead at +15.
- Sev1: first public update within 30 minutes (target), reminders at +20/+30.
- Metric source: incident-bot timestamps (incident declared vs first Statuspage publish event).

2) Accuracy audits
- Weekly automated audit job: incident tags (affected component_id/region/capability + impacted routes) compared to what was marked on Statuspage.
- Outputs: (a) mismatch count, (b) mismatch types (wrong region, too coarse component, missing capability split), (c) time-to-correct status, (d) owners for follow-up.
- We’ll sample 100% of Sev0/Sev1 and 20% of Sev2 for the first month.

3) Support impact / deflection
- Baseline: count of tickets with “status inaccurate” / “status page says OK but X failing” (Support will provide tags/macros).
- KPI: 30% reduction in those escalations by end of Week 8 (directional; not a hard SLA), plus time-to-resolution for disputes.
- Qualitative: Support survey after 2 weeks of rollout (“fewer back-and-forth” / “easier to point customers at component-level incident and summary”).

4) Rollout gates
- Dogfood (internal view + incident drills): validate taxonomy + bot workflow + anti-flap logic.
- Limited public (subset of components/regions): monitor for confusion + flapping + excessive comms overhead.
- Full rollout: only after we meet (a) TTFU adherence >= 80% for Sev0/Sev1 in dogfood + limited public and (b) mismatch rate trending down week-over-week.
- Rollback criteria: repeated component flapping; significant customer confusion; on-call load increase without benefit.

DRI for mapping source-of-truth: proposing Ravi Menon (service-to-component mapping) for the mapping layer, with Rafael as approval owner (SRE) and Allison for observability schema/labels review. We’ll route PR reviews through SRE + Observability for changes.

If you want different DRIs, tell us and we’ll adjust.

Talia


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 12:03 PM PT
Subject: Re: Status page transparency upgrades — scope approval + measurement plan ask

This works.

Two tweaks:
- Please phrase the support KPI as “goal / expected outcome” rather than a commitment (agree with your note).
- For rollout gates, add a specific on-call burden check: “median time spent on comms tasks” for IC/Comms Lead, pre vs post.

DRIs proposed are fine. Ensure changes to mapping have a clear review checklist (naming, region coverage, dashboards/alerts links, and Dedicated/Private sensitivity).

Neha

source.subject: Re: CCPA/CPRA role definition and subprocessor timeline for GreenMart
messages:
From: Amanda Li <amanda.li@greenmart.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Cc: GreenMart Legal <legal@greenmart.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: CCPA/CPRA role clarification + subprocessor timeline

Hi Rafael,

Thanks for the demo last week — GreenMart's product team was impressed. We're moving toward a procurement review and our privacy team has a couple of ask items before we can finalize SOW/PO:  

1) CCPA/CPRA role: we need explicit confirmation in the DPA that Redwood will be engaged as a "Service Provider" (not a Controller or Processor for our purposes) for sale/processing exclusions under CA law. Please confirm the exact clause text you prefer and whether Redwood will accept the CPRA Service Provider definition (we attached our template spreadsheet).

2) Subprocessors: we require a 30-day notice prior to onboarding any new subprocessor that will process personal data for GreenMart, plus the ability to review the subprocessor list quarterly. Can you confirm Redwood's current subprocessors relevant to telemetry, hosting, and analytics and provide the timeline/process for notification?

3) Interstate transfers / telemetry: some of our customers are in states that treat certain telemetry as "personal information" under new amendments — we need to ensure there is no persistent identification crossing state lines without customer opt-in. Can Redwood provide a short description of how telemetry (latency/p95 logs, model input prefixes used for caching) is handled and whether any PII is stored/transferred across state boundaries?

Attached: GreenMart-Questionnaire-CCPA.xlsx with question mapping. Procurement would like a redline or DPA-draft in the next 5 business days if possible.

Best,
Amanda
Privacy Manager, GreenMart Inc.

--
Attachment: GreenMart-Questionnaire-CCPA.xlsx (xlsx, 45KB)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 3 Nov 2025 10:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: CCPA/CPRA role clarification + subprocessor timeline

Forwarding GreenMart's asks — summary below and suggested owners: Priyom (legal) for DPA language/subprocessor notification; Marcus (infra) for telemetry handling; Laura (SE) to help with the subprocessor inventory and procurement Qs.

Quick notes for context: GreenMart is a mid-market retail customer in procurement stage (deal GM-2025-201). They want Redwood explicitly scoped as a Service Provider under CPRA, 30-day prior notice for subprocessors, and details on telemetry and interstate transfer behavior. Procurement deadline: redline in ~5 business days.

Can we aim to:  
- Priyom: provide a proposed DPA redline and confirm Redwood's position on 30-day notice vs standard notice (we typically publish a subprocessor list monthly and provide immediate notice for critical changes).
- Marcus: short plain-language telemetry summary and whether any logs that could be considered PI leave region boundaries as part of our hosted offering.
- Laura: inventory of current subprocessors (hosting, monitoring, analytics) and any planned changes in next 90 days.

Thanks — Rafael
Account Exec, Redwood Inference

-----Original Message-----
From: Amanda Li <amanda.li@greenmart.com>
Sent: Mon, 3 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Cc: GreenMart Legal <legal@greenmart.com>
Subject: CCPA/CPRA role clarification + subprocessor timeline

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 4 Nov 2025 11:48:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: CCPA/CPRA role clarification + subprocessor timeline

Rafael / team,

Thanks — I pulled the questionnaire and drafted a redline DPA (attached: DPA-draft-v2.docx) that:  
- Explicitly defines Redwood as a "Service Provider" under the CPRA/CCPA definitions, with the standard limited-use language and no commercial sale of covered personal information.
- Adds a 30-day prior notice commitment for onboarding new subprocessors that will process GreenMart data, with the caveat that for critical security patches or emergency switchover (e.g., decommissioning an environment), we will provide notice as soon as practicable and remediate with equivalent controls.
- Clarifies telemetry: by default Redwood collects aggregated telemetry and transient runtime logs necessary for serving/observability. We do not persistently store raw customer inputs unless explicitly enabled (customer-managed storage or on-prem/private deployments). Any caching of prefixes for KV cache is transient and expires per configured retention (default 24 hours).

Subprocessor inventory (summary):
- Cloud hosting: Redwood-hosted compute (primary: Redwood-managed clusters; provider contracts with large cloud vendors)
- Telemetry/metrics: Segment (metrics aggregator), Datadog (SRE metrics/alerts)
- Model artifacts & storage: Object store partners (encrypted at rest); details in appendix of redline

I recommend we send the redline and the CCPA addendum draft PDF (light-touch language) to GreenMart and offer a 30-minute call to walk through the clauses. Laura — can you attach the detailed subprocessors list and projected changes over next 90 days? Marcus — please review the telemetry paragraph; if we need to change retention defaults or add state-specific controls (e.g., block telemetry export for CA residents), flag that and we'll propose a contractual option.

Attachment: DPA-draft-v2.docx (docx, 120KB)

--
Priyom Das
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Amanda Li <amanda.li@greenmart.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Cc: GreenMart Legal <legal@greenmart.com>
Date: Fri, 7 Nov 2025 16:24:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: CCPA/CPRA role clarification + subprocessor timeline

Priyom — thanks for the quick turnaround and the clear summary. We reviewed the redline and the CCPA addendum draft. A few follow-ups:  

- The service provider language looks acceptable; GreenMart legal will make minor edits but agrees to the limited-use framing.
- For subprocessors, GreenMart can accept the 30-day notice model. Please: 1) provide the full current subprocessor CSV (company, role, contact, location) and 2) confirm you will send a notification email to our privacy alias 30 days prior to any onboarding.
- Telemetry: request that Redwood include an optional clause that prohibits export of telemetry-derived identifiers across state boundaries for datasets identified as containing California residents, unless GreenMart explicitly opts in. Is this something Redwood can offer as an add-on or configuration for hosted customers?

Procurement asks that we route a DocuSign-ready DPA next week; can Redwood upload the redline to the DocuSign link and confirm an expected signature timeline? If you can also include the subprocessor CSV and SOC2 attestation link, that would accelerate procurement.

I'll loop in procurement once we have the DocuSign envelope and CSV.

Best,
Amanda Li
Privacy Manager, GreenMart Inc.

--
Attachment: CCPA-Addendum-draft.pdf (pdf, 28KB)

Is this something Redwood can offer as an add-on or configuration for hosted customers?

Procurement asks that we route a DocuSign-ready DPA next week; can Redwood upload the redline to the DocuSign link and confirm an expected signature timeline? If you can also include the subprocessor CSV and SOC2 attestation link, that would accelerate procurement.

I'll loop in procurement once we have the DocuSign envelope and CSV.

Best,
Amanda Li
Privacy Manager, GreenMart Inc.

--
Attachment: CCPA-Addendum-draft.pdf (pdf, 28KB)

Subject: Request: latest SOC 2 Type II + bridge letter for renewal/expansion PO

Hi Olga,

As we prepare the procurement packet for the upcoming renewal and the incremental capacity add-on (RFP reference D-98432), our procurement and InfoSec teams need the following by close of business next Monday:

- Most recent SOC 2 Type II report covering the last 12 months (prefer a downloadable PDF)
- Bridge letter or interim attestation if the report coverage does not include the current audit window
- Completed CAIQ (or SIG-lite) spreadsheet for the services that will be in scope for our expansion

Subject: Re: Request: latest SOC 2 Type II + bridge letter for renewal/expansion PO

Hi Jane — thanks for the clear list. Short answers up front: yes, we can provide the SOC 2 Type II PDF and a bridge letter; we can also prefill a CAIQ csv to accelerate your InfoSec review.

I'm attaching: SOC2_Redwood_2024_TypeII.pdf (full report), redwood_bridge_letter_draft.pdf (standard interim attestation we provide to procurement teams), and a procurement_evidence_checklist.xlsx that maps common CAIQ questions to our evidence artifacts.

A few notes:
- The attached Type II covers the period 2024-06-01 through 2025-05-31.
- The bridge letter covers the gap from 2025-06-01 to 2025-11-30 and confirms no control changes that would impact the scope.
- We can deliver a completed CAIQ (prefill) within 3 business days; full evidence bundles usually take 7–10 business days depending on scope items you flag.

Regarding procurement windows: if Northridge needs the PO to provision in the FY window you listed (PO by 2025-12-10), we recommend the PO signature occur no later than 2025-12-01 to allow legal/finance to process amendments for the expansion. olga.petrov@redwood.inference.com

To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Nov 2025 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: latest SOC 2 Type II + bridge letter for renewal/expansion PO

Karthik / Rafael — forwarding Northridge's ask (below). Key items: they need the Type II PDF + bridge to accept the PO for an expansion add-on and their internal PO cutoff is 2025-12-10.

Questions for you:
- Karthik: can we hold pricing/credits for the expansion while procurement runs their review? They want provisioning in January if PO is by 12/10. Any room for a short-term credit or promotional uplift to help close?
- Rafael: if they sign the PO and rely on the bridge letter (our standard interim attestation), does Legal need to approve any additional language in the bridge that references the expansion? Should we propose an explicit clause for acceptance of the bridge for provisioning?

I attached our draft bridge letter for reference. Looking for quick thumbs up so I can send the finalized materials to Jane.

Thanks — Olga

-----Original Message-----
[Quoted message included above]

From: Jane Dawson <jane.dawson@northridgetx.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Michael Hart <michael.hart@northridgetx.com>, procure@northridgetx.com
Date: Thu, 6 Nov 2025 15:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: latest SOC 2 Type II + bridge letter for renewal/expansion PO

Olga — appreciate the quick turn. We reviewed the Type II and the bridge draft with InfoSec. Michael confirmed the bridge language is acceptable pending one small change: we need an explicit sentence stating that the bridge covers the specific services included in the expansion add-on ("Inference Serving API and Dedicated Capacity"). Can your team add that clause and reissue?

Also: we can accept a signed PO referencing the bridge letter as interim coverage, provided the bridge is dated and countersigned. We will route the PO for internal approvals next week; target: PO issued by 2025-12-08. Please send the updated bridge and the prefilled CAIQ csv when ready — the CAIQ will help us finalize the security checklist faster.

Once we have the corrected bridge we will sign the DocuSign envelope and push the PO through. Thanks for the assist — Jane

--

source.subject: Onsite Incident Playbook & Pre-Travel Checklist for Executive Workshop
messages:
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Emma Stone <emma.stone@horizonhealth.com>
Cc: procurement <procurement@horizonhealth.com>
Date: 2025-10-28T08:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive workshop — preliminary safety & logistics items

Team / Emma,

As we lock in the November 17–19 executive workshop in Boston, I want us to finalize an incident & health playbook that we can share with Horizon Health attendees and procurement. Key asks from me for this thread:

- Confirm local emergency contacts and nearest hospitals (both general and occupational clinic).
- Define notification chain for incidents (minor injury, lost badge, severe medical event, civil unrest/evacuation).
- Decide whether Redwood provides an on-site medical resource (nurse) or we expect the customer to provide.
- Travel advisories: current flu/RSV guidance, any local air quality or transit disruptions expected.
- Contingency plan if an attendee cannot travel (virtual fallback, recording policy, reimbursement/insurance).

I attached a rough risk matrix and a sample travel checklist to start. Please treat this as internal draft until we have sign-off from procurement. Emma — if Horizon has internal policies we should align to, can you share?

Attachments: [onsite_risk_matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet) — draft], [travel_checklist.docx]

Thanks,
Laura
Laura Bennett
Director, Customer Programs | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0122

From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Emma Stone <emma.stone@horizonhealth.com>
Cc: procurement <procurement@horizonhealth.com>
Date: 2025-10-29T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive workshop — preliminary safety & logistics items

Laura — quick pass from Security & Compliance (high level):

Summary recommendations:
- Incident taxonomy:
  - Level 1 (Minor): non-disruptive medical issues, lost property — notify event lead & CS rep within 1 hour.
  - Level 2 (Serious): require medical attention/ambulance — notify security lead + PR (if customer-facing) and legal; start an incident report within 24 hours.
  - Level 3 (Critical): evacuation, violent incident, or fatality — immediate 911, notify exec sponsor, and Redwood CISO on pager.

- Notification chain (internal): Event Lead (Marissa) -> Laura (Director) -> Vivek (Sales/Account) -> Mina (Compliance) -> CISO if Level 3.
- Data handling: any PHI or health data collected (e.g., vaccination status) must be stored only in our encrypted customer folder and deleted after 90 days unless customer requests otherwise.
- Medical support: recommend budget for a contracted on-site nurse (8am–6pm daily) for three days for exec-level events; cost estimate ~ $2,000/day.
- Insurance: require participants to have travel health insurance; provide recommended provider list.
- Local resources (Boston):
  - Massachusetts General Hospital — Emergency Department: +1 617-726-2000
  - Brigham and Women's Hospital: +1 617-732-5500
  - On-call occupational clinic — contact info in attached PDF.

I attached an "emergency_contacts.pdf" with phone numbers and our escalation template. I also included sample language for a one-page indemnity addendum if procurement needs it.

Mina Haddad
Head, Security & Compliance | Redwood Inference
mina.haddad@redwood.ai

[Attachment: emergency_contacts.pdf (PDF)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Emma Stone <emma.stone@horizonhealth.com>
Date: 2025-10-30T14:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive workshop — preliminary safety & logistics items

Adding ops detail from Events/Onsite:

Logistics & contingencies:
- Primary hotel: The Beacon Hotel — block reserved for Redwood/Horizon. Shuttle service scheduled to run 7am–10pm between the hotel and event space.
- AV/backup: We will have redundant streaming (two encoders) and a standby conferencing room if connectivity fails.
- Backup plan: If travel is disrupted for >30% of attendees, we switch to hybrid with live facilitators on-site and remote execs joining via secure link.
- Local transport advisories: MBTA maintenance expected on Nov 18 evening; recommend providing ride-hail vouchers for attendees that night.

Risk register highlights (from onsite_risk_matrix.xlsx):
- Weather disruption: low-probability, medium-impact — contingency: shift final day agenda by 2 hours and extend virtual Q&A window.
- Venue capacity change: low-probability — we have an alternate room with 40% capacity reduction that can be reserved within 24 hours.

Please confirm whether Horizon prefers Redwood to secure the on-site nurse or if you'd like us to include a line item on the SOW for you to contract locally.

Marissa Cole
Senior Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai | +1 (206) 555-0199

[Attachment: onsite_risk_matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet) — see tab: "Event Risks"]

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Emma Stone <emma.stone@horizonhealth.com>
Date: 2025-11-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive workshop — preliminary safety & logistics items

Thanks team — budget note and a request:

- Approve up to $7,000 for on-site nurse + incident contingency reserve (covers nurse for 3 days + minor incident reimbursements). OK from my side.
- Please prepare a 1-page indemnity addendum for procurement. If Horizon needs us to sign an addendum, route via DocuSign; link below when ready.

Also, Laura — can we include an optional daily symptom check form for attendees (self-attestation)? If so, make it optional and routed directly to a secure folder for Horizon only. Mina — ping me if that requires extra controls.

Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Sales, East | Redwood Inference
vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai | +1 (212) 555-0177

Related DocuSign (placeholder): https://www.docusign.com/agreements/ABC-EX-SAFE-2025

From: Emma Stone <emma.stone@horizonhealth.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-02T16:43:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive workshop — preliminary safety & logistics items

Laura & team,

Thanks for the thorough draft — Horizon has reviewed the materials and we are generally aligned. A few quick items from our side:

- Horizon will not require Redwood to provide a nurse; we can provide on-site clinical support but appreciate Redwood's proposal to budget for it. We will confirm and inform procurement by Friday.
- We can share our internal emergency contact list (attached) and our preferred indemnity language; I redlined a single paragraph in your draft.
- We appreciate the optional symptom check. Our legal team asks that any health attestations be stored only on Horizon-controlled systems; can Redwood provide the form and we will host it?

Forwarding our travel checklist and HR emergency contacts for your intake.

On Tue, Oct 30, 2025 at 14:22, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Adding ops detail from Events/Onsite:
>
> Logistics & contingencies:
> - Primary hotel: The Beacon Hotel — block reserved for Redwood/Horizon. Shuttle service scheduled to run 7am–10pm between the hotel and event space.
> - AV/backup: We will have redundant streaming (two encoders) and a standby conferencing room if connectivity fails.

Emma Stone
VP, Strategic Partnerships | Horizon Health
emma.stone@horizonhealth.com | +1 (617) 555-0844

[Attachment: travel_checklist.docx (from Horizon)]

- Data handling: any PHI or health data collected (e.g., vaccination status) must be stored only in our encrypted customer folder and deleted after 90 days unless customer requests otherwise.

source.subject: Planned: EU-West priority-tenant drain for NIC security patch (opt-in/opt-out)
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: ops@acme-financial.com, kate.nguyen@fintech.io
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-10-12T08:12:00Z
Subject: Planned: EU-West priority-tenant drain for NIC security patch (opt-in/opt-out)

Hi team,

We're scheduling a targeted maintenance activity in eu-west to deploy a critical NIC driver security patch that addresses an out-of-band CVE affecting some 100Gbps NIC microcode paths. The change requires a rolling tenant-aware drain for affected hosts to preserve KV cache and avoid elevated tail latency during the update.

Why this matters: the patch fixes a vulnerability that could cause packet drops under specific workloads and we've observed small error-rate spikes on a canary fleet. We want to be proactive for customers with high-throughput inference pools.

Planned approach (high level):
- Tenant-aware rolling drain: instances will be drained of active connections and warm KV caches saved where possible.
- Staggered windows across availability zones to avoid region-wide disruption.
- Priority opt-in: customers on committed Dedicated pools and those flagged as \"priority tenants\" will be offered earliest windows and a coordinated rollback plan.

Proposed windows for priority tenants (please confirm availability):
- Option A: 2025-11-02 00:00 - 04:00 UTC
- Option B: 2025-11-03 22:00 - 02:00 UTC

Attachments: runbook (eu-west-nic-patch-runbook.pdf) and a CSV of the current priority tenant list (priority-tenant-list.csv).

Ops@acme / Kate: please let us know which window you prefer and any blackout constraints.

Next steps (internal): I'll collect preferred windows and crowdsource a short pre-maint checklist (load low, KV snapshot validation). Grace, can you coordinate the communications page and status banner? Jonas, please vet the rollback steps and Vanessa, can you confirm SRE on-call coverage?

Thanks,
Rafael

--
Rafael Mendes
Senior Technical Account Manager
Redwood Inference
From: ops@acme-financial.com
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-10-12T11:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Planned: EU-West priority-tenant drain for NIC security patch (opt-in/opt-out)

Rafael,

Thanks for the heads up. We appreciate the priority window offer. A couple quick notes from our side:
- Blackout: We have a scheduled batch job nightly 01:30-02:15 UTC that is sensitive to latency.
- Can you confirm expected KV cache impact during the drain if a warm snapshot fails?
- Will there be a read-only fallback or traffic migration option?

Preference: Option A (2025-11-02) would be better for our team, but only if the critical path avoids 01:30-02:15. If Option A is a hard no for that sub-window, we may take Option B.

Regards,
M. Patel
Platform Ops, Acme Financial
ops@acme-financial.com
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: ops@acme-financial.com
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-10-12T12:10:00Z
Subject: Re: Planned: EU-West priority-tenant drain for NIC security patch (opt-in/opt-out)

Maya / Acme Ops — good questions. Short answers below, longer details are in the runbook.

- Blackout window: we can avoid 01:30-02:15 by shifting the per-host drain schedule within Option A. We will explicitly mark that sub-window as blackout for your tenant.
- KV snapshot failure: if we cannot persist a warm KV snapshot we will first attempt an in-place KV cache reconciliation step (micro-retries of kvcache write). If that fails we will fall back to a cold restart of the runtime but will keep dependency routing active to the tenant's other healthy replicas where available. We expect higher latency for the affected tenant for up to two minutes in the cold-restart fallback scenario.
- Traffic migration: there is no seamless cross-region migration in this plan; we will attempt intra-region failover to other host replicas in the same pool. We will coordinate route weighting to minimize client disruption.

Internal notes / action items:
- Jonas: confirm rollback concrete steps (snapshot restore path + kernel rollback command) and timing constraints.
- Vanessa: confirm SRE on-call and that we have a dedicated war-room channel.
- Grace: update the status page with tenant-specific blackout windows and include a per-tenant opt-out link.

I'll hold Option A for Acme and mark the 01:30-02:15 blackout for their tenant. Please confirm by EOD.

Thanks,
Rafael
From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: ops@acme-financial.com, kate.nguyen@fintech.io
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-10-12T16:45:00Z
Subject: RE: Planned: EU-West priority-tenant drain for NIC security patch (opt-in/opt-out)

Hi Maya / Kate,

Thanks for the quick reply and the constraints — noted.

Summary / what to expect:
- Window reserved: 2025-11-02 00:00 - 04:00 UTC (Acme: blackout honored 01:30-02:15).
- Runbook: eu-west-nic-patch-runbook.pdf (attached) — includes step-by-step drain, validation checks, and rollback commands.
- Paging & war room: #war-eu-west-maintenance (invites sent), SRE lead Vanessa Ortiz.
- Opt-out: If you prefer to opt-out or pick the Option B window, please reply with \"OPT-OUT\" or \"REQUEST-B\" by 2025-10-15 23:59 UTC.

We will publish a status banner with tenant-specific timing and an opt-out link later today. If there are any additional constraints (eg. extra replication replicas to warm) let us know and we'll add them to the pre-checklist.

Regards,
Grace

--
Grace O'Connor
VP, Customer Operations
Redwood Inference

To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-10-12T11:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Planned: EU-West priority-tenant drain for NIC security patch (opt-in/opt-out)

Rafael,

Thanks for the heads up. We appreciate the priority window offer. A couple quick notes from our side:
- Blackout: We have a scheduled batch job nightly 01:30-02:15 UTC that is sensitive to latency.
- Can you confirm expected KV cache impact during the drain if a warm snapshot fails?
- Will there be a read-only fallback or traffic migration option?

Preference: Option A (2025-11-02) would be better for our team, but only if the critical path avoids 01:30-02:15. If Option A is a hard no for that sub-window, we may take Option B.

Regards,
M. Patel
Platform Ops, Acme Financial
ops@acme-financial.com


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: ops@acme-financial.com
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-10-12T12:10:00Z
Subject: Re: Planned: EU-West priority-tenant drain for NIC security patch (opt-in/opt-out)

Maya / Acme Ops — good questions. Short answers below, longer details are in the runbook.

- Blackout window: we can avoid 01:30-02:15 by shifting the per-host drain schedule within Option A. We will explicitly mark that sub-window as blackout for your tenant.
- KV snapshot failure: if we cannot persist a warm KV snapshot we will first attempt an in-place KV cache reconciliation step (micro-retries of kvcache write). If that fails we will fall back to a cold restart of the runtime but will keep dependency routing active to the tenant's other healthy replicas where available. We expect higher latency for the affected tenant for up to two minutes in the cold-restart fallback scenario.
- Traffic migration: there is no seamless cross-region migration in this plan; we will attempt intra-region failover to other host replicas in the same pool. We will coordinate route weighting to minimize client disruption.

Internal notes / action items:
- Jonas: confirm rollback concrete steps (snapshot restore path + kernel rollback command) and timing constraints.
- Vanessa: confirm SRE on-call and that we have a dedicated war-room channel.
- Grace: update the status page with tenant-specific blackout windows and include a per-tenant opt-out link.

I'll hold Option A for Acme and mark the 01:30-02:15 blackout for their tenant. Please confirm by EOD.

Thanks,
Rafael


From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: ops@acme-financial.com, kate.nguyen@fintech.io
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-10-12T16:45:00Z
Subject: RE: Planned: EU-West priority-tenant drain for NIC security patch (opt-in/opt-out)

Hi Maya / Kate,

Thanks for the quick reply and the constraints — noted.

Summary / what to expect:
- Window reserved: 2025-11-02 00:00 - 04:00 UTC (Acme: blackout honored 01:30-02:15).
- Runbook: eu-west-nic-patch-runbook.pdf (attached) — includes step-by-step drain, validation checks, and rollback commands.
- Paging & war room: #war-eu-west-maintenance (invites sent), SRE lead Vanessa Ortiz.
- Opt-out: If you prefer to opt-out or pick the Option B window, please reply with \"OPT-OUT\" or \"REQUEST-B\" by 2025-10-15 23:59 UTC.

We will publish a status banner with tenant-specific timing and an opt-out link later today. If there are any additional constraints (eg. extra replication replicas to warm) let us know and we'll add them to the pre-checklist.

Regards,
Grace

--
Grace O'Connor
VP, Customer Operations
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Clarify: default retention for request traces, prompts, and model outputs
messages:
From: Ava Collins <ava.collins@neptune-fin.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Default retention for request traces and model outputs - procurement Q

Hi Rafael,

We're finalizing our procurement checklist and need a clear statement of Redwood's default data retention for a few artifact classes so our privacy team can sign off. Specifically: request traces (raw request text + headers), user prompts, model outputs (generated text), and any interim streaming buffers (if used). Also need to know the default residency for those logs (US/EU) and whether proofs of deletion are available.

I've attached a short spreadsheet with the fields procurement wants filled (column per artifact type). Can you confirm the default values we should put in? This can be high level — we just need the initial values for the SOW.

Appreciate it,
Ava Collins
Security & Privacy, Neptune Financial
[Attachment: neptune-data-questions.xlsx]

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Ava Collins <ava.collins@neptune-fin.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Sep 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Default retention for request traces and model outputs - procurement Q

Thanks for the spreadsheet, Ava — good questions. High level answers (default behavior in Hosted mode unless customer requests otherwise):

- Request traces (raw request bodies + metadata): retained by default for 30 days. This covers complete request payloads, headers we log for routing, and any K/V session cache snapshots.
- Prompts (distinct "user input" field captured as prompt text): treated as user-submitted inputs; default retention is 30 days aligned with request traces. Customers can opt into shorter windows or request immediate non-retention via account settings or API flags at onboarding.
- Model outputs (generated text responses): by default we retain generated outputs for 30 days when associated with a request for debugging and billing reconciliation. Outputs are considered derivable artifacts and can be excluded from default capture when the customer enables strict no-capture mode.
- Streaming/interim buffers: streaming tokens are ephemeral. Anything in a transient buffer is only persisted if the final response is captured under the default retention rules above.

Residency: by default logs and artifacts are stored in the region tied to the customer's account (US regions for US customers). For EU residency customers we store data exclusively in EU regions — this must be requested during onboarding or via an account migration.

Proofs of deletion: we can provide deletion evidence for account-scoped erasure in the form of a runbook entry and a deletion job ID. For SOC 2/in-audit artifacts we provide a confirmation export (signed runbook excerpt) showing the deletion job and tombstone timestamps.

Next steps: Kimberly / Neha — can you add any details on retention exceptions (billing, legal hold) and the formal wording procurement prefers for SOWs? Ava, I can put the answers into your spreadsheet and return a short procurement-ready paragraph.

— Rafael
Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: soc2-data-retention-excerpt.pdf]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Ava Collins <ava.collins@neptune-fin.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Default retention for request traces and model outputs - procurement Q

Adding a few operational clarifications for procurement wording:

- Exceptions: Default 30-day retention is overridden when (a) customer enables a longer retention window, (b) legal hold is requested, or (c) data is retained for billing reconciliation up to 365 days in aggregated (non-identifiable) telemetry. We do not retain PII beyond configured windows unless required by subpoena/legal hold.
- Deletion workflow: A standard account erasure request triggers an asynchronous deletion job. We will remove primary artifacts and replace records with tombstones that indicate deletion timestamps. The deletion job returns a Job ID (e.g., DEL-YYYYMMDD-XXXXX) and a log snippet that can be shared for audit.
- Residual backups: Backups are cycled and are not used for data recovery after a deletion job; however, per standard backup TTLs, it can take up to 7 days for all backup snapshots to expire and be removed from all mediums. We can include a backup-expiry note in the SOW if the customer needs a shorter window (requires a support plan addendum).

Procurement phrasing suggestion (short):
"Redwood's hosted inference default capture retains request payloads, user prompts, and generated outputs for 30 days. Customers may request shorter or longer retention windows; data residency (US/EU) is selectable at account creation or via migration. Deletion requests produce a deletion job ID and an audit runbook excerpt as proof of erasure. Exceptions for legal holds and billing reconciliation are applied per account and documented in the SOW."

Ava — happy to refine if Neptune wants more explicit lines about backups or longer-term aggregated telemetry.

Kimberly Park
Director, Product Operations — Redwood Inference
[Internal note: see INF-4421 for deletion job schema and sample runbook]

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Default retention for request traces and model outputs - procurement Q

Neha — forwarding this thread. Can you draft a short, procurement-ready statement for Neptune (one paragraph + a bullet for exceptions) and attach the SOC2 excerpt Kimberly referenced? They asked for language to paste into the SOW and for a deletion-proof example.

Forwarded message:
> From: Ava Collins <ava.collins@neptune-fin.com>
> To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
> Date: Wed, 10 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
> Subject: Default retention for request traces and model outputs - procurement Q
>
> [original message omitted]

Thanks — please keep this brief. I'll route the final text back to Ava.

— Rafael
Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Vendr evidence: clarifying manifest/attachment convention for Acme profile
messages:
From: Elena Torres <elena@vendr.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick question on proof-of-possession attachments for Acme

Hi Rafael —

Thanks again for starting the Vendr profile for Acme Payments. Before we mark the basic profile as complete I'd like to confirm what you want to include as 'proof of possession' for the S3 snapshots. Our minimal flow accepts either a signed manifest CSV or a zip of logs. Can Redwood provide a signed manifest, or do you prefer we accept a temporary presigned S3 bundle?

If you can drop the manifest into the portal I can reference it in our SIG ingest. For reference, our portal will accept a small CSV (name, path, sha256) and a signed cover note.

Thanks,

Elena Torres
Vendor Onboarding, Vendr
elena@vendr.com

Attachment(s): none

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena@vendr.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2025 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on proof-of-possession attachments for Acme

Elena —

Appreciate the note. We prefer to upload a signed manifest (CSV) to the portal rather than presigned S3 links for this account. Kimberly will generate the manifest from the evidence bucket and sign it with the KMS key. Two quick asks:

- Can Vendr accept a CSV that references objects in a bucket we control (no public presigns) so long as the CSV includes sha256 and a signed cover note?
- Is there a preferred filename convention you'd like (e.g., acme_proof_manifest_20250914.csv)?

Jordan, can you own the upload once Kimberly signs it?

Rafael
Head of Partnerships
Redwood Inference

Attachment(s): none

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena@vendr.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2025 13:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on proof-of-possession attachments for Acme

Hi all —

Confirming we will produce a manifest CSV and a short signed cover note. Plan:

1) I will export object list and SHA256 for the Acme evidence prefix (s3://redwood-evidence/acme/).
2) Generate manifest acme_proof_manifest_20250914.csv with columns: filename, s3_path, sha256, size_bytes.
3) Sign a cover_note_acme_20250914.txt using our KMS signing key and attach both to the Vendr portal.

Jordan — once I upload, can you complete the portal form and flag the claim 'SOC2-snapshot' so we don't need a full log bundle?

Raf — attaching a short sample of the CSV and the cover note draft for Elena to confirm the format.

Best,

Kimberly Park
Security Operations Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachment(s): acme_proof_manifest_sample.csv (sample), cover_note_draft.txt (draft)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena@vendr.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2025 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on proof-of-possession attachments for Acme

All good — I'll take the upload after Kimberly signs. Quick checklist I'm following:

- Confirm manifest filename: acme_proof_manifest_20250914.csv
- Upload manifest + cover note to: https://app.vendr.com/portal/ACME/claim/12345
- Mark claim type: SOC2-snapshot
- Set TTL on claim to 90 days (per Acme procurement request)

I'll upload today and ping here with the portal confirmation.

Jordan
Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachment(s): none

From: Elena Torres <elena@vendr.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2025 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on proof-of-possession attachments for Acme

Thanks — that works. Portal accepts the CSV + signed cover note. Two final clarifications:

1) Filename convention above is fine.
2) Please add the uploaded manifest as a claim and include the link to the object list in the comment field (we'll not fetch from your bucket; we only record the manifest for SIG/SOC2 evidence).

Once uploaded I'll mark the profile as 'evidence received' and loop Acme's security reviewer if anything else is needed.

Cheers,

Elena Torres
Vendor Onboarding, Vendr

Attachment(s): none

source.subject: BrightBridge Summit — attendee voucher allocation (promo codes)
messages:
From: Elise Thornton <elise@brightbridge.co>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Cc: events@brightbridge.co
Date: Mon, 1 Sep 2025 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Voucher batch request for BrightBridge Summer Summit booth

Hi Hannah —

Thanks again for agreeing to sponsor the BrightBridge Summer Summit table this year. We had a last-minute ask from our marketing team: could Redwood provide 150 attendee promo vouchers (one-use trial credits) to distribute at the event? They want short single-use codes to hand out to attendees that unlock a 30-day trial with $50 of credits.

I attached the attendee distribution CSV (columns: code_id, intended_recipient, notes) as a placeholder — we won't publish recipient names, but the ops team wants a clear mapping for auditing.

From our compliance side: would Redwood be able to confirm data retention for the codes (expiry window and whether we can set single-use enforcement)? Also, any logo placement requirements for co-marketing in the confirmation email?

Elise
Event Partnerships, BrightBridge
Attachments: BrightBridge_voucher_list.csv

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 1 Sep 2025 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Voucher batch request for BrightBridge Summer Summit booth

Forwarding the request from BrightBridge — they want 150 single-use promo vouchers ($50 credit / 30-day trial).

Two asks for ops/legal before I reply to Elise:
- Can we enforce single-use codes and an expiration policy of 60 days from issue?
- Any constraints on co-marketing (we typically include a small Redwood footer in partner emails) — Rafael, do we need signoff for logo usage here?

Vanessa — can you confirm whether credits come from the promo pool (Q3 marketing credits) or whether we need a special one-off allocation from sales? If we need to bill, Marcus can advise on accounting treatment.

Goal: get back to BrightBridge with an answer today if possible — they go to print on Wednesday for the physical handouts.

Thanks,
Hannah
Director, Partnerships
Redwood Inference

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 1 Sep 2025 13:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Voucher batch request for BrightBridge Summer Summit booth

Hannah — quick update from marketing ops: we have a Q3 promo pool dedicated to events (code block: EVNT-Q3-2025) with ~4k remaining in face value. 150 * $50 = $7,500 face value — within budget but will need a manual allocation entry.

If you want these to be single-use and 60-day expiration, we can generate them via the promo service but need the following:
- final code naming convention (we usually prefix with BRB- to track partner)
- confirmation that codes will be redeemed only for hosted trials (not embeddable for private deployments)

I can spin up the batch today if finance approves the allocation. Attaching the public promo-policy excerpt for legal constraints.

Attachments: redwood-promo-policy-sep2025.pdf

-Vanessa
Marketing Ops Manager

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 2 Sep 2025 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Voucher batch request for BrightBridge Summer Summit booth

Confirmed — finance is OK to allocate $7,500 from the Q3 events pool. I just need the final batch name and a note that these are partner-sponsored promotional credits (for our internal ledger). No additional PO required for this amount.

Rafael — Rafael, please confirm logo usage for the co-branded email. If legal is happy with the standard footer and a single-line co-sponsor acknowledgement, we can proceed.

Once Rafael signs off, Vanessa can request the batch and I'll mark the allocation in the budget tracker.

Thanks,
Marcus
Head of Revenue Operations

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>, Elise Thornton <elise@brightbridge.co>
Date: Thu, 4 Sep 2025 16:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Voucher batch request for BrightBridge Summer Summit booth

Hi all —

Legal has reviewed the promo-policy excerpt and the proposed co-marketing text. We are OK with the standard Redwood footer and a single-line: "Sponsored by Redwood Inference" on the event handout. No special contract amendment required for this voucher batch.

Operational notes (please keep for audit):
- Batch name: BRB-SUMMIT-SEP25
- Quantity: 150 single-use codes
- Credit: $50 redeemable on hosted Redwood API accounts only
- Expiry: 60 days from issuance
- Redemption limiter: single-use per code; codes bound to first registered email address

Vanessa — please generate the codes and share the final CSV with both BrightBridge and Finance. Marcus — mark the $7,500 allocation to the EVNT-Q3-2025 line.

Elise — once you confirm where the codes will be printed (handouts vs. QR cards), we will send the final CSV.

Cheers,
Rafael Mendes
Sr. Counsel, Redwood Inference

source.subject: SIG/CAIQ intake — SCIM delta & RBAC evidence for Medify
messages:
From: Alex Jones <alex.jones@medify.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 7 Aug 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG intake — SCIM delta & RBAC evidence (Medify)

Hi Rafael / Monica,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Our SOC and procurement teams are running the SIG/CAIQ intake for Redwood as part of our vendor approval. To keep things moving we need a small package of artifacts focused on identity & access controls (SSO/SAML, SCIM, RBAC). Specifically: 

- One or two SCIM delta samples (CSV) showing user provisioning events and role assignments over a 48h window. Redacted PII is fine.
- A role mapping spreadsheet (who maps to which Redwood roles + owner) and any short notes about least-privilege enforcement.
- One SAML assertion example (not a real private key, just the assertion/sample headers) showing the NameID and common attributes.
- A short narrative (<= 250 words) describing how you enforce least privilege for customer accounts — we use that for SIG questions.

Target for our procurement review is next Friday, Aug 14. Can you confirm whether you can provide the sample artifacts by EOD Tue Aug 11 and whether you can deliver them via a secure Drive link or an SFTP drop? Also let us know if you need an NDA or specific consent language for user data samples.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Jones
Security Program Manager — Medify Health
alex.jones@medify.com

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Alex Jones <alex.jones@medify.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 7 Aug 2025 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG intake — SCIM delta & RBAC evidence (Medify)

Hi Alex — thanks for the clear list. We can provide the artifacts you requested. Quick plan: 

1) SCIM delta sample (48h slice, PII redacted) — Monica will produce a CSV export with event timestamps and attribute diffs.
2) RBAC mapping workbook — we’ll attach a role -> permission -> owner sheet and a short owner-attestation column.
3) SAML assertion example — we’ll include an anonymized assertion (headers + attributes only).
4) Least-privilege narrative — I’ll draft the 200–250 word explanation and include references to our role review cadence.

If that works we’ll upload the artifacts to a locked Drive folder and share a link. We do not need an NDA for redacted samples, but if you prefer we can generate the samples under a limited-scope processing agreement — let me know.

Deliverable timeline: artifacts uploaded by Tue Aug 11 EOD PT. If procurement needs them sooner we can accelerate one item (SAML example) by EOD Mon Aug 10.

Next steps: Monica will confirm the SCIM export window and Sean will validate the role-owner attestations.

—Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
rafael_mendes@redwood.com

From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 7 Aug 2025 13:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG intake — SCIM delta & RBAC evidence (Medify)

Rafael / Sean — I can pull the SCIM delta slice. Notes/constraints: 

- I’ll export a 48-hour window from our provisioning system (UTC 2025-08-03T00:00 to 2025-08-04T00:00 as a safe example) — will redact emails and replace with user IDs.

Hi Rafael / Monica,

source.subject: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Elena —

Thanks again for the productive call yesterday. BrightCore's procurement team asked us to confirm how claim notices and evidence handoff would work operationally before we finalize the order form. To keep things moving I attached a short matrix that maps: notice windows, evidence responsibilities, and a proposed defense-control compromise.

High-level asks from your side we heard:
- 30 day notice window for alleged third-party IP claims
- BrightCore prefers to retain defendant control for any defense where you are named
- Evidence export must meet your procurement checklist (PII redaction, chain-of-custody)

Our suggested middle-ground (in doc):
- 45 day notice window to allow triage and for us to collect technical evidence
- Right to assume defense when Redwood reasonably determines it is in the best interest of the defense, with mandatory cooperation and limited reimbursement for third-party counsel fees where applicable
- Evidence checklist with secure upload link and 7-day SLA for acknowledgement

Can you review the attached redlines and tell us which items are deal-breakers for procurement? If necessary, Kim from Legal is looped for contract tradeoffs.

Thanks,
Rafael
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
Attachments: BrightCore_Redlines_v2.docx (redlines), evidence-checklist.xlsx (template)

From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Chris Nolan <chris.nolan@brightcore.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Rafael —

Thanks for the summary and the redlines. I reviewed the doc with our procurement counsel. A few notes from our side:

1) Notice window: procurement insists on 30 days. Anything longer exposes our procurement cadence.
2) Defense control: we can accept a limited right for Redwood to step in only if Redwood pays for counsel and any settlement you negotiate. If Redwood takes control, BrightCore needs approval rights for any settlement over $50k.
3) Evidence: we'll use your evidence checklist but require that the first transfer be validated by our security team; that validation may take up to 10 business days for the first incident.

I've attached a version with inline comments and a suggested compromise in section 6 (defense_control). If we can't land this in the order form we will need to push to procurement leadership for an exception, which will delay signature.

Elena
Senior Procurement Manager — BrightCore
Attachments: BrightCore_Redlines_v2.docx (BrightCore comments)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Kim —

Forwarding BrightCore's comments (attached). Procurement is firm on 30 days and wants control-preserving language on settlement approval. Their suggested $50k approval threshold is new.

Can Legal propose a short clause that preserves BrightCore's approval on large settlements but allows Redwood to assume defense earlier for technical triage? We want to avoid multiple exec exception cycles.

Key constraints:
- Procurement timeline: we have two-week runway before their quarter-end procurement freeze.
- Ops/security: they require first-incident validation up to 10 business days.

Suggested quick options from Sales:
A) Accept 30-day notice, Redwood gets defense control after 14 days if BrightCore hasn't engaged counsel and Redwood covers counsel costs; settlement approval for amounts > $50k requires BrightCore sign-off.
B) Keep our 45-day notice, but provide a documented expedite path (engineer-led evidence handoff + 72-hour acknowledgement SLA) to get to defense conversation sooner.

Appreciate a short redline we can send back today.

Rafael
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

On Tue, Jul 22, 2025 at 11:02 AM Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com> wrote:
> Rafael —
> 
> Thanks for the summary and the redlines. I reviewed the doc with our procurement counsel. A few notes from our side:
> 
> 1) Notice window: procurement insists on 30 days. Anything longer exposes our procurement cadence.
> 2) Defense control: we can accept a limited right for Redwood to step in only if Redwood pays for counsel and any settlement you negotiate. If Redwood takes control, BrightCore needs approval rights for any settlement over $50k.
> 3) Evidence: we'll use your evidence checklist but require that the first transfer be validated by our security team; that validation may take up to 10 business days for the first incident.
> 
> I've attached a version with inline comments and a suggested compromise in section 6 (defense_control). If we can't land this in the order form we will need to push to procurement leadership for an exception, which will delay signature.
> 
> Elena
> Senior Procurement Manager — BrightCore
> Attachments: BrightCore_Redlines_v2.docx (BrightCore comments)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Rafael —

Thanks for the flag and for forwarding BrightCore's notes. Below is a concise redline proposal we can push back with today that balances procurement concerns and our operational needs.

Proposed compromise (to insert into 6. Defense & Third-Party Claims):
- Notice: 30 calendar days from receipt of written notice.
- Defense control: BrightCore may initially control the defense. Redwood may assume control if: (i) BrightCore fails to reasonably and promptly pursue the defense within 14 calendar days after receipt of Redwood's written request; or (ii) prompt technical access is required to preserve evidence and Redwood provides written notice and pays reasonable defense costs incurred after assumption.
- Settlement approval: Redwood will not settle any claim that imposes any payment or obligation on BrightCore without BrightCore's prior written consent for settlements > $50,000. For settlements <= $50,000 Redwood will provide 7 business days' notice before execution.
- Evidence transfer: Redwood will provide a secure evidence upload with authenticated transfer. BrightCore security validation for first incident acknowledged; Redwood acknowledges a 10-business day validation window for first transfer and will expedite thereafter per the evidence-checklist template.
- Cooperation: each party will cooperate and provide reasonable assistance; Redwood will not disclose BrightCore customer data outside counsel without written consent except as required by law.

Rationale: this keeps procurement's 30-day ask, gives Redwood a time-limited path to assume control for triage and preservation, preserves BrightCore's veto on larger settlements, and documents the security validation expectation.

If Sales wants to present both option A (faster assumption + settlement cap) and option B (45-day notice with expedite path) I can draft both redlines, but my preference is to keep to a single clean compromise to avoid confusion during procurement review.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0172

Attachments: none

On Tue, Jul 22, 2025 at 1:30 PM Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com> wrote:
> Kim —
> 
> Forwarding BrightCore's comments (attached). Procurement is firm on 30 days and wants control-preserving language on settlement approval. Their suggested $50k approval threshold is new.
> 
> Can Legal propose a short clause that preserves BrightCore's approval on large settlements but allows Redwood to assume defense earlier for technical triage? We want to avoid multiple exec exception cycles.
> 
> Rafael
> Attachments: BrightCore_Redlines_v2.docx, evidence-checklist.xlsx

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
Date: Wed, 23 Jul 2025 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Team —

That's a clear path. I suggest we send Kim's single-compromise redline back to BrightCore with a short cover note: call out procurement's 30-day ask, confirm Redwood's 14-day assume-defense trigger for triage, and confirm BrightCore's settlement approval threshold. Offer a 30-minute alignment call with procurement + legal tomorrow (Thu) at 10am PT.

Elena — can you do 10am PT tomorrow for a quick alignment call? If not, propose an alternative and we'll send the redline in parallel. We need signature before the procurement freeze at month-end.

To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Elena —

Thanks again for the productive call yesterday. BrightCore's procurement team asked us to confirm how claim notices and evidence handoff would work operationally before we finalize the order form. To keep things moving I attached a short matrix that maps: notice windows, evidence responsibilities, and a proposed defense-control compromise.

High-level asks from your side we heard:
- 30 day notice window for alleged third-party IP claims
- BrightCore prefers to retain defendant control for any defense where you are named
- Evidence export must meet your procurement checklist (PII redaction, chain-of-custody)

Our suggested middle-ground (in doc):
- 45 day notice window to allow triage and for us to collect technical evidence
- Right to assume defense when Redwood reasonably determines it is in the best interest of the defense, with mandatory cooperation and limited reimbursement for third-party counsel fees where applicable
- Evidence checklist with secure upload link and 7-day SLA for acknowledgement

Can you review the attached redlines and tell us which items are deal-breakers for procurement? If necessary, Kim from Legal is looped for contract tradeoffs.

Thanks,
Rafael
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
Attachments: BrightCore_Redlines_v2.docx (redlines), evidence-checklist.xlsx (template)

From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Chris Nolan <chris.nolan@brightcore.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Rafael —

Thanks for the summary and the redlines. I reviewed the doc with our procurement counsel. A few notes from our side:

1) Notice window: procurement insists on 30 days. Anything longer exposes our procurement cadence.
2) Defense control: we can accept a limited right for Redwood to step in only if Redwood pays for counsel and any settlement you negotiate. If Redwood takes control, BrightCore needs approval rights for any settlement over $50k.
3) Evidence: we'll use your evidence checklist but require that the first transfer be validated by our security team; that validation may take up to 10 business days for the first incident.

I've attached a version with inline comments and a suggested compromise in section 6 (defense_control). If we can't land this in the order form we will need to push to procurement leadership for an exception, which will delay signature.

Elena
Senior Procurement Manager — BrightCore
Attachments: BrightCore_Redlines_v2.docx (BrightCore comments)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Kim —

Forwarding BrightCore's comments (attached). Procurement is firm on 30 days and wants control-preserving language on settlement approval. Their suggested $50k approval threshold is new.

Can Legal propose a short clause that preserves BrightCore's approval on large settlements but allows Redwood to assume defense earlier for technical triage? We want to avoid multiple exec exception cycles.

Key constraints:
- Procurement timeline: we have two-week runway before their quarter-end procurement freeze.
- Ops/security: they require first-incident validation up to 10 business days.

Suggested quick options from Sales:
A) Accept 30-day notice, Redwood gets defense control after 14 days if BrightCore hasn't engaged counsel and Redwood covers counsel costs; settlement approval for amounts > $50k requires BrightCore sign-off.
B) Keep our 45-day notice, but provide a documented expedite path (engineer-led evidence handoff + 72-hour acknowledgement SLA) to get to defense conversation sooner.

Appreciate a short redline we can send back today.

Rafael
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

On Tue, Jul 22, 2025 at 11:02 AM Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com> wrote:
> Rafael —
> 
> Thanks for the summary and the redlines. I reviewed the doc with our procurement counsel. A few notes from our side:
> 
> 1) Notice window: procurement insists on 30 days. Anything longer exposes our procurement cadence.
> 2) Defense control: we can accept a limited right for Redwood to step in only if Redwood pays for counsel and any settlement you negotiate. If Redwood takes control, BrightCore needs approval rights for any settlement over $50k.
> 3) Evidence: we'll use your evidence checklist but require that the first transfer be validated by our security team; that validation may take up to 10 business days for the first incident.
> 
> I've attached a version with inline comments and a suggested compromise in section 6 (defense_control). If we can't land this in the order form we will need to push to procurement leadership for an exception, which will delay signature.
> 
> Elena
> Senior Procurement Manager — BrightCore
> Attachments: BrightCore_Redlines_v2.docx (BrightCore comments)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@brightcore.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order Form: Claim-notice SLA matrix & defense control proposal

Rafael —

Thanks for the flag and for forwarding BrightCore's notes. Below is a concise redline proposal we can push back with today that balances procurement concerns and our operational needs.

Proposed compromise (to insert into 6. Defense & Third-Party Claims):
- Notice: 30 calendar days from receipt of written notice.
- Defense control: BrightCore may initially control the defense. Redwood may assume control if: (i) BrightCore fails to reasonably and promptly pursue the defense within 14 calendar days after receipt of Redwood's written request; or (ii) prompt technical access is required to preserve evidence and Redwood provides written notice and pays reasonable defense costs incurred after assumption.
- Settlement approval: Redwood will not settle any claim that imposes any payment or obligation on BrightCore without BrightCore's prior written consent for settlements > $50,000. For settlements <= $50,000 Redwood will provide 7 business days' notice before execution.
- Evidence transfer: Redwood will provide a secure evidence upload with authenticated transfer. BrightCore security validation for first incident acknowledged; Redwood acknowledges a 10-business day validation window for first transfer and will expedite thereafter per the evidence-checklist template.
- Cooperation: each party will cooperate and provide reasonable assistance; Redwood will not disclose BrightCore customer data outside counsel without written consent except as required by law.

Rationale: this keeps procurement's 30-day ask, gives Redwood a time-limited path to assume control for triage and preservation, preserves BrightCore's veto on larger settlements, and documents the security validation expectation.

If Sales wants to present both option A (faster assumption + settlement cap) and option B (45-day notice with expedite path) I can draft both redlines, but my preference is to keep to a single clean compromise to avoid confusion during procurement review.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0172

Attachments: none
