source.subject: Vendor setup: BrightWave - marketplace procurement and PO routing
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Malik <sara@brightwave.io>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor setup: BrightWave - marketplace procurement and PO routing

Hi Sara,

Great to meet you during the call yesterday — thanks for taking time to walk through BrightWave's use case. To keep momentum on the procurement side we need to get your vendor profile set up so our finance and marketplace teams can route POs and list any private offers. Can you send the following items (or confirm if already in your portal)?

- Legal entity name and remit-to address
- W9 (or W-8 if non-US)
- Primary PO/billing contact (name + email + phone)
- Vendor tax ID / VAT or EIN
- Preferred payment terms (NET30/NET45/etc)
- Any existing AWS/Azure/GCP seller IDs for marketplace mapping
- Completed onboarding form (I can share our template if easier)

If you can drop those by end of this week, I’ll map them to our marketplace onboarding checklist and loop in Finance + Marketplace Ops to issue a PO for your first committed pilot. Proposed timeline: vendor docs by 7/18, PO issued 7/21, test listing/private-offer created 7/28.

Thanks — I’ll follow up with internal owners once I have your files.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

[Attachment: RW_Vendor_Procurement_Template.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)]

From: Sara Malik <sara@brightwave.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@brightwave.io
Date: Wed, 16 Jul 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor setup: BrightWave - marketplace procurement and PO routing

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the clear list — attaching what we have.

Attached: BrightWave_W9.pdf; BrightWave_Vendor_Onboarding_Form.xlsx; BrightWave_Security_Questionnaire.xlsx

Quick answers:
- Legal name: BrightWave LLC
- Remit-to: 420 Harbor Way, Suite 300, San Francisco, CA 94107
- EIN: 12-3456789
- Billing contact: Amy Chen (amy.chen@brightwave.io) — procurement@brightwave.io is monitored by billing
- Preferred terms: NET30 (can do NET45 for annual deals)
- AWS Seller ID: A1B2C3D4E5F
- We do have a basic security spreadsheet attached; if you have a more detailed SOC/ISO request let us know and we’ll coordinate with our security team.

Two questions for you before we proceed:
1) Will Redwood create the marketplace listing and route our invoice through your reseller relationship, or do you expect BrightWave to be the primary marketplace seller?
2) Any special PO wording required for private offers on AWS/Azure?

I’m looping in our procurement alias. Happy to jump on a short call if helpful.

—Sara
Sara Malik
Head of Partnerships, BrightWave
sara@brightwave.io
[Attachments: BrightWave_W9.pdf, BrightWave_Vendor_Onboarding_Form.xlsx, BrightWave_Security_Questionnaire.xlsx]

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Malik <sara@brightwave.io>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Jul 2025 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor setup: BrightWave - marketplace procurement and PO routing

Sara — thanks for the prompt response and the attachments. Looping in Laura (Finance) to confirm invoicing/terms and Vanessa (Marketplace Ops) to confirm the technical/marketplace steps.

Quick answers to your questions:
- We support both flows: (A) BrightWave lists as the seller in the marketplace (preferred if you want direct billing to customers), or (B) Redwood acts as a reseller/offer owner for a private offer (common for early pilots). We can do either — Vanessa will advise on which path speeds onboarding.
- PO wording: for private offers we usually need (i) Redwood purchase order number, (ii) invoice to Redwood Inference, and (iii) contract reference RW-2025-BW-12 for tracking. Laura, can you confirm the exact PO wording we should ask BrightWave to include?

Proposed mutual action plan (quick):
- 7/18: BrightWave returns completed onboarding form + W9 + security questionnaire
- 7/21: Redwood Finance issues initial PO (NET30) for pilot fees
- 7/23: Marketplace Ops establishes test private offer / or maps seller IDs depending on chosen flow
- 7/28: Test purchase and billing verification

I’ve attached our procurement template for your convenience. If that timeline looks OK I’ll kick-off the PO request.

Best,
Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: RW_Vendor_Procurement_Template.docx]

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Sara Malik <sara@brightwave.io>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Jul 2025 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor setup: BrightWave - marketplace procurement and PO routing

Hi all — tagging in Marketplace Ops. Below is a concise checklist per cloud to get BrightWave fully mapped and able to receive POs/private-offers.

AWS Marketplace (fast path):
- Seller ID (you provided A1B2C3D4E5F)
- W9 on file
- Signed resale agreement if Redwood will act as reseller
- Contact for fulfillment and invoice delivery (procurement@brightwave.io)
- For private offers: need purchaser account ID + Redwood PO# in invoice

Azure Marketplace:
- Publishable offer (SaaS or VM) with offer id
- Microsoft ISV registration + tax profile
- Invoice remit details + PO reference

GCP Marketplace:
- Partner registration and project mapping
- Billing account and invoice contact

Attachments and links: marketplace_onboarding_checklist.pdf (walks through keysteps and sample PO language).

When you confirm the flow (reseller vs direct seller) we will: 1) create a ticket with each marketplace, 2) run a test transaction, 3) update HubSpot with the marketplace mapping.

Doc notes: For private-offer flows we advise Net30 and invoicing to Redwood with the PO# on the invoice. If BrightWave prefers to be seller-of-record, we will instead map your seller IDs and Redwood will issue invoices directly to customers.

Next steps for BrightWave: send Amy Chen’s contact + remit-to invoice email and confirm whether you want Redwood to hold private offers or have BrightWave listed as seller.

Cheers,
Vanessa Ortiz
Marketplace Operations Manager, Redwood Inference
vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com
[Attachment: redwood_marketplace_onboarding_checklist.pdf]

On Wed, Jul 16, 2025 at 10:05 AM Sara Malik <sara@brightwave.io> wrote:
> Attached: BrightWave_W9.pdf; BrightWave_Vendor_Onboarding_Form.xlsx; BrightWave_Security_Questionnaire.xlsx
>
> Quick answers:
> - Legal name: BrightWave LLC
> - Remit-to: 420 Harbor Way, Suite 300, San Francisco, CA 94107
> - EIN: 12-3456789
> - Billing contact: Amy Chen (amy.chen@brightwave.io)
> - Preferred terms: NET30
> - AWS Seller ID: A1B2C3D4E5F

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Sara Malik <sara@brightwave.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor setup: BrightWave - marketplace procurement and PO routing

Hi Sara — finance here. Thanks for the documents; W9 is in good order. A couple items to finalize so we can issue the PO:

1) Please confirm the remit-to email for invoices (we need a monitored mailbox for PDF invoices).
2) Attach the vendor bank wiring instructions if you accept wires (optional for NET30).
3) Please confirm whether invoices should reference Redwood contract RW-2025-BW-12 and the PO number when we issue it.

We will issue a standard PO (NET30) once the remit contact and invoicing method are confirmed. I attached our PO template showing required fields.

Thanks —
Laura Bennett
Head of Finance, Redwood Inference
laura_bennett@redwood.com
[Attachment: PO_template_redwood_v1.pdf]

source.subject: Post-demo: streaming capacity and peak-slice sizing follow-up
messages:
From: Lukas Meyer <lukas@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@sh-health.com>; Maria Ortega <maria.ortega@sh-health.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: streaming capacity and peak-slice sizing follow-up

Thanks again for taking the demo today — great conversation. This note captures the call outcomes and a short set of asks so we can size a pilot cluster and confirm SLOs. TL;DR: we need a short telemetry sample + confirmation on peak window characteristics to finalize a sizing proposal.

Key takeaways from today's session:
- Primary flows: realtime patient-summary generation (streaming completions) + nightly batch embedding for indexing.
- Desired latency SLO for interactive flows: 95th <= 300ms token roundtrip (goal), with a softer 500ms fall-back during sustained peaks.
- Typical steady-state: ~12 QPS (chat requests) at ~350 tokens per request on average.
- Observed daily peak: a brief crest of ~420 QPS for ~1.5 minutes during morning sync; also smaller crests ~80 QPS for ~10 minutes.
- Streaming specifics: up to 12 concurrent streaming connections per shard, average stream length ~18 tokens/sec, but spikes to 60 tokens/sec during batch summaries.

What we need from you to complete the sizing: 
1) Send a 24–48 hour telemetry extract (API logs) with per-request token counts and timestamps — even a CSV is fine. We referenced an example format in the Drive link.
2) Confirm whether the morning crest is an all-users fan-out (global) or limited to a few tenants — this changes routing/fallback policies.
3) Clarify whether you require strict 95th P95 latency enforcement across all traffic or only for logged-in premium users.

Next steps I will take:
- Rafael will produce a quick pilot sizing (GPU count, shard layout, streaming slot config) and a conservative cost estimate.
- I'll draft an SLA-aligned proposal and a mutual action plan for a 6-week pilot with milestones.

Attachments: I flagged where a traces export would be helpful.

Thanks — I can jump on a short follow-up call if helpful.

— Lukas Meyer
Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
l: +1 415 555 0120
Redwood Inference
From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@sh-health.com>
To: Lukas Meyer <lukas@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Ortega <maria.ortega@sh-health.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: streaming capacity and peak-slice sizing follow-up

Lukas — thanks for the recap and the clear asks. A few clarifications and I attached a small telemetry bundle (sageware-p95-traces-20250630.zip) with 36 hours of anonymized request logs.

Quick answers: 
- The morning crest (420 QPS) is driven by a single scheduled sync across ~120k active users — it fans out across our US region only.
- Premium users (~8% of requests) should have the tighter 95th <= 300ms requirement; standard users are ok with the softer 500ms target.
- Streaming: the 12 concurrent streams you saw are per logical shard for the service — we currently run 3 logical shards per region during steady state. During the crest we see all shards hit ~10–12 active streams each.

Notes on the attached logs: time column is UTC; token_count is approximate (we calculate client-side). Let me know if you need a different export.

— Alex
Alex Turner
Head of Platform Engineering, SageWare Health
alex.turner@sh-health.com
[attachment: sageware-p95-traces-20250630.zip]
From: Lukas Meyer <lukas@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@sh-health.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 11:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: streaming capacity and peak-slice sizing follow-up

Alex — got the logs, thank you. Rafael and I reviewed the sample and sketched a conservative sizing. A few provisional observations before we finalize the PDF (attached as redwood-sizing-proposal-v1.pdf in the Drive link):

Observations from the traces (quick summary):
- Median tokens/request: ~340; 95th tokens/request: ~980.
- Peak crest pattern matches an all-region fan-out in the US; effective simultaneous request count during crest ~48k (back-of-envelope).
- Streaming token-rate spikes are short (<2 minutes) but frequent during sync windows.

Preliminary sizing (conservative pilot):
- Pilot cluster: 6 x A100-style GPU nodes (4-model-shards layout), each node runs 3 streaming slots + 8 batch slots.
- Slot projection: supports ~120 concurrent streaming connections per region with graceful degradation; can absorb the morning crest with an autoscaling trigger to add 2 nodes within ~90s.
- Expected steady-state cost: model serving cost estimated at ~$4.8k/day (pilot config) — we will tighten this after we iterate on quantization/packing choices.

Open questions for you to confirm:
1) Do we want the autoscaler to prefer adding nodes (horizontal) or expanding streaming slots per node (vertical) during a crest? We recommend horizontal for predictability.
2) Can you confirm whether the morning sync is shiftable (i.e., could it be staggered) — even 5–10% fan-out smoothing dramatically changes cost.

Planned next steps:
- Rafael to finalize the sizing doc and produce a lower-cost “optimized” config (pack/quantize) for comparison by Thu 7/3.
- Lukas / Laura to prepare a 6-week pilot mutual action plan and a doc link for telemetry onboarding.

I'll attach the sizing PDF to the Drive folder and flag action items in the HubSpot deal. If you prefer, we can schedule a 30-minute tech deep-dive with Rafael this week.

Cheers,
Lukas
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwood.com>
To: Lukas Meyer <lukas@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jul 2025 09:31:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: sizing proposal v1 notes (do not externalize)

Internal note — quick confirmation on the size math before I lock the PDF: 
- Using the supplied logs, conservative concurrency peak estimate: ~52k simultaneous token-emitting requests across region. With KV cache and prefix reuse assumptions (30% reuse) we can reduce token compute ~22%.
- Baseline throughput targets to promise in pilot (customer-facing): 95th latency <= 300ms for premium traffic up to 1.2k steady QPS, and burst handling to 420 QPS with degraded routing/backpressure for standard users.
- Suggest we include an explicit fallback policy: if shed rate > 15% for > 45s, automatically route standard users to a lower-cost quantized variant (latency target 450–500ms).

Action items for PDF v1 -> v2:
- Add explicit test plan: synthetic crest simulation (420 QPS for 2 minutes) + streaming concurrency test (120 concurrent streams for 3 minutes).
- Add recommended tracing hooks and a small telemetry template to onboard their logs (column names + timezone guidance).

I will finalize and drop into the Drive folder by EOD today.

— Rafael Mendes
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

1) Send a 24–48 hour telemetry extract (API logs) with per-request token counts and timestamps — even a CSV is fine. We referenced an example format in the Drive link.
2) Confirm whether the morning crest is an all-users fan-out (global) or limited to a few tenants — this changes routing/fallback policies.
3) Clarify whether you require strict 95th P95 latency enforcement across all traffic or only for logged-in premium users.

Next steps I will take:
- Rafael will produce a quick pilot sizing (GPU count, shard layout, streaming slot config) and a conservative cost estimate.
- I'll draft an SLA-aligned proposal and a mutual action plan for a 6-week pilot with milestones.

Attachments: I flagged where a traces export would be helpful.

Thanks — I can jump on a short follow-up call if helpful.

— Lukas Meyer
Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
l: +1 415 555 0120
Redwood Inference


From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@sh-health.com>
To: Lukas Meyer <lukas@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Ortega <maria.ortega@sh-health.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: streaming capacity and peak-slice sizing follow-up

Lukas — thanks for the recap and the clear asks. A few clarifications and I attached a small telemetry bundle (sageware-p95-traces-20250630.zip) with 36 hours of anonymized request logs.

Quick answers: 
- The morning crest (420 QPS) is driven by a single scheduled sync across ~120k active users — it fans out across our US region only.
- Premium users (~8% of requests) should have the tighter 95th <= 300ms requirement; standard users are ok with the softer 500ms target.
- Streaming: the 12 concurrent streams you saw are per logical shard for the service — we currently run 3 logical shards per region during steady state. During the crest we see all shards hit ~10–12 active streams each.

Notes on the attached logs: time column is UTC; token_count is approximate (we calculate client-side). Let me know if you need a different export.

— Alex
Alex Turner
Head of Platform Engineering, SageWare Health
alex.turner@sh-health.com
[attachment: sageware-p95-traces-20250630.zip]


From: Lukas Meyer <lukas@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@sh-health.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 11:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: streaming capacity and peak-slice sizing follow-up

Alex — got the logs, thank you. Rafael and I reviewed the sample and sketched a conservative sizing. A few provisional observations before we finalize the PDF (attached as redwood-sizing-proposal-v1.pdf in the Drive link):

Observations from the traces (quick summary):
- Median tokens/request: ~340; 95th tokens/request: ~980.
- Peak crest pattern matches an all-region fan-out in the US; effective simultaneous request count during crest ~48k (back-of-envelope).
- Streaming token-rate spikes are short (<2 minutes) but frequent during sync windows.

Preliminary sizing (conservative pilot):
- Pilot cluster: 6 x A100-style GPU nodes (4-model-shards layout), each node runs 3 streaming slots + 8 batch slots.
- Slot projection: supports ~120 concurrent streaming connections per region with graceful degradation; can absorb the morning crest with an autoscaling trigger to add 2 nodes within ~90s.
- Expected steady-state cost: model serving cost estimated at ~$4.8k/day (pilot config) — we will tighten this after we iterate on quantization/packing choices.

Open questions for you to confirm:
1) Do we want the autoscaler to prefer adding nodes (horizontal) or expanding streaming slots per node (vertical) during a crest? We recommend horizontal for predictability.
2) Can you confirm whether the morning sync is shiftable (i.e., could it be staggered) — even 5–10% fan-out smoothing dramatically changes cost.

Planned next steps:
- Rafael to finalize the sizing doc and produce a lower-cost “optimized” config (pack/quantize) for comparison by Thu 7/3.
- Lukas / Laura to prepare a 6-week pilot mutual action plan and a doc link for telemetry onboarding.

I'll attach the sizing PDF to the Drive folder and flag action items in the HubSpot deal. If you prefer, we can schedule a 30-minute tech deep-dive with Rafael this week.

Cheers,
Lukas


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwood.com>
To: Lukas Meyer <lukas@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jul 2025 09:31:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: sizing proposal v1 notes (do not externalize)

Internal note — quick confirmation on the size math before I lock the PDF: 
- Using the supplied logs, conservative concurrency peak estimate: ~52k simultaneous token-emitting requests across region. With KV cache and prefix reuse assumptions (30% reuse) we can reduce token compute ~22%.
- Baseline throughput targets to promise in pilot (customer-facing): 95th latency <= 300ms for premium traffic up to 1.2k steady QPS, and burst handling to 420 QPS with degraded routing/backpressure for standard users.
- Suggest we include an explicit fallback policy: if shed rate > 15% for > 45s, automatically route standard users to a lower-cost quantized variant (latency target 450–500ms).

Action items for PDF v1 -> v2:
- Add explicit test plan: synthetic crest simulation (420 QPS for 2 minutes) + streaming concurrency test (120 concurrent streams for 3 minutes).
- Add recommended tracing hooks and a small telemetry template to onboard their logs (column names + timezone guidance).

I will finalize and drop into the Drive folder by EOD today.

— Rafael Mendes
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Prospect Q&A: execution locations, subprocessor contract chain & erase commitments
messages:
From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@havenhealth.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
Cc: Haven Health Security <security@havenhealth.com>
Date: 2025-06-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick Qs on where data is processed and deletion guarantees

Hi Olga —

Thanks for the demo today. Before we move this through procurement, our security team had a few concrete questions about runtime geography and subprocessor chain that I hope you can answer or point us to evidence for. Short list below; feel free to answer inline or attach docs.

1) For the Hosted API, what regions might inference execution land in when we do not pin a region? Do you ever route requests across borders for availability?
2) If we require US-only execution, what are our supported options (Hosted pin, Dedicated, Private)? What is the fastest path to a US-only guarantee?
3) Can you share the current contracted subprocessor chain (we need vendor names + country of operation) and the contract layer that governs them?
4) Deletion behavior: when we request data deletion for a customer payload, what caches/logs are purged, and what are your timelines for tombstone vs full erase?
5) Key management: does Hosted use Redwood-managed KMS by default? Is BYOK available for Dedicated/Private?

We plan to include these in our vendor risk assessment. Appreciate concise answers and any attachments we can include in the risk file.

Thanks,
Amit Shah
Head of Security - Haven Health
amit.shah@havenhealth.com
+1 415-555-0189

Attachments: none
From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@havenhealth.com>
Cc: Haven Health Security <security@havenhealth.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-18T11:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick Qs on where data is processed and deletion guarantees

Amit —

Thanks for the clear list. I summarized answers below and attached an evidence packet (Residency_Roadmap.pdf, Subprocessor_List_2025.csv, SOC2_Report_redacted.pdf). Rafael and Marissa are copied for any technical follow-ups.

1) Hosted API routing: by default we route requests to the nearest healthy serving pool based on region and latency. Active regions in production: us-east-1 (Virginia), us-west-2 (Oregon), eu-west-1 (Ireland), asia-southeast-1 (Singapore). If you do not pin a region, requests may be routed to any of these based on routing policy (latency/capacity). We do not intentionally route across borders for customers who have specified regional restrictions — however, unpinned traffic is latency-optimized and thus may leave the US for some EU/Asia customers.

2) US-only guarantee: the fastest path is to pin your account to a US region on the Hosted plan (we can pin to us-east-1 or us-west-2). For stricter contractual guarantees and isolated execution, Dedicated (reserved GPU pool in a US region) is the recommended enterprise option — Dedicated provides predictable capacity and region isolation. Private offers the highest assurance (VPC deployment or on-prem bundle) where you control the network and KMS. We can include a clause in the contract to prevent cross-border failover for Hosted-pinned accounts if agreed in advance.

3) Subprocessors & contract chain: attached CSV lists our primary subprocessors (compute providers, object storage, monitoring). High level: compute partners (regional GPU hosts) — US/EU/APAC, ephemeral cache providers — US/EU, observability vendor — US-based, backups replicated to our encrypted cold store (US/EU). All subprocessors perform under our DPA and flow-down clauses (we attach standard flow-down language in the Subprocessor_List file). If you need redlines, Rafael can coordinate SOW/DPA changes.

4) Deletion behavior: operational summary: when a deletion request is received we remove pointers/tombstones from hot storage within 24 hours and start purge of KV/prefix caches (default TTLs: 12 hours for transient caches). For Hosted accounts: hot data tombstoned within 24 hours, underlying ephemeral caches purged within 72 hours, and backups may be eligible for schedule-based purge within up to 90 days (we track and report this). For Dedicated/Private, we can commit to reduced backup windows in contract (e.g., 30-day backup purge) since we control retention policies. We provide an erase report upon request.

5) Key management: Hosted uses Redwood-managed KMS by default (keys in-region). BYOK is available for Dedicated and Private (customer-managed KMS via ACM/KMS integration or HSM in Private). For high-sensitivity use, Private lets you host the key material behind your KMS; Dedicated supports bring-your-own-key for enterprise plans.

Next steps: attached is the Residency_Roadmap (calls out exact regions per product), the Subprocessor_List for vendor names and countries, and a redacted SOC2 for controls. If you want a >30 minute security deep-dive we can schedule Rafael + Marissa to walk your team through the architecture and provide contract language.

If you want me to produce a concise one-page statement of US-only commitments for procurement, tell me which product (Hosted pin / Dedicated / Private) you prefer and I will draft it.

Best,

--
Olga Petrov
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.com
+1 617-555-0123

Attachments: Residency_Roadmap.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB); Subprocessor_List_2025.csv (CSV); SOC2_Report_redacted.pdf (PDF)
From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@havenhealth.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
Cc: Haven Health Security <security@havenhealth.com>
Date: 2025-06-19T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick Qs on where data is processed and deletion guarantees

Olga —

Thanks for the quick turn. A couple of follow-ups after reviewing the attachments:

A) For Hosted pinned to us-east-1: is there any scenario where traffic could still egress to eu-west-1 for model weights or routing (for example, during failover or for model shard fetching)? We need to confirm whether model artifact fetches or scheduled replication could cause cross-border transfer.

B) For backups: you mentioned up to 90 days for Hosted. Can you confirm if any backups are stored outside the US for Hosted accounts pinned to a US region? We need explicit country-level statements for our vendor risk file.

C) For subprocessors: the CSV lists a monitoring/observability vendor with EU presence. Is telemetry that contains customer request identifiers ever processed in EU locations? We need assurance that PII/identifiers originating in the US won’t be sent to EU analytics clusters.

If possible, could you provide a one-paragraph statement we can include in our risk assessment that states the residency commitments for a Hosted-pinned-us-east account (short bullet points)?

Appreciate it — we are near the procurement sign-off but want clear language.

—Amit
From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@havenhealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Haven Health Security <security@havenhealth.com>
Date: 2025-06-20T15:32:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick Qs on where data is processed and deletion guarantees

Amit —

Short answers suitable for your risk file below, and then a few clarifying bullets. Happy to convert to a stamped procurement addendum if you need contract language.

One-paragraph residency statement (Hosted account pinned to us-east-1):
- Redwood routes inference requests to serving pools in the specified region (us-east-1) when an account is pinned; model artifacts and ephemeral KV caches are pulled from our replicated regional artifact stores and do not transit outside the pinned region under normal operation. In rare emergency failover scenarios, and only with prior contractual allowance, we may route to an alternate US region (us-west-2) — we do not route pinned us-east-1 traffic to EU/APAC regions. Backups for Hosted-pinned-us-east accounts are maintained within our US encrypted cold store; backup retention and geo-replication behavior is governed by the product tier and contract.

Clarifying bullets:
- Model artifact fetches: artifacts are regionally replicated; we do not fetch model shards from EU hosts for a us-east pinned account.
- Failover: emergency failover routing is constrained to US regions only for accounts with a US-only pin; cross-border failover requires explicit contractual permission.
- Backups: for Hosted-pinned-us-east we keep backups within US data centers by default; any cross-border backup replication is opt-in and documented in the DPA.
- Telemetry/observability: request metadata that may contain identifiers is sanitized for analytics pipelines; telemetry that contains full request identifiers is only routed to processing clusters within the account’s region unless the customer opts into centralized analytics. We can provide a telemetry-processing attestation signed by our security team.
- Subprocessor chain & contracts: all listed subprocessors operate under our DPA with flow-down clauses; where a subprocessor has multinational presence we can provide country-of-operation for the nodes handling your account on request.

Operational commitments & next steps:
- If Haven needs a written US-only assurance for procurement we will produce a one-page addendum for the SOW/DPA stating: region pining, backup locality, failover constraints, KMS model. Rafael can scope any necessary contract redlines; Marissa can run the technical attestation call.

Would you like Rafael and I to join a 30–45 minute call with your security reviewer to finalize language? If so propose times and we’ll get it on the calendar.

Best regards,

--
Olga Petrov
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.com
+1 617-555-0123

Attachments referenced earlier: Residency_Roadmap.pdf; Subprocessor_List_2025.csv; SOC2_Report_redacted.pdf

Attachments: Residency_Roadmap.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB); Subprocessor_List_2025.csv (CSV); SOC2_Report_redacted.pdf (PDF)

From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@havenhealth.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
Cc: Haven Health Security <security@havenhealth.com>
Date: 2025-06-19T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick Qs on where data is processed and deletion guarantees

Olga —

Thanks for the quick turn. A couple of follow-ups after reviewing the attachments:

A) For Hosted pinned to us-east-1: is there any scenario where traffic could still egress to eu-west-1 for model weights or routing (for example, during failover or for model shard fetching)? We need to confirm whether model artifact fetches or scheduled replication could cause cross-border transfer.

B) For backups: you mentioned up to 90 days for Hosted. Can you confirm if any backups are stored outside the US for Hosted accounts pinned to a US region? We need explicit country-level statements for our vendor risk file.

C) For subprocessors: the CSV lists a monitoring/observability vendor with EU presence. Is telemetry that contains customer request identifiers ever processed in EU locations? We need assurance that PII/identifiers originating in the US won’t be sent to EU analytics clusters.

If possible, could you provide a one-paragraph statement we can include in our risk assessment that states the residency commitments for a Hosted-pinned-us-east account (short bullet points)?

Appreciate it — we are near the procurement sign-off but want clear language.

—Amit


From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@havenhealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Haven Health Security <security@havenhealth.com>
Date: 2025-06-20T15:32:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick Qs on where data is processed and deletion guarantees

Amit —

Short answers suitable for your risk file below, and then a few clarifying bullets. Happy to convert to a stamped procurement addendum if you need contract language.

One-paragraph residency statement (Hosted account pinned to us-east-1):
- Redwood routes inference requests to serving pools in the specified region (us-east-1) when an account is pinned; model artifacts and ephemeral KV caches are pulled from our replicated regional artifact stores and do not transit outside the pinned region under normal operation. In rare emergency failover scenarios, and only with prior contractual allowance, we may route to an alternate US region (us-west-2) — we do not route pinned us-east-1 traffic to EU/APAC regions. Backups for Hosted-pinned-us-east accounts are maintained within our US encrypted cold store; backup retention and geo-replication behavior is governed by the product tier and contract.

Clarifying bullets:
- Model artifact fetches: artifacts are regionally replicated; we do not fetch model shards from EU hosts for a us-east pinned account.
- Failover: emergency failover routing is constrained to US regions only for accounts with a US-only pin; cross-border failover requires explicit contractual permission.
- Backups: for Hosted-pinned-us-east we keep backups within US data centers by default; any cross-border backup replication is opt-in and documented in the DPA.
- Telemetry/observability: request metadata that may contain identifiers is sanitized for analytics pipelines; telemetry that contains full request identifiers is only routed to processing clusters within the account’s region unless the customer opts into centralized analytics. We can provide a telemetry-processing attestation signed by our security team.
- Subprocessor chain & contracts: all listed subprocessors operate under our DPA with flow-down clauses; where a subprocessor has multinational presence we can provide country-of-operation for the nodes handling your account on request.

Operational commitments & next steps:
- If Haven needs a written US-only assurance for procurement we will produce a one-page addendum for the SOW/DPA stating: region pining, backup locality, failover constraints, KMS model. Rafael can scope any necessary contract redlines; Marissa can run the technical attestation call.

Would you like Rafael and I to join a 30–45 minute call with your security reviewer to finalize language? If so propose times and we’ll get it on the calendar.

Best regards,

--
Olga Petrov
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.com
+1 617-555-0123

Attachments referenced earlier: Residency_Roadmap.pdf; Subprocessor_List_2025.csv; SOC2_Report_redacted.pdf

source.subject: Escalation: Rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage for Redwood Private (Cascade Financial Group)
source.body:
From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa.chen@cascadefg.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Becker <tom.becker@cascadefg.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Redwood Private)

Markus,

We’re preparing our next Redwood Private upgrade in our VPC environment and need written clarity on two items before we’ll schedule the maintenance window:

1) Rollback guarantees: if the upgrade fails (including DB migration issues), can we roll back to the prior version and what is the expected time to restore service? We need something more concrete than “best effort.”

2) Support coverage: we will be doing this in our standard maintenance window (Saturday 1:00–4:00am ET). We need to know if Redwood will have engineering/SRE coverage during the window, and what escalation path exists if rollback is required.

We’ve had issues with other vendors where “rollback” meant “rebuild the cluster,” which is not acceptable for us.

Please respond by EOD Thursday so we can confirm the window with our change advisory board.

Thanks,
Alyssa

—
Alyssa Chen
Director, Platform Operations
Cascade Financial Group



From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 10:01 AM
Subject: Fwd: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Cascade)

Fwd’ing escalation from Cascade. They’re asking for (a) rollback “guarantees” incl. DB migration failure case and (b) engineering/SRE coverage for Sat 1–4am ET window.

Can you please send:
- What we can state publicly re: rollback semantics (current GA vs what’s in-flight), and any hard constraints (N-1 only, schema migration caveats).
- What support coverage we can commit to for this specific window (named SRE? on-call bridge? limitations).

They’re asking for response by Thu EOD.

Markus

—
Markus Klein
Solutions Engineering
Redwood Inference



From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 10:47 AM
Subject: Re: Fwd: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Cascade)

For support coverage:

- We can offer “upgrade window coverage” as a planned event: open an internal incident bridge at start of window, have SRE on-call (Rafael) + one release eng point (Connor or Devon) on standby.
- For a Sat 1–4am ET window, we can commit to: ack within 15 minutes on the bridge + continuous coverage until upgrade completes or rollback is confirmed.
- If they want this as a contractual commitment, it needs to be captured as a paid premium support addendum / named escalation path, but for this upcoming window we can do it as a one-off (assuming 48h notice and precheck outputs shared beforehand).

I’d avoid using the word “guarantee” on RTO/RPO. We can give typical timings and what determines rollback time (snapshot restore vs logical restore).

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference



From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 11:26 AM
Subject: Re: Fwd: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Cascade)

On rollback semantics (what we can say today):

- Supported upgrade path is N-1 → N for Redwood Private. We do not currently support skipping major versions without a review.
- Rollback is supported to the previously running version provided:
  (a) the upgrade hasn’t crossed a non-reversible schema boundary, and
  (b) the recommended backups/snapshots were taken before applying migrations.
- Our current guidance: take a pre-upgrade backup of the control-plane DB + config state; if on EBS/CSI, take a volume snapshot; if managed DB, take a point-in-time snapshot.
- For migration failures: we run migrations with guardrails (lock timeouts, idempotent steps), but in the event of a failure we can roll back application components immediately and then restore DB to the pre-upgrade snapshot.

What I’d like to avoid: “guaranteeing” a fixed rollback time without knowing their storage/backend. We can give a typical range and define prerequisites (snapshot ready, prechecks clean, artifacts available).

If helpful, we can offer a rollback drill in staging prior to prod.

Vivek

—
Vivek Kulkarni
Product, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference



From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 12:05 PM
Subject: Re: Fwd: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Cascade)

Two notes for the written response:

1) Please avoid hard “guarantees” unless it’s in their contract. Use “supported rollback procedure” and “expected restoration time is typically X–Y given prerequisites.”

2) Since they’re regulated, mention auditability: we can provide an upgrade report (precheck results, timestamps for backup start/complete, and change summary). If they require SIEM evidence, we can provide audit log entries for upgrade start/stop + rollback invocation in our newer Private releases (verify what version they are on / targeting).

Naomi

—
Naomi Feldman
Security/Compliance Product Reviewer
Redwood Inference



From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 4, 2025 at 9:18 AM
Subject: Draft response to Cascade (rollback + support coverage)

All — draft below. Please reply with edits/approvals. If no objections by 2pm PT I’ll send.

---
Alyssa,

Thanks — understood on the need for operational clarity.

Rollback (supported procedure)
- For Redwood Private, we support in-place upgrades on the supported path (N-1 → N). As part of the upgrade workflow, Redwood requires a pre-upgrade backup/snapshot of the control-plane database and configuration state.
- If an upgrade fails (including during database migrations), the supported remediation is:
  1) stop/roll back the application components to the prior running version, and
  2) restore the control-plane database from the pre-upgrade snapshot (if migrations were applied and need to be reverted).
- In environments with pre-upgrade snapshots in place, service restoration is typically on the order of tens of minutes. Exact timing depends on the snapshot mechanism (managed DB PITR vs volume snapshot) and the size of the database.

Upgrade window support coverage
- We can staff your planned maintenance window (Sat 1:00–4:00am ET) with Redwood support + SRE escalation.
- Proposed plan: you share precheck outputs and the target version 48 hours prior; we open a live bridge at the start of the window; Redwood SRE/release escalation is available throughout until completion or rollback confirmation.

Auditability
- We can provide an upgrade report containing precheck results and timestamps for backup/upgrade steps. If required, we can also provide audit log entries that reflect upgrade start/stop and rollback invocation (version dependent).

If you’d like, we can also run a rollback drill in a non-production environment prior to the production change window.

---

Markus



From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alyssa Chen <alyssa.chen@cascadefg.com>
Cc: Tom Becker <tom.becker@cascadefg.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Bcc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 4, 2025 at 4:38 PM
Subject: Re: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Redwood Private)
Attachments: Redwood-Private-Upgrade-Rollback-Guidance-DRAFT-2025-06-04.pdf (application/pdf)

Alyssa,

Thanks — understood on the need for operational clarity before you take this to CAB.

Rollback (supported procedure)
- For Redwood Private, we support in-place upgrades on the supported path (N-1 → N). As part of the upgrade workflow, we require a pre-upgrade backup/snapshot of the control-plane database and configuration state.
- If an upgrade fails (including during database migrations), the supported remediation is:
  1) roll back the application components to the prior running version, and
  2) restore the control-plane database from the pre-upgrade snapshot if migrations were applied and need to be reverted.
- With pre-upgrade snapshots in place, service restoration is typically on the order of tens of minutes. Exact timing depends on your snapshot mechanism (managed DB PITR vs volume snapshot) and the size of the database.

Upgrade window support coverage
- We can staff your planned maintenance window (Sat 1:00–4:00am ET) with Redwood support and SRE escalation.
- To do so safely, we ask that you share the target version and precheck outputs at least 48 hours prior. We’ll open a live bridge at the start of the window and maintain coverage until completion or rollback is confirmed.

Auditability
- We can provide an upgrade report containing precheck results and timestamps for backup/upgrade steps. Where enabled for the version you’re running/targeting, we can also provide audit log entries reflecting upgrade start/stop and rollback invocation.

I’ve attached a short draft guidance PDF you can use for your internal change record; we can tailor it to your exact DB/snapshot approach if you confirm which backend you’re using.

If helpful, we can also run a rollback drill in a non-production environment prior to the production window.

Can you confirm:
1) current Private version and target version,
2) database backend (RDS/Postgres in-cluster/etc.), and
3) whether the window is firm at Sat 1:00am ET?

Markus

—
Markus Klein
Solutions Engineering
Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwoodinference.com

Fwd’ing escalation from Cascade. They’re asking for (a) rollback “guarantees” incl. DB migration failure case and (b) engineering/SRE coverage for Sat 1–4am ET window.

Can you please send:
- What we can state publicly re: rollback semantics (current GA vs what’s in-flight), and any hard constraints (N-1 only, schema migration caveats).
- What support coverage we can commit to for this specific window (named SRE? on-call bridge? limitations).

They’re asking for response by Thu EOD.

Markus

—
Markus Klein
Solutions Engineering
Redwood Inference



From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 10:47 AM
Subject: Re: Fwd: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Cascade)

For support coverage:

- We can offer “upgrade window coverage” as a planned event: open an internal incident bridge at start of window, have SRE on-call (Rafael) + one release eng point (Connor or Devon) on standby.
- For a Sat 1–4am ET window, we can commit to: ack within 15 minutes on the bridge + continuous coverage until upgrade completes or rollback is confirmed.
- If they want this as a contractual commitment, it needs to be captured as a paid premium support addendum / named escalation path, but for this upcoming window we can do it as a one-off (assuming 48h notice and precheck outputs shared beforehand).

I’d avoid using the word “guarantee” on RTO/RPO. We can give typical timings and what determines rollback time (snapshot restore vs logical restore).

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference



From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 11:26 AM
Subject: Re: Fwd: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Cascade)

On rollback semantics (what we can say today):

- Supported upgrade path is N-1 → N for Redwood Private. We do not currently support skipping major versions without a review.
- Rollback is supported to the previously running version provided:
  (a) the upgrade hasn’t crossed a non-reversible schema boundary, and
  (b) the recommended backups/snapshots were taken before applying migrations.
- Our current guidance: take a pre-upgrade backup of the control-plane DB + config state; if on EBS/CSI, take a volume snapshot; if managed DB, take a point-in-time snapshot.
- For migration failures: we run migrations with guardrails (lock timeouts, idempotent steps), but in the event of a failure we can roll back application components immediately and then restore DB to the pre-upgrade snapshot.

What I’d like to avoid: “guaranteeing” a fixed rollback time without knowing their storage/backend. We can give a typical range and define prerequisites (snapshot ready, prechecks clean, artifacts available).

If helpful, we can offer a rollback drill in staging prior to prod.

Vivek

—
Vivek Kulkarni
Product, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference



From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 12:05 PM
Subject: Re: Fwd: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Cascade)

Two notes for the written response:

1) Please avoid hard “guarantees” unless it’s in their contract. Use “supported rollback procedure” and “expected restoration time is typically X–Y given prerequisites.”

2) Since they’re regulated, mention auditability: we can provide an upgrade report (precheck results, timestamps for backup start/complete, and change summary). If they require SIEM evidence, we can provide audit log entries for upgrade start/stop + rollback invocation in our newer Private releases (verify what version they are on / targeting).

Naomi

—
Naomi Feldman
Security/Compliance Product Reviewer
Redwood Inference



From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 4, 2025 at 9:18 AM
Subject: Draft response to Cascade (rollback + support coverage)

All — draft below. Please reply with edits/approvals. If no objections by 2pm PT I’ll send.

---
Alyssa,

Thanks — understood on the need for operational clarity.

Rollback (supported procedure)
- For Redwood Private, we support in-place upgrades on the supported path (N-1 → N). As part of the upgrade workflow, Redwood requires a pre-upgrade backup/snapshot of the control-plane database and configuration state.
- If an upgrade fails (including during database migrations), the supported remediation is:
  1) stop/roll back the application components to the prior running version, and
  2) restore the control-plane database from the pre-upgrade snapshot (if migrations were applied and need to be reverted).
- In environments with pre-upgrade snapshots in place, service restoration is typically on the order of tens of minutes. Exact timing depends on the snapshot mechanism (managed DB PITR vs volume snapshot) and the size of the database.

Upgrade window support coverage
- We can staff your planned maintenance window (Sat 1:00–4:00am ET) with Redwood support + SRE escalation.
- Proposed plan: you share precheck outputs and the target version 48 hours prior; we open a live bridge at the start of the window; Redwood SRE/release escalation is available throughout until completion or rollback confirmation.

Auditability
- We can provide an upgrade report containing precheck results and timestamps for backup/upgrade steps. If required, we can also provide audit log entries that reflect upgrade start/stop and rollback invocation (version dependent).

If you’d like, we can also run a rollback drill in a non-production environment prior to the production change window.

---

Markus



From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alyssa Chen <alyssa.chen@cascadefg.com>
Cc: Tom Becker <tom.becker@cascadefg.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Bcc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 4, 2025 at 4:38 PM
Subject: Re: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Redwood Private)
Attachments: Redwood-Private-Upgrade-Rollback-Guidance-DRAFT-2025-06-04.pdf (application/pdf)

Alyssa,

Thanks — understood on the need for operational clarity before you take this to CAB.

Rollback (supported procedure)
- For Redwood Private, we support in-place upgrades on the supported path (N-1 → N). As part of the upgrade workflow, we require a pre-upgrade backup/snapshot of the control-plane database and configuration state.
- If an upgrade fails (including during database migrations), the supported remediation is:
  1) roll back the application components to the prior running version, and
  2) restore the control-plane database from the pre-upgrade snapshot if migrations were applied and need to be reverted.
- With pre-upgrade snapshots in place, service restoration is typically on the order of tens of minutes. Exact timing depends on your snapshot mechanism (managed DB PITR vs volume snapshot) and the size of the database.

Upgrade window support coverage
- We can staff your planned maintenance window (Sat 1:00–4:00am ET) with Redwood support and SRE escalation.
- To do so safely, we ask that you share the target version and precheck outputs at least 48 hours prior. We’ll open a live bridge at the start of the window and maintain coverage until completion or rollback is confirmed.

Auditability
- We can provide an upgrade report containing precheck results and timestamps for backup/upgrade steps. Where enabled for the version you’re running/targeting, we can also provide audit log entries reflecting upgrade start/stop and rollback invocation.

I’ve attached a short draft guidance PDF you can use for your internal change record; we can tailor it to your exact DB/snapshot approach if you confirm which backend you’re using.

If helpful, we can also run a rollback drill in a non-production environment prior to the production window.

Can you confirm:
1) current Private version and target version,
2) database backend (RDS/Postgres in-cluster/etc.), and
3) whether the window is firm at Sat 1:00am ET?

Markus

—
Markus Klein
Solutions Engineering
Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwoodinference.com

source.subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access
source.body:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Maya Ellis <maya.ellis@quantaledger.example>
Cc: QuantaLedger Legal <legal@quantaledger.example>
Date: Sun, Jun 1, 2025 at 8:22 AM
Subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access

Maya  confirming we can keep serving/storage in the US region for the Private deployment.

On your question about the EU-based rollout subcontractor: we can approve a *time-bounded exception* that allows that individual to access deployment logs and operational metadata during rollout. This does *not* include access to prompts/responses or any payload-level content. Access will be least-privilege and fully audited.

If you confirm the identity + SSO details, well document the exception and proceed.

Logan


From: Maya Ellis <maya.ellis@quantaledger.example>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: QuantaLedger Legal <legal@quantaledger.example>
Date: Mon, Jun 2, 2025 at 9:10 AM
Subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access

Thanks. Please clarify in writing: scope (logs/metadata only), duration, and that the contractor cannot access prompts/responses.


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: QuantaLedger Legal <legal@quantaledger.example>
Cc: Maya Ellis <maya.ellis@quantaledger.example>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 4, 2025 at 11:52 AM
Subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access

Confirmed. The exception covers out-of-region human access to operational metadata only (deployment logs, request ids/timestamps, admin audit logs) for 2025-06-05 through 2025-07-31. Prompts/responses are excluded. Access is via least-privilege role mapping and audited.

Aisha Rahman
Head of Security, Redwood Inference


From: Maya Ellis <maya.ellis@quantaledger.example>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 4, 2025 at 12:10 PM
Subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access

Accepted. Please proceed with the RBAC setup and send us the final exception record reference.

source.subject: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation
messages:
From: Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Hi Rafael,

Thanks for taking this on. As part of our SIG/SOC2 intake I'm collecting artifact examples related to access control and short-lived credentials. Can you provide: 

- An export showing SCIM delta events for the last 30 days (provision/deprovision).
- At least one SAML assertion sample (redacted) for federated SSO that includes group/role claims.
- Evidence of service-account rotation and the most recent rotation runbook.
- A short note describing how ephemeral session tokens are issued/tracked (where logs live).

We need these artifacts for the external assessor by EOD 22 May. If anything is sensitive, point me to the secure drive link and the assessor will request access.

Regards,
Marta Velasquez
Security Lead, HealthDocs
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 May 2025 09:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Hi Marta — thanks for the list. Looping in Soojin (IAM eng) and Irene (security ops) to pull the SCIM export and provide the SAML assertion sample. Jordan will share the rotation runbook + recent rotation audit.

Soojin/Irene/Jordan — ETA for the artifacts? We should place anything sensitive in the secure Drive folder Marta listed.

I'll be available for a quick sync if the assessor wants to walk through the artifacts.

- Rafael
Director, Security Programs
Redwood Inference
From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 May 2025 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Marta / Rafael — attaching a redacted SCIM delta export (last 30 days) and a redacted SAML assertion sample. Attachment stubs below: 

Attachments: scim_delta_sample_202505-export.csv (CSV, 85 rows, user/provisioning events), saml_assertion_sample.txt (redacted assertion, removed PII)

Notes: 
- The SCIM export includes create/update/delete events keyed by user id with timestamps and the originating provisioning source (Okta -> SCIM). We scrubbed email addresses and replaced them with internal ids per our redaction policy.
- SAML assertions include group membership under the 'roles' attribute; team-to-role mapping is documented in the runbook Jordan will attach.

If the assessor needs full email addresses for a sampling exercise we can provide them under an NDA and via the secure folder.

— Soojin
IAM Engineer
From: OSS Assessments <auditor@oss-assessments.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
Date: Wed, 21 May 2025 11:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Thanks — received the SCIM and SAML samples. Two follow-ups: 

1) For rotation evidence, can you provide the last two rotation job logs for the service-account fleet and a note on the automated enforcement (cron/CI job id + owner)?
2) For ephemeral sessions: we need an example log that ties a short-lived token issuance to a user session and the corresponding audit entry that records token revocation/expiry. Prefer a single request/response trace.

If those logs are sensitive, point us to the minimal redacted extracts.

Best,
OSS Assessments (on behalf of HealthDocs)
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: OSS Assessments <auditor@oss-assessments.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 May 2025 14:50:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Hi all — attaching the rotation policy and two redacted rotation job logs. Attachment stubs: service-account-rotation-policy-v1.2.pdf, rotation-job-20250428.log, rotation-job-20250512.log.

Summary: 
- Rotation is enforced monthly via an internal rotation pipeline (CI job id: infra-rotate-svc-01, owner: infra-runbook@redwood.com). 
- The job records include key identifiers and a hash of the rotated credential; we keep the raw secret in the KMS only.
- Ephemeral tokens are issued by the auth-service (auth.redwood.internal) and recorded in the session-audit stream (Elastic indices: sessions-YYYY.MM). A representative session issuance + expiry pair is in the session-audit export link (Drive).

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-HealthDocs-evidence (access by permission only).

If you want a 30-min walkthrough I can host a screenshare and show the job output (redacted) and the session trace.

— Jordan
Principal Infra Engineer
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>, OSS Assessments <auditor@oss-assessments.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 May 2025 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Marta / team — all requested artifacts have been uploaded to the shared Drive folder. Summary of next steps: 

- Jordan to run a 30-min walkthrough on Fri 23 May at 10:00 PT (invitation sent).
- Soojin will prepare a tight redaction pack if the assessor needs PII-preserving samples.
- Irene will provide a short control narrative (1 page) describing log retention, access controls to the session indices, and how we map SCIM deltas to identity owners.

If anything else is required before your assessment kickoff, please flag and we'll prioritize.

Regards,
Rafael Mendes
Director, Security Programs
Redwood Inference

Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 May 2025 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Marta / team — all requested artifacts have been uploaded to the shared Drive folder. Summary of next steps: 

- Jordan to run a 30-min walkthrough on Fri 23 May at 10:00 PT (invitation sent).
- Soojin will prepare a tight redaction pack if the assessor needs PII-preserving samples.
- Irene will provide a short control narrative (1 page) describing log retention, access controls to the session indices, and how we map SCIM deltas to identity owners.

If anything else is required before your assessment kickoff, please flag and we'll prioritize.

Regards,
Rafael Mendes
Director, Security Programs
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Hi Rafael,

Thanks for taking this on. As part of our SIG/SOC2 intake I'm collecting artifact examples related to access control and short-lived credentials. Can you provide: 

- An export showing SCIM delta events for the last 30 days (provision/deprovision).
- At least one SAML assertion sample (redacted) for federated SSO that includes group/role claims.
- Evidence of service-account rotation and the most recent rotation runbook.
- A short note describing how ephemeral session tokens are issued/tracked (where logs live).

We need these artifacts for the external assessor by EOD 22 May. If anything is sensitive, point me to the secure drive link and the assessor will request access.

Regards,
Marta Velasquez
Security Lead, HealthDocs


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 May 2025 09:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Hi Marta — thanks for the list. Looping in Soojin (IAM eng) and Irene (security ops) to pull the SCIM export and provide the SAML assertion sample. Jordan will share the rotation runbook + recent rotation audit.

Soojin/Irene/Jordan — ETA for the artifacts? We should place anything sensitive in the secure Drive folder Marta listed.

I'll be available for a quick sync if the assessor wants to walk through the artifacts.

- Rafael
Director, Security Programs
Redwood Inference


From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 May 2025 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Marta / Rafael — attaching a redacted SCIM delta export (last 30 days) and a redacted SAML assertion sample. Attachment stubs below: 

Attachments: scim_delta_sample_202505-export.csv (CSV, 85 rows, user/provisioning events), saml_assertion_sample.txt (redacted assertion, removed PII)

Notes: 
- The SCIM export includes create/update/delete events keyed by user id with timestamps and the originating provisioning source (Okta -> SCIM). We scrubbed email addresses and replaced them with internal ids per our redaction policy.
- SAML assertions include group membership under the 'roles' attribute; team-to-role mapping is documented in the runbook Jordan will attach.

If the assessor needs full email addresses for a sampling exercise we can provide them under an NDA and via the secure folder.

— Soojin
IAM Engineer


From: OSS Assessments <auditor@oss-assessments.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
Date: Wed, 21 May 2025 11:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Thanks — received the SCIM and SAML samples. Two follow-ups: 

1) For rotation evidence, can you provide the last two rotation job logs for the service-account fleet and a note on the automated enforcement (cron/CI job id + owner)?
2) For ephemeral sessions: we need an example log that ties a short-lived token issuance to a user session and the corresponding audit entry that records token revocation/expiry. Prefer a single request/response trace.

If those logs are sensitive, point us to the minimal redacted extracts.

Best,
OSS Assessments (on behalf of HealthDocs)


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: OSS Assessments <auditor@oss-assessments.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marta Velasquez <security@healthdocs.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 May 2025 14:50:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request: ephemeral access & service-account rotation

Hi all — attaching the rotation policy and two redacted rotation job logs. Attachment stubs: service-account-rotation-policy-v1.2.pdf, rotation-job-20250428.log, rotation-job-20250512.log.

Summary: 
- Rotation is enforced monthly via an internal rotation pipeline (CI job id: infra-rotate-svc-01, owner: infra-runbook@redwood.com). 
- The job records include key identifiers and a hash of the rotated credential; we keep the raw secret in the KMS only.
- Ephemeral tokens are issued by the auth-service (auth.redwood.internal) and recorded in the session-audit stream (Elastic indices: sessions-YYYY.MM). A representative session issuance + expiry pair is in the session-audit export link (Drive).

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-HealthDocs-evidence (access by permission only).

If you want a 30-min walkthrough I can host a screenshare and show the job output (redacted) and the session trace.

— Jordan
Principal Infra Engineer

source.subject: Re: RFI - Resilience and FFIEC controls (First National Bank)
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Lisa Hammond <lisa.hammond@fnb.com>, procurement@fnb.com
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 Mar 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: RFI - Resilience and FFIEC controls (First National Bank)

Hi Lisa / Procurement team,

Thanks for sending over the RFI. Attached is our initial response for Section A (controls, uptime targets, and audit artifacts). Highlights:
- We meet FFIEC-aligned resilience expectations; runbooks and failover tests conducted quarterly.
- SOC 2 Type II available; summary attached.
- Network diagram and isolation controls attached.

Two clarifications we expect you may ask for:
1) If you want raw logs or a scoped environment for audit review we can provide a limited read-only access window.
2) For on-prem / private deployments we can supply an architecture pack outlining HSM/KMS integrations.

Attached: FNB_RFI_Response_SectionA.pdf; SOC2-summary.pdf; Network-architecture-diagram.png

Next steps: We can schedule a 30-min follow-up to walk through our resilience evidence and agree on any additional artifacts. Rafael and Naomi are copied to answer technical and compliance Qs.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
(415) 555-0123

---
Attachment stubs: FNB_RFI_Response_SectionA.pdf (PDF, 18 pages); SOC2-summary.pdf (PDF, 3 pages); Network-architecture-diagram.png (PNG, 1 file)

From: Lisa Hammond <lisa.hammond@fnb.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@fnb.com
Date: Wed, 19 Mar 2025 11:08:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: RFI - Resilience and FFIEC controls (First National Bank)

Ben,

Thanks for the quick turn. Initial read looks promising. A few items procurement and I flagged that need more detail before we move to vendor scoring:
- Can you provide the most recent tabletop/resilience test report (redacted) and date of last DR failover?
- Confirm RTO/RPO SLAs for hosted vs. private deployments and any differences by region.
- We need an SSP or equivalent that maps controls to FFIEC domains (operations, vendor mgmt, business continuity).

If possible, share answers in an annotated PDF (so we can track line-level responses). Procurement would like the artifacts by EOD Friday (21 Mar). If you need more time, let me know.

Regards,

Lisa Hammond
Director, Vendor Risk
First National Bank
lisa.hammond@fnb.com

On Wed, 19 Mar 2025 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks again — appreciate the turnaround.

---
Note: procurement@fnb.com requested the vendor to denote any items that require NDA/TPA before data sharing.

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 Mar 2025 13:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FNB RFI - follow-ups for Lisa

Ben / Rafael,

Saw Lisa's follow-up. Draft plan to respond:
1) Tabletop report: redact customer identifiers and provide executive summary + date (we ran a cross-region tabletop on 2025-01-15; full DR failover was a simulated switchover in 2024-11-09 with <5 minutes downtime observed). Attach redacted incident timeline.
2) RTO/RPO: Hosted API (multi-az): RTO 2 minutes for control-plane failover; RPO near zero for session continuity with KV caching; Dedicated pools: RTO 5-15 minutes depending on failover policy; Private on-prem: depends on customer topology — we provide recommended configs and expected RTOs in the SSP.
3) SSP: I will assemble an FFIEC-mapped SSP extract and annotate the PDF. I will pull the control matrix mapping today and upload to Drive.

I'll prepare an annotated PDF and a short cover memo addressing procurement's NDA question (we can provide redacted artifacts without additional NDA; full raw logs require a signed DPA amendment). Rafael — can you confirm the technical numbers for RTO/RPO and attach the network diagram with callouts?

--
Naomi Feldman
Security & Compliance Lead
Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwood.ai

---
Attachment plan: redacted_tabletop_execsummary.pdf (to be created); SSP_FFIEC_extract.pdf (to be created); link to internal Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cFNB-RFI-response

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Lisa Hammond <lisa.hammond@fnb.com>, procurement@fnb.com
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 Mar 2025 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: RFI - Resilience and FFIEC controls (First National Bank)

Lisa / procurement team,

Per your request, please find the attached annotated RFI PDF and the redacted tabletop executive summary. Summary of the deliverables:
- FNB_RFI_Response_SectionA.pdf (annotated with line-by-line responses to the FFIEC-style questions)
- redacted_tabletop_execsummary.pdf (exec summary of 2025-01-15 tabletop + 2024-11-09 simulated failover)
- SOC2-summary.pdf (already provided)
- Network-architecture-diagram.png (with callouts; Rafael updated to show cross-AZ failover paths)

Key points:
- We performed a coordinated failover exercise on 2024-11-09 with an observed recovery window of under 5 minutes for in-flight sessions using our active-standby control-plane pattern.
- For hosted customers in US regions, our standard SLA is 99.95% monthly with an RTO target of under 5 minutes for control-plane events; Dedicated customers can opt into a 99.99% profile with reserved capacity and cross-AZ replication.
- SSP extract maps Redwood controls to FFIEC domains and includes artifact references (SOC 2, runbooks, test logs). We are willing to grant a limited, read-only audit window for on-site reviewers under an executed NDA/DPA amendment.

Links: Artifact bundle on Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cFNB-RFI-response
DocuSign for DPA amendment (if needed): https://app.docusign.com/prepare/abc123-fnb-rfi

If that looks complete, would you like to schedule a 30-min walkthrough next week? Rafael and Naomi can join for the technical deep dive.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
(415) 555-0123

---
Attachment stubs: FNB_RFI_Response_SectionA.pdf (annotated, PDF, 20 pages); redacted_tabletop_execsummary.pdf (PDF, 2 pages); SOC2-summary.pdf (PDF, 3 pages); Network-architecture-diagram.png (PNG, updated)
