source.subject: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)
source.body:
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 9:14 AM PDT
Subject: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)
Attachments: AuditExporter_ThreatModel_v0.3.pdf

Team,

Thanks for walking through the audit export threat model yesterday. Capturing notes + required mitigations we need before Security can sign off for limited beta.

Key risks discussed:
1) Secret exposure (Splunk HEC token, Datadog API key, S3 assume-role creds) via logs, metrics labels, config endpoints
2) Data exfil/SSRF via destination configuration (customer-controlled URLs / hosts)
3) Cross-tenant leakage (exporter config sync, checkpoints, DLQ)
4) PII/sensitive data leakage in exported audit events (esp. user agent / IP + any freeform “details” fields)

Required mitigations (must-have for beta enablement):
A. Destination validation + egress controls
- Enforce allowlist patterns per destination type (Splunk HEC hostnames, Datadog intake domain; no arbitrary URLs)
- Reject private IP ranges / link-local / localhost / non-HTTPS
- DNS resolution check should be done at config validation time (control plane) AND guardrail at send time (exporter) to prevent post-config DNS rebinding.
- Add explicit outbound egress allowlist hooks in exporter runtime (we can treat this as “deny by default” except known sinks).

B. Secrets handling
- No raw tokens/keys stored in exporter config. Control plane should store refs only (secret manager/KMS-backed) and exporter fetches via scoped identity.
- Ensure secret material never enters logs (including debug). Scrub headers in error logs.
- Rotation: document how to rotate destination secrets without downtime; exporter should pick up new secret within N minutes.

C. Tenant isolation
- Checkpoint/DLQ storage namespace must be per-tenant AND per-export-config (avoid shared keys).
- Config sync: exporter should always include tenant_id/workspace_id in the authz context when pulling config. No “list all configs then filter in memory”.

D. PII minimization
- Confirm we are not exporting raw prompt text, completion text, headers, etc. Audit export should stick to canonical audit event schema only.
- If including ip_address/user_agent, ensure they’re optional + only on events where policy allows. Please confirm redaction rules are applied before export.

Sign-off criteria (Security):
1) Above A/B/C/D items implemented + reviewed (PR links ok)
2) Unit tests for destination validation edge cases (localhost, 169.254.169.254, RFC1918, punycode, http downgrade)
3) Runbook updated with “pause exports” + “rotate secret” + “destination validation failures” sections
4) Threat model doc updated to v1.0 with “beta scope disclaimers” (hosted/dedicated only; private out of scope)

Can you reply with owners + target dates? If helpful, Kevin/Hanae and I can do a 30 min follow-up review once changes are in.

- Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 10:02 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

Marcus — super helpful, thank you.

Proposed owners/dates:
- A (destination validation + egress hooks): Connor + my team. Target EOW for validation in control plane + exporter-side guard. We’ll implement “destination type”-based validation (Splunk/Datadog fixed domains; S3 is AWS SDK / no URL). For Splunk we’ll support allowlisted FQDNs only (no IP literal).
- B (secrets handling): Hanae, with Benji (not on thread) to wire secret refs end-to-end. Target early next week; we already have secret refs in control plane draft but need to ensure exporter never sees raw values except at send time.
- C (tenant isolation): my team; checkpoint + DLQ prefixes will include tenant_id + export_config_id. We’ll add a guard that rejects missing tenant_id.
- D (PII): Kevin to confirm policy; on platform side we can enforce schema allowlist at export boundary.

Re: DNS rebinding guard at send time — makes sense. We can do a periodic re-resolve + compare with CIDR denylist. Need to be careful about adding latency per batch.

Will post PR links here as they land.

-Ethan


From: Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 11:18 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

Adding a few specifics on secrets so we’re aligned:

- Storage: Control plane stores destination credentials encrypted w/ KMS and returns only a credential_ref to exporter via config sync.
- Retrieval: Exporter uses its service identity to call internal Secrets API “GetCredential(tenant_id, credential_ref)”. We should scope authz to tenant_id + export_config_id.
- Logging: we’ll add a redaction filter for headers/fields named token/api_key/secret/access_key and ensure adapter errors don’t include request headers.
- Rotation: we can support “create new credential + update export config to point to new ref”; exporter will pick up on next config refresh. Suggest default refresh interval 60s for beta.

Ask: for Splunk HEC “test connection” in Console — please ensure the backend does not echo the token back in response payloads (we’ve seen this bug elsewhere).

-Hanae


From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 1:07 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)
Attachments: ENG-5189-review-checklist.txt

+1 to all.

PII/policy confirmations:
- IP address + user agent are permitted ONLY for auth/session/security events (login, token creation/revocation, permission changes, export config changes). Not permitted for inference request-level events.
- “details” field must remain structured + allowlisted (no freeform blobs). If any service emits a string payload into details, it should be dropped or replaced with “[REDACTED]”.
- Export boundary should enforce the audit schema v2 allowlist. If an event has unknown keys, exporter should drop those keys and increment a metric.

Also, please ensure we don’t export internal network identifiers (pod IPs, node names) in source.* fields.

-Kevin


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 2:46 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

From SRE side, the “pause exports” / “kill switch” requirement is important and also security-adjacent (limits blast radius if a sink is misconfigured).

Requests:
- Expose an operator control to disable an export config quickly (control plane toggle that exporter honors within 1–2 min).
- Alerts should NOT include destination hostnames/tokens. For destination, include only destination_type + export_config_id.
- Add an explicit metric for validation failures (config rejected) vs delivery failures (adapter).

Once you have the first cut of destination validation, we can do a quick chaos test by forcing downstream 429s + verifying the exporter doesn’t “fail open” to alternate hosts.

-Sean

From SRE side, the “pause exports” / “kill switch” requirement is important and also security-adjacent (limits blast radius if a sink is misconfigured).

Requests:
- Expose an operator control to disable an export config quickly (control plane toggle that exporter honors within 1–2 min).
- Alerts should NOT include destination hostnames/tokens. For destination, include only destination_type + export_config_id.
- Add an explicit metric for validation failures (config rejected) vs delivery failures (adapter).

Once you have the first cut of destination validation, we can do a quick chaos test by forcing downstream 429s + verifying the exporter doesn’t “fail open” to alternate hosts.

-Sean


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 4:37 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

This all looks good.

A couple clarifications for “beta sign-off” so we don’t block on nice-to-haves:

Must-have for enabling the first beta tenants:
- Destination validation (including private-range/localhost/metadata IP protections) + exporter-side enforcement
- Secret refs end-to-end + redaction in logs/errors
- Per-tenant/per-config namespacing for checkpoints + DLQ
- Schema allowlist enforcement at export boundary (drop unknown keys)
- Kill switch / pause exports path documented in runbook

Can land after first beta enablement (but before expanding cohort):
- DNS rebinding continuous monitoring (as long as we have the exporter-side CIDR denylist and disallow IP literals)
- Additional punycode edge-case tests

Please tag me on the PRs for A/B/C so we can do a fast review turnaround.

- Marcus

On Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 10:02 AM Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Marcus — super helpful, thank you.
>
> Proposed owners/dates:
> - A (destination validation + egress hooks): Connor + my team. Target EOW for validation in control plane + exporter-side guard...

source.subject: SRE review: Dedicated staged rollout gates + alerting
source.body:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 7, 2025 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: SRE review: Dedicated staged rollout gates + alerting

Priya (+ Lauren/Marissa/Connor),

SRE review notes on the proposed default rollout gates + alerting behavior for “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools”. Overall direction is good; the big thing we need is to keep promotion criteria aligned with (a) error budget / incident posture and (b) capacity headroom so the canary stage doesn’t create its own outage.

Below are SRE requirements + suggested default thresholds. Happy to jump on a 20 min sync if we want to lock this today.

---
1) Promotion eligibility must be error-budget aware (not just instantaneous metrics)
- We should block promote when the pool is already burning budget / in incident.
- Proposal: “No promote” if error budget burn > 2% in the last 60m OR active incident flag for the pool/region.
- If we don’t have budget burn wiring for v1, minimum acceptable: block promote if 5xx > 1% (rolling 15m) OR 429 > 2% (rolling 15m) across the pool.

Rationale: otherwise rollout automation will amplify existing degradation.

---
2) Gate defaults (v1) — keep them simple + consistent with Dedicated SLOs
Assuming typical Dedicated tier SLOs (confirm with Lauren):

A) Error gate
- Pass if (5xx_rate + upstream_timeout_rate) <= 0.5% for 15m
- AND 429_rate <= 1.0% for 15m
- Min sample size: >= 500 requests in window for that stage (otherwise “Insufficient data” -> hold, not fail)

B) Latency gate
- Pass if p95 latency <= max( baseline_p95 * 1.10, baseline_p95 + 50ms )
- AND p99 latency <= max( baseline_p99 * 1.15, baseline_p99 + 100ms )
- Window: 15m; require >= 200 requests

Notes:
- The relative-to-baseline piece matters because absolute numbers vary a lot by model/context length.
- Baseline should be the prior stable stage (e.g., current prod partition), not “global SLO target”, otherwise we get false fails.

C) Saturation / headroom gate (this is the one that will save us from alert storms + customer pain)
- Pass if GPU_util_p95 <= 85% (15m)
- AND queue_depth_p95 <= threshold (model-dependent, but we can start with <= 2 * steady-state)
- AND “headroom” >= 15% at stage level (see below)

Headroom definition suggestion:
- headroom = (available_tokens_per_sec - observed_tokens_per_sec) / available_tokens_per_sec
- For small pools where TPS estimation is noisy, use replica-based guardrail: canary must reserve at least 2 replicas OR >= 10% of replicas, whichever is larger.

D) OOM / GPU health gate
- Pass if OOM_rate <= 0.05% of requests (15m) and no GPU unhealthy nodes added in last 10m.

---
3) Missing data / telemetry lag behavior
This is the biggest source of “noise” during oncall:
- Gate evaluation should be fail-closed for “Pass” (i.e., don’t allow auto-promote when data is stale/missing).
- BUT: missing data should NOT automatically flip the rollout to “Failed” (otherwise we’ll page during telemetry issues).

Proposed states:
- PASS / FAIL / INSUFFICIENT_DATA
- INSUFFICIENT_DATA => stage stays “Held (needs operator)” with a specific reason (“metrics stale > 5m” vs “sample size < N”).

---
4) Alerting requirements (avoid paging ourselves into oblivion)
We should separate “customer-impacting” vs “rollout health” alerts:

A) Page (SRE oncall) ONLY when:
- gate fail correlates with pool-level customer impact signals (elevated 5xx across pool, sustained 429, or p99 blowup), OR
- rollback automation fails / stuck in “abort requested” > 5m, OR
- promotion causes multi-region imbalance / starvation (routing guardrails tripped)

B) Ticket / Slack notify for:
- stage gate FAIL confined to canary partition with no pool-level impact
- INSUFFICIENT_DATA states
- repeated flapping across windows (e.g., pass/fail oscillation) — this is an action item for thresholds/queries, not a page

Also: please ensure we annotate dashboards with “Promote/Hold/Abort/Force-*” events so triage doesn’t require log spelunking.

---
5) Console / UX copy (customer expectations)
If customers see “Gate failed” they will assume regression.
- Recommend differentiating:
  - “Gate failed: regression detected” (real FAIL)
  - “Gate not evaluated: missing data” (INSUFFICIENT_DATA)
- And make the “Force promote” path explicitly warn it may violate SLOs + is audit logged.

If we can agree, I’ll treat this as SRE sign-off for default gate logic for beta, with the assumption we’ll tune thresholds with initial dogfood.

Thanks,
Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Execution Lead
Redwood Inference



From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 7, 2025 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: SRE review: Dedicated staged rollout gates + alerting

This is great, thank you.

Two questions before we lock:
1) For the baseline-relative latency check: do you have a preference on baseline window? (e.g., prior 1h of “stable” stage vs last 15m). We’re trying to avoid baselines drifting during daily traffic changes.
2) For “no promote when burning budget”: can we start with your “minimum acceptable” (5xx/429) for beta and add true budget burn integration in GA? I don’t want to block beta on wiring.

Also +1 on not paging for INSUFFICIENT_DATA. We got burned by that last quarter.

Priya


On Fri, Mar 7, 2025 at 9:14 AM Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Priya (+ Lauren/Marissa/Connor),
> ...



From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 7, 2025 at 12:18 PM PST
Subject: Re: SRE review: Dedicated staged rollout gates + alerting

+1 to Rafael’s saturation/headroom point — we saw the 429 spike pattern in early partition tests when canary was too small vs burst.

For Dedicated, I strongly prefer we enforce a hard minimum canary size for small reservations:
- If pool replicas < 20, do replica-based stages (e.g., 2 replicas canary, then 20%, then 50%, then 100%)
- If pool replicas >= 20, traffic % is okay.

Also: for 429 gating, I’d suggest we treat 429 as “capacity signal” not “app error” — i.e., block promotion and recommend increasing canary partition, rather than immediately concluding runtime regression.

If we’re aligned, I’ll update PM-827 with these defaults and what’s configurable.

Lauren



From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 7, 2025 at 3:02 PM PST
Subject: Re: SRE review: Dedicated staged rollout gates + alerting

Answers inline.

1) Baseline window preference
- Suggest baseline = p95/p99 for the “stable” partition over last 60m, recomputed every 5m.
- If traffic is highly non-stationary, we can cap drift by using median of 12x5m buckets (robust to short spikes).
- Key is that baseline source is NOT the canary partition.

2) Error budget wiring
Yes, we can start with the proxy signals (5xx/429 + incident flag if available) for beta.
- Please keep the policy surface the same so we can swap to true burn-rate later without changing semantics.
- SRE requirement for GA: integrate burn-rate at least at pool+region granularity.

Lauren’s note on minimum canary size: agree.
- I’d like to see that enforced by validation (plan create/update) so we don’t rely on operators remembering it.
- If we do that, 429 should become much less noisy as an alert.

Final ask from SRE to consider this “signed off” for beta:
- Alert routing spec in writing (what pages vs tickets), even if it’s short.
- Console copy distinguishes FAIL vs INSUFFICIENT_DATA (so customers don’t open sev-1 tickets for telemetry lag).

If you want, I can write the first pass of the “page vs ticket” matrix and send it to Connor/Marissa for inclusion in the launch checklist.

Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Execution Lead
Redwood Inference

2) Error budget wiring
Yes, we can start with the proxy signals (5xx/429 + incident flag if available) for beta.
- Please keep the policy surface the same so we can swap to true burn-rate later without changing semantics.
- SRE requirement for GA: integrate burn-rate at least at pool+region granularity.

Lauren’s note on minimum canary size: agree.
- I’d like to see that enforced by validation (plan create/update) so we don’t rely on operators remembering it.
- If we do that, 429 should become much less noisy as an alert.

Final ask from SRE to consider this “signed off” for beta:
- Alert routing spec in writing (what pages vs tickets), even if it’s short.
- Console copy distinguishes FAIL vs INSUFFICIENT_DATA (so customers don’t open sev-1 tickets for telemetry lag).

If you want, I can write the first pass of the “page vs ticket” matrix and send it to Connor/Marissa for inclusion in the launch checklist.

Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Execution Lead
Redwood Inference

source.body:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go
Attachments: runtime-1.19-tp-stability-canary-readout-2025-03-03.pdf; tp-fix-rollout-checklist-v3.docx

Requesting go/no-go today for Runtime 1.19 rollout focused on TP stability fixes.

Proposed sequencing (Dedicated-first):
1) Dedicated canary: 2 clusters (A100 80GB + H100), 10% traffic per cluster, 24 hours.
2) Dedicated ramp: 25% -> 50% -> 100% over 3–4 days if green.
3) Hosted: start 5% after Dedicated hits 50% and we’ve had 48 hours without new hang signatures.

Ask:
- SRE: risk acceptance + paging/rollback criteria.
- Product: Dedicated-first sequencing + comms readiness.

Key gating checks are in the attached checklist; canary readout attached. Primary owners: Noah (tech), Connor (rollout), Rafael (SRE on-call readiness), Lauren/Owen (customer/support readiness).

Links for quick reference:
- Linear ENG-4829 (release plan)
- Linear ENG-4821 (initiative)
- Dashboard panels/alerts (observability-pack/pr-412)

If we’re aligned, Connor will schedule the Dedicated canary window for tomorrow morning PT.

Marissa Cole
Rollouts / Canary Product Owner
Redwood Inference

---

From: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:31 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Adding a short canary summary here (details in the PDF):

What’s in 1.19 (TP stability slice):
- Scheduler guardrail to prevent collective starvation (PR-21841)
- NCCL stream/event sync hardening (PR-21853)
- TP watchdog + safe abort/drain (PR-21860) behind flag TP_WATCHDOG=on
- KV compaction safety in TP (PR-21866)
- Fix deadlock on cancelled streaming requests (PR-21890)
- Better NCCL error surfacing (PR-21912)
- Safer defaults/guardrails for TP concurrency caps (PR-21897) behind flag TP_SAFE_DEFAULTS=on

Canary (perf-canary + benchmark-lab) results:
- Repro harness (mixed prefill/decode) that previously wedged within 20–40 mins: now no hangs over 6 hours x 3 runs on A100-80GB (TP=4) and 4 hours x 2 runs on H100 (TP=4).
- Watchdog behaved as designed under fault injection: abort -> drain -> new replica takes traffic (no global GPU wedging).
- p50/p95 latency: no meaningful change; p99 improved on the specific mixed workload due to fewer stalls/timeouts. We do see a ~0.8–1.6% throughput hit in a microbench when NCCL sync is forced; we’ve scoped sync to only the collective boundaries and left fast path unchanged.

Known risks / what we didn’t solve:
- If NCCL itself hard-hangs at the driver level, watchdog will drain but we may still need node recycle (runbook covers).
- False positive risk on watchdog for long-context bursts; we set defaults conservatively and will enable by tier.

My recommendation: proceed with Dedicated-first as Marissa outlined. Keep TP_WATCHDOG off for first 10% canary, then enable after 2 hours if error budget looks clean.

Noah

---

From: Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:54 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

SRE/Obs notes:
- The new heartbeat gap alert is configured to page only when (a) heartbeat gap > 90s AND (b) request timeout rate > threshold in same cluster. This avoids paging on transient backpressure.
- Dedicated canary dashboards are ready; alert routes to eng-sre + eng-runtime-oncall.
- Please keep additional NCCL debug logging OFF by default (INT-2219), and only flip for a 30–60 min window if we need deeper traces.

No objections from me to canarying tomorrow, assuming we keep rollback criteria strict.

Aisha

---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:13 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

SRE approval contingent on these explicit rollback triggers during canary (Dedicated):

Rollback immediately if any of the following occur in canary clusters:
1) >2 replica drains/hour due to TP stall signals (watchdog or heartbeat gap) for 2 consecutive hours.
2) p99 latency +25% vs baseline for 30 minutes (cluster-level) with correlated GPU util < 40% (stall signature).
3) Any recurrence of the "decode iteration stuck" signature (same as Feb 17 Sev1) lasting >5 minutes.

Operational readiness:
- On-call runbook is updated with the drain/replace and concurrency cap steps.
- We will have an SRE (me or Tomáš) in the canary channel for the first 4 hours.

I support Dedicated-first. Hosted should wait until Dedicated 50% is green for 48 hours (as Marissa said).

Rafael

---

From: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Product is aligned with Dedicated-first sequencing.

Request: for the first 24h canary window, please keep TP_SAFE_DEFAULTS=on (concurrency/batch guardrails) in Dedicated canary clusters even if some accounts are configured higher today. I’d rather take a small throughput hit than risk a stall while we validate.

Lauren/Owen: please prep the short customer update template (we can send proactively to the accounts that had escalations in Feb).

Paula

---

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Got it. Owen and I will draft a short note today for the Feb escalation accounts:
- what’s changing in 1.19
- canary timeline and how we’re gating
- what signals we’re watching
- what we need from them if they see timeouts (request IDs + approximate times)

One question for Noah: should we promise any change to the current interim mitigation (concurrency cap) during canary, or keep it in place until 1.19 hits 100% Dedicated?

Lauren

---

From: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 11:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Keep the interim concurrency cap in place through canary + first ramp (to 50%).

If we’re clean for 48 hours at 50% with watchdog enabled and no new stall signatures, we can start relaxing caps per account (one at a time) with SRE watching.

For customer messaging: I’d avoid promising immediate cap removal; message it as “stability improvements rolling out; we’ll reassess caps as we validate.”

Noah

---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 11:47 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

If approved, proposed schedule:

Tue 3/4
- 09:00–11:00 PT: Dedicated canary start (10%) on ded-a100-usw + ded-h100-use
- 11:00 PT: enable TP_WATCHDOG in canary clusters (if green) + keep TP_SAFE_DEFAULTS on
- 17:00 PT: checkpoint + decide whether to hold overnight or proceed to 25% Wed AM

Wed 3/5
- 09:30 PT: ramp to 25% (if no rollback triggers)

All checkpoints + comms are in ENG-4829.

Connor

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 12:22 PM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Go for Dedicated canary tomorrow with Rafael’s rollback criteria.

Two conditions:
1) We do not expand Hosted exposure until Dedicated has 48 hours green at 50% with watchdog enabled.
2) Any recurrence resembling the Feb 17 stall signature: treat as Sev2 minimum, stop the rollout, and page runtime lead + SRE lead.

Thanks for keeping this tight. Let’s keep comms crisp and avoid flipping multiple knobs at once outside the planned checkpoints.

Sean Gallagher
Director, Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference

---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 1:07 PM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Confirmed.

Action items:
- Connor: schedule Tue 3/4 canary and post checkpoints in #eng-releases.
- Rafael/Aisha: validate alert routes + confirm on-call coverage for first 4 hours.
- Noah: final confirmation of flag plan (TP_SAFE_DEFAULTS on from start; TP_WATCHDOG enabled after 2h if green).
- Lauren/Owen: draft customer update (no promises on cap removal).

We’ll treat any Feb-17-like signature as immediate stop + escalation per Sean.

Marissa

text:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go
Attachments: runtime-1.19-tp-stability-canary-readout-2025-03-03.pdf; tp-fix-rollout-checklist-v3.docx

Requesting go/no-go today for Runtime 1.19 rollout focused on TP stability fixes.

Proposed sequencing (Dedicated-first):
1) Dedicated canary: 2 clusters (A100 80GB + H100), 10% traffic per cluster, 24 hours.
2) Dedicated ramp: 25% -> 50% -> 100% over 3–4 days if green.
3) Hosted: start 5% after Dedicated hits 50% and we’ve had 48 hours without new hang signatures.

Ask:
- SRE: risk acceptance + paging/rollback criteria.
- Product: Dedicated-first sequencing + comms readiness.

Key gating checks are in the attached checklist; canary readout attached. Primary owners: Noah (tech), Connor (rollout), Rafael (SRE on-call readiness), Lauren/Owen (customer/support readiness).

Links for quick reference:
- Linear ENG-4829 (release plan)
- Linear ENG-4821 (initiative)
- Dashboard panels/alerts (observability-pack/pr-412)

If we’re aligned, Connor will schedule the Dedicated canary window for tomorrow morning PT.

Marissa Cole
Rollouts / Canary Product Owner
Redwood Inference

---

From: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:31 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Adding a short canary summary here (details in the PDF):

What’s in 1.19 (TP stability slice):
- Scheduler guardrail to prevent collective starvation (PR-21841)
- NCCL stream/event sync hardening (PR-21853)
- TP watchdog + safe abort/drain (PR-21860) behind flag TP_WATCHDOG=on
- KV compaction safety in TP (PR-21866)
- Fix deadlock on cancelled streaming requests (PR-21890)
- Better NCCL error surfacing (PR-21912)
- Safer defaults/guardrails for TP concurrency caps (PR-21897) behind flag TP_SAFE_DEFAULTS=on

Canary (perf-canary + benchmark-lab) results:
- Repro harness (mixed prefill/decode) that previously wedged within 20–40 mins: now no hangs over 6 hours x 3 runs on A100-80GB (TP=4) and 4 hours x 2 runs on H100 (TP=4).
- Watchdog behaved as designed under fault injection: abort -> drain -> new replica takes traffic (no global GPU wedging).
- p50/p95 latency: no meaningful change; p99 improved on the specific mixed workload due to fewer stalls/timeouts. We do see a ~0.8–1.6% throughput hit in a microbench when NCCL sync is forced; we’ve scoped sync to only the collective boundaries and left fast path unchanged.

Known risks / what we didn’t solve:
- If NCCL itself hard-hangs at the driver level, watchdog will drain but we may still need node recycle (runbook covers).
- False positive risk on watchdog for long-context bursts; we set defaults conservatively and will enable by tier.

My recommendation: proceed with Dedicated-first as Marissa outlined. Keep TP_WATCHDOG off for first 10% canary, then enable after 2 hours if error budget looks clean.

Noah

---

From: Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>

- The new heartbeat gap alert is configured to page only when (a) heartbeat gap > 90s AND (b) request timeout rate > threshold in same cluster. This avoids paging on transient backpressure.
- Dedicated canary dashboards are ready; alert routes to eng-sre + eng-runtime-oncall.
- Please keep additional NCCL debug logging OFF by default (INT-2219), and only flip for a 30–60 min window if we need deeper traces.

No objections from me to canarying tomorrow, assuming we keep rollback criteria strict.

Aisha

SRE approval contingent on these explicit rollback triggers during canary (Dedicated):

Rollback immediately if any of the following occur in canary clusters:
1) >2 replica drains/hour due to TP stall signals (watchdog or heartbeat gap) for 2 consecutive hours.
2) p99 latency +25% vs baseline for 30 minutes (cluster-level) with correlated GPU util < 40% (stall signature).
3) Any recurrence of the "decode iteration stuck" signature (same as Feb 17 Sev1) lasting >5 minutes.

Operational readiness:
- On-call runbook is updated with the drain/replace and concurrency cap steps.
- We will have an SRE (me or Tomáš) in the canary channel for the first 4 hours.

I support Dedicated-first. Hosted should wait until Dedicated 50% is green for 48 hours (as Marissa said).

Rafael

source.subject: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback
source.body:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 6:48 PM PT
Subject: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback
Attachments: Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Beta_Enablement_Plan_v0.3.pdf; RolloutPlan_Migration_Checklist_v0.2.txt

All — sharing the proposed release plan for enabling Dedicated staged rollouts in prod for beta accounts/pools.

High-level goal: land schema + control plane + routing + telemetry stage tags safely (feature flagged), then enable in a single internal Dedicated pool as a prod canary, then expand to 1–2 beta customers.

Proposed sequence (prod):

0) Preconditions (before any prod enablement)
- Confirm the following are in the release train (or already deployed):
  - DB model + migrations: PR-28411
  - Promote/abort + audit hooks: PR-28458
  - Policy engine gate eval v1 (missing data behavior + caching): PR-28476
  - Routing split-by-stage: PR-28503
  - Stage tags in metrics/traces: PR-28521

1) Deploy (flags OFF) — Mon 3/3 AM PT
- Deploy control plane + routing changes with staged rollout feature flags disabled by default.
- Run additive DB migrations (no destructive changes).
- Run backfill job for any existing “pool rollouts” config to DedicatedPoolRolloutPlan rows (where applicable). This is idempotent.

2) Canary enablement — Mon 3/3 PM PT
- Enable on 1 internal Dedicated pool (single-region if possible; if not, one region at a time).
- Only allow manual gates for first 24h (operators promote stages; gate evidence still computed/displayed).
- Watch dashboard: p95/p99, error rate, 429/drop, saturation/headroom, OOM, GPU health.

3) Beta enablement (limited) — Wed 3/5
- Enable for 1 design partner account + 1 internal “high QPS” pool.
- Keep “force-promote/hold” admin-only.
- Require SRE sign-off before enabling multi-region pools.

Feature flags (current naming proposal; can adjust if needed)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_enabled (master switch)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_allow_accountlist (scoping gate)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_stage_tags_enabled (telemetry labeling)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_auto_gate_eval_enabled (auto-pass/auto-fail)

Canary success criteria (first 24h)
- No sustained increase in 429/drop attributable to partitioning (queue depth stable).
- No cardinality regressions in metrics (telemetry cost stable; label allowlist holding).
- Promote/abort actions idempotent under retries (no duplicate stage transitions).

Rollback plan (fast)
- Flip OFF dedicated_staged_rollouts_enabled + stage_tags_enabled (immediate effect on routing + telemetry labeling).
- Abort any active rollout plans (API supports abort; routing reverts to baseline “stable” partition).
- No DB rollback planned (additive schema). If needed, we can disable codepaths and leave rows unused.

Open items / asks
- Priya: confirm OK with “additive migration + no down-migration” posture for beta.
- Rafael: confirm alerting/oncall routing for gate failures during canary (page vs ticket).
- Lauren/Marissa: confirm beta account list + whether customer admins see “auto gate eval” initially or we keep manual-only for beta week 1.

Attachment has more detail on sequencing + command snippets.

Thanks,
Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 7:26 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Looks good overall.

Two questions/concerns from the platform side:
1) Migration/backfill: confirm it’s truly additive and safe to run with mixed versions (old control plane still running) for at least one deploy window. If the new tables are required for reads, we should have a “dual-read” or “read-old-if-missing” behavior.

2) Promotion idempotency: for promote/abort we should be using idempotency keys + optimistic concurrency (etag / version) so the Console can retry safely. I want to avoid “double promote” when there’s an API timeout.

If both are already addressed in PR-28458, then 👍.

Also: for rollback, please explicitly call out what happens to in-flight streams (pinned vs reroute). Even if we “don’t reroute,” we need to make sure we aren’t killing connections.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
Engineering Sponsor, Platform
Redwood Inference


---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 9:04 AM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

+1 on the plan.

From SRE side, I want to make sure the canary runbook is explicit on:

- Gate eval behavior under missing/late telemetry. Are we failing closed (i.e., gate = UNKNOWN => block promotion) or failing open? My preference is fail-closed with an admin override, but we need it documented.

- Alert routing during canary:
  - If we see 429/queue depth spike that’s clearly tied to partitioning, that should page Fleet/SRE immediately.
  - If a gate fails (latency/error) but traffic is low (1%), that’s a ticket unless customer impact is obvious.

- “Disable telemetry stage tags” needs to be its own rollback lever (cardinality risk). If we have to shut tags off, does the Console gate evidence panel degrade gracefully?

If you can add a short section to the checklist for: what to capture for post-incident review (links to dashboards, rollout plan ID, stage ID, timing), that will help.

Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Execution Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 2:17 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Thanks — replies inline.

On Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 7:26 PM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> 1) Migration/backfill: confirm it’s truly additive and safe to run with mixed versions

Confirmed.
- Schema is additive (new tables + indexes). No existing tables modified.
- Read paths remain compatible if the rollout plan rows don’t exist yet: control plane treats “no plan” as “no staged rollout enabled for pool.”
- Backfill job can run after deploy; it only creates plans where a pool has explicitly opted-in via the new flag.

> 2) Promotion idempotency

PR-28458 includes:
- Idempotency key header supported on promote/abort.
- Optimistic concurrency on plan_version (reject if stale; Console retries after refresh).

> for rollback, what happens to in-flight streams

Current behavior: in-flight streaming requests are pinned to the selected partition at request start; disabling flags affects new requests only. No connection termination as part of rollback.

Rafael — on gate behavior + alerts:
- Missing/late telemetry: we are FAIL-CLOSED for auto-gate eval (gate status = UNKNOWN blocks auto-promotion). Manual promotion is still possible with admin override, which emits an audit event + requires reason.
- Stage tags rollback: yes, stage_tags_enabled is a separate lever. Console should show “Evidence unavailable (telemetry tags disabled)” rather than an empty panel.

I’ll update the checklist doc to include:
- What to capture (plan_id, stage_id, region, timestamps, dashboard links, last 50 routing decisions sample if available).
- Paging thresholds for 429/queue spike during canary.

Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>

title: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback
preview:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 6:48 PM PT
Subject: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback
Attachments: Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Beta_Enablement_Plan_v0.3.pdf; RolloutPlan_Migration_Checklist_v0.2.txt

All — sharing the proposed release plan for enabling Dedicated staged rollouts in prod for beta accounts/pools.

High-level goal: land schema + control plane + routing + telemetry stage tags safely (feature flagged), then enable in a single internal Dedicated pool as a prod canary, then expand to 1–2 beta customers.

Proposed sequence (prod):

0) Preconditions (before any prod enablement)
- Confirm the following are in the release train (or already deployed):
  - DB model + migrations: PR-28411
  - Promote/abort + audit hooks: PR-28458
  - Policy engine gate eval v1 (missing data behavior + caching): PR-28476
  - Routing split-by-stage: PR-28503
  - Stage tags in metrics/traces: PR-28521

1) Deploy (flags OFF) — Mon 3/3 AM PT
- Deploy control plane + routing changes with staged rollout feature flags disabled by default.
- Run additive DB migrations (no destructive changes).
- Run backfill job for any existing “pool rollouts” config to DedicatedPoolRolloutPlan rows (where applicable). This is idempotent.

2) Canary enablement — Mon 3/3 PM PT
- Enable on 1 internal Dedicated pool (single-region if possible; if not, one region at a time).
- Only allow manual gates for first 24h (operators promote stages; gate evidence still computed/displayed).
- Watch dashboard: p95/p99, error rate, 429/drop, saturation/headroom, OOM, GPU health.

3) Beta enablement (limited) — Wed 3/5
- Enable for 1 design partner account + 1 internal “high QPS” pool.
- Keep “force-promote/hold” admin-only.
- Require SRE sign-off before enabling multi-region pools.

Feature flags (current naming proposal; can adjust if needed)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_enabled (master switch)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_allow_accountlist (scoping gate)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_stage_tags_enabled (telemetry labeling)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_auto_gate_eval_enabled (auto-pass/auto-fail)

Canary success criteria (first 24h)
- No sustained increase in 429/drop attributable to partitioning (queue depth stable).
- No cardinality regressions in metrics (telemetry cost stable; label allowlist holding).
- Promote/abort actions idempotent under retries (no duplicate stage transitions).

Rollback plan (fast)
- Flip OFF dedicated_staged_rollouts_enabled + stage_tags_enabled (immediate effect on routing + telemetry labeling).
- Abort any active rollout plans (API supports abort; routing reverts to baseline “stable” partition).
- No DB rollback planned (additive schema). If needed, we can disable codepaths and leave rows unused.

Open items / asks
- Priya: confirm OK with “additive migration + no down-migration” posture for beta.
- Rafael: confirm alerting/oncall routing for gate failures during canary (page vs ticket).
- Lauren/Marissa: confirm beta account list + whether customer admins see “auto gate eval” initially or we keep manual-only for beta week 1.

Attachment has more detail on sequencing + command snippets.

Thanks,
Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 7:26 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Looks good overall.

Two questions/concerns from the platform side:
1) Migration/backfill: confirm it’s truly additive and safe to run with mixed versions (old control plane still running) for at least one deploy window. If the new tables are required for reads, we should have a “dual-read” or “read-old-if-missing” behavior.

2) Promotion idempotency: for promote/abort we should be using idempotency keys + optimistic concurrency (etag / version) so the Console can retry safely. I want to avoid “double promote” when there’s an API timeout.

If both are already addressed in PR-28458, then 👍.

Also: for rollback, please explicitly call out what happens to in-flight streams (pinned vs reroute). Even if we “don’t reroute,” we need to make sure we aren’t killing connections.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
Engineering Sponsor, Platform
Redwood Inference

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 9:04 AM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

+1 on the plan.

From SRE side, I want to make sure the canary runbook is explicit on:

- Gate eval behavior under missing/late telemetry. Are we failing closed (i.e., gate = UNKNOWN => block promotion) or failing open? My preference is fail-closed with an admin override, but we need it documented.

- Alert routing during canary:
  - If we see 429/queue depth spike that’s clearly tied to partitioning, that should page Fleet/SRE immediately.
  - If a gate fails (latency/error) but traffic is low (1%), that’s a ticket unless customer impact is obvious.

- “Disable telemetry stage tags” needs to be its own rollback lever (cardinality risk). If we have to shut tags off, does the Console gate evidence panel degrade gracefully?

If you can add a short section to the checklist for: what to capture for post-incident review (links to dashboards, rollout plan ID, stage ID, timing), that will help.

Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Execution Lead
Redwood Inference

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 2:17 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Thanks — replies inline.

On Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 7:26 PM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> 1) Migration/backfill: confirm it’s truly additive and safe to run with mixed versions

Confirmed.
- Schema is additive (new tables + indexes). No existing tables modified.
- Read paths remain compatible if the rollout plan rows don’t exist yet: control plane treats “no plan” as “no staged rollout enabled for pool.”
- Backfill job can run after deploy; it only creates plans where a pool has explicitly opted-in via the new flag.

> 2) Promotion idempotency

PR-28458 includes:
- Idempotency key header supported on promote/abort.
- Optimistic concurrency on plan_version (reject if stale; Console retries after refresh).

> for rollback, what happens to in-flight streams

Current behavior: in-flight streaming requests are pinned to the selected partition at request start; disabling flags affects new requests only. No connection termination as part of rollback.

Rafael — on gate behavior + alerts:
- Missing/late telemetry: we are FAIL-CLOSED for auto-gate eval (gate status = UNKNOWN blocks auto-promotion). Manual promotion is still possible with admin override, which emits an audit event + requires reason.
- Stage tags rollback: yes, stage_tags_enabled is a separate lever. Console should show “Evidence unavailable (telemetry tags disabled)” rather than an empty panel.

I’ll update the checklist doc to include:
- What to capture (plan_id, stage_id, region, timestamps, dashboard links, last 50 routing decisions sample if available).
- Paging thresholds for 429/queue spike during canary.

Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements
source.body:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements

Team — we need to lock the decision today on gate evaluation behavior when telemetry is missing/late, and on override permissions + audit fields. This has become a blocker for policy engine + Console flows.

Decision we need:
1) Missing metrics / telemetry lag: do we fail-open or fail-closed?
2) What actions are allowed as overrides (force-promote, force-hold, force-abort/rollback), who can do them (RBAC), and what must be recorded in audit logs?

Proposed default (based on SRE feedback + Dedicated customer expectations):
- Fail-closed for *automatic promotion* when required signals are missing/stale.
- Do NOT auto-abort on missing metrics alone (hold stage + alert), because telemetry outages shouldn’t cause rollbacks.
- Allow explicit operator override with justification, and capture the full context in audit.

Open questions:
- Do we treat “partial missing” (one region missing, others present) as missing for the stage overall?
- Do we allow customers (Dedicated admins) to force-promote in v1, or Redwood-only?

If we align on this, I’ll ask the teams to update ADR + implementation tickets today.

— Priya
Engineering Sponsor, Platform


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements

+1 to fail-closed for auto-promotion.

SRE perspective:
- Promotion must be deterministic and defensible post-incident. If we “fail-open” we’ll end up promoting during telemetry gaps and have no evidence trail.
- But I agree we should NOT auto-rollback just because telemetry is down. That’s a separate class of incident.

Re partial missing (multi-region): treat as missing for the stage overall if:
- The stage is configured as multi-region (i.e., routing can land in that region), and
- The gate requires a regional view (latency/error/saturation), and
- One region is stale > staleness threshold.

Otherwise it’ll be too easy to promote while a problematic region is “dark.”

Overrides:
- Force-promote should be Redwood-only at GA (at least initially). Customers can request via support / TAM; oncall can execute.
- Require “reason” text + link to ticket/incident (Jira/Slack) in audit event.

Rafael
SRE (Dedicated)


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements

Agree with Rafael.

From a Dedicated PM lens, I’m worried about two failure modes we’ve already seen in early testing:
1) Telemetry lag makes the Console look “broken” and blocks promotion.
2) If we fail-closed, customers will push hard for self-serve overrides.

Suggestion:
- For v1: Redwood-defined signals are required for auto-promote, but we add very clear UX states:
  - PASS / FAIL / UNKNOWN (stale/missing)
  - UNKNOWN blocks auto-promote and changes the CTA to “Request override” (or “Force promote (admin)”).
- Customer self-serve override: not in MVP. We’ll get it wrong on RBAC + comms. But we should design the audit trail so it could support it later.

If possible, can we set a staleness grace window (e.g., allow up to 2–3 minutes) so transient scrapes don’t constantly flip UNKNOWN?

Lauren
Product, Dedicated


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>; Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2025 at 10:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements

On Console + workflow:

If we go fail-closed for auto-promote, we need to be explicit about what action is actually blocked.
- “Auto-promote” vs “Promote” (manual button) becomes confusing unless we define that the Promote button itself is gated.

Proposal (UX + backend contract):
- Promotion action evaluates gates at click time. If gates are FAIL or UNKNOWN, the Promote button is disabled by default.
- There is an “Override & Promote” action only visible to users with the override permission.
- The override flow requires:
  - reason (required)
  - optional link (Jira/incident)
  - checkbox acknowledging “metrics missing/unknown may hide regressions”

We also need a consistent message when UNKNOWN:
- “Telemetry delayed or missing for required signals in region us-east-1. Stage promotion is blocked until signals are fresh or an admin override is used.”

This lines up with ENG-5980/Console endpoints we’re working on.

Marissa
Product, Console & Rollouts


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2025 at 11:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements

Release / ops note: if promotion can be blocked by UNKNOWN, we should have an operational escape hatch that doesn’t require code changes mid-incident.

Ask:
- Ensure the override permission can be assigned to oncall admins (not a one-off DB edit).
- Ensure audit events are complete and exported (we’ve already had gaps in new event types before).

Also please confirm we are NOT auto-rolling back on UNKNOWN. That would be a nightmare during telemetry outages.

Connor
Release Engineering


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2025 at 1:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements

Locking decision (v1):

A) Missing / stale telemetry handling
- Gate evaluation returns one of: PASS / FAIL / UNKNOWN.
- UNKNOWN is used when any *required* signal is missing or stale beyond the staleness threshold.
- For multi-region pools: if any eligible region is UNKNOWN for a required gate, the stage gate result is UNKNOWN (i.e., stage is not promotable).
- UNKNOWN blocks promotion by default (i.e., “Promote stage” is not allowed).
- We will NOT auto-abort/rollback solely due to UNKNOWN. Instead:
  - stage remains in RUNNING (or HELD if operator chooses),
  - we alert/ticket, and
  - Console shows the missing/stale signal details.

Staleness threshold (initial): 3 minutes.
- If teams strongly object, bring data; otherwise we’ll tune after dogfood.

B) Overrides / permissions
- Override actions (v1): Force-promote, Hold, Abort.
- Force-promote is only available to Redwood operator/admin roles (not customer admins) for v1.
- Console will surface an “Override & Promote” action only when the caller has the override permission.

C) Audit logging requirements (must-have for beta)
Every promote/abort/hold/override must emit an audit event with:
- actor (user/service principal)
- pool_id / rollout_plan_id / stage_id
- action (PROMOTE | ABORT | HOLD | FORCE_PROMOTE)
- from_stage -> to_stage (where relevant)
- gate_summary at decision time (PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN per gate)
- evaluation window + staleness info (including which signals were stale/missing)
- override flag + reason (required for any override)
- optional “reference link” field (Jira/incident URL or ID)

Next steps (owners):
- Marissa: update Console copy/UX requirements to reflect UNKNOWN + override flow (ENG-5980).
- Connor: confirm release checklist includes RBAC assignment + audit export validation.
- Rafael: confirm alerting behavior for UNKNOWN (ticket vs page) and which oncall owns it.
- I’ll sync with the policy engine team to reflect this in ENG-5941 and update the ADR text.

Please reply by EOD only if you see a critical issue; otherwise we’ll proceed.

— Priya

title: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements
preview:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cross-team decision: gate evaluation failure-handling (missing metrics), overrides, audit requirements

Team — we need to lock the decision today on gate evaluation behavior when telemetry is missing/late, and on override permissions + audit fields. This has become a blocker for policy engine + Console flows.

Decision we need:
1) Missing metrics / telemetry lag: do we fail-open or fail-closed?
2) What actions are allowed as overrides (force-promote, force-hold, force-abort/rollback), who can do them (RBAC), and what must be recorded in audit logs?

Proposed default (based on SRE feedback + Dedicated customer expectations):
- Fail-closed for *automatic promotion* when required signals are missing/stale.
- Do NOT auto-abort on missing metrics alone (hold stage + alert), because telemetry outages shouldn’t cause rollbacks.
- Allow explicit operator override with justification, and capture the full context in audit.

Open questions:
- Do we treat “partial missing” (one region missing, others present) as missing for the stage overall?
- Do we allow customers (Dedicated admins) to force-promote in v1, or Redwood-only?

If we align on this, I’ll ask the teams to update ADR + implementation tickets today.

— Priya
Engineering Sponsor, Platform

source.subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)
source.body:
From: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Hi all — requesting final security/compliance approval on the permissions + audit approach for Incident Bot v2 before we merge the remaining PRs and enable beta for SRE rotation.

Summary of proposed model (details in attached PDF + scope matrix):

1) Service identity + scopes (least privilege)
- Bot runs as service identity: svc-incident-bot
- Two new internal scopes:
  - incident_bot:config_diff:read (customer-safe, redacted output only)
  - incident_bot:telemetry:read_privileged (bounded query templates; tenant-aware guardrails)
- No write scopes. No direct access to raw secret stores.

2) Config diff endpoint access
- Endpoint returns ONLY allowlisted fields by default; everything else is either omitted or redacted.
- Redaction layer runs server-side (not in Slack bot) to prevent leakage if Slack rendering changes.
- For Sev0/Sev1 only: break-glass path allows on-call to request an expanded diff view (still redacted for secrets, but includes more non-secret fields).
  - This requires incident_id + reason, plus elevated on-call role.

3) Privileged telemetry access
- Query service exposes template-based queries (no arbitrary PromQL/SQL).
- Hard limits:
  - time window max 30m (defaults to 15m)
  - max series/rows per query (bounded)
  - per-incident caching + coalescing
  - rate limits + circuit breaker during upstream slowness

4) Audit logging
- Audit event emitted for:
  - every config diff request (including tenant/service identifiers + incident_id)
  - every privileged telemetry request (including template id + bounds)
  - every break-glass elevation attempt (approved/denied)
- Audit events go to SIEM pipeline with retention aligned to current internal standard.

Open items we want sign-off on today:
- Is the break-glass flow acceptable as described (Sev0/Sev1 only, logged, time-limited)?
- Are the audit event fields sufficient for later review/investigation?

Attachments:
- incident-bot-v2-permissions-audit-approval-summary-v3.pdf
- incident-bot-scope-matrix-v2.xlsx

title: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)
preview:
From: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Hi all — requesting final security/compliance approval on the permissions + audit approach for Incident Bot v2 before we merge the remaining PRs and enable beta for SRE rotation.

Summary of proposed model (details in attached PDF + scope matrix):

1) Service identity + scopes (least privilege)
- Bot runs as service identity: svc-incident-bot
- Two new internal scopes:
  - incident_bot:config_diff:read (customer-safe, redacted output only)
  - incident_bot:telemetry:read_privileged (bounded query templates; tenant-aware guardrails)
- No write scopes. No direct access to raw secret stores.

2) Config diff endpoint access
- Endpoint returns ONLY allowlisted fields by default; everything else is either omitted or redacted.
- Redaction layer runs server-side (not in Slack bot) to prevent leakage if Slack rendering changes.
- For Sev0/Sev1 only: break-glass path allows on-call to request an expanded diff view (still redacted for secrets, but includes more non-secret fields).
  - This requires incident_id + reason, plus elevated on-call role.

3) Privileged telemetry access
- Query service exposes template-based queries (no arbitrary PromQL/SQL).
- Hard limits:
  - time window max 30m (defaults to 15m)
  - max series/rows per query (bounded)
  - per-incident caching + coalescing
  - rate limits + circuit breaker during upstream slowness

4) Audit logging
- Audit event emitted for:
  - every config diff request (including tenant/service identifiers + incident_id)
  - every privileged telemetry request (including template id + bounds)
  - every break-glass elevation attempt (approved/denied)
- Audit events go to SIEM pipeline with retention aligned to current internal standard.

Open items we want sign-off on today:
- Is the break-glass flow acceptable as described (Sev0/Sev1 only, logged, time-limited)?
- Are the audit event fields sufficient for later review/investigation?

Attachments:
- incident-bot-v2-permissions-audit-approval-summary-v3.pdf
- incident-bot-scope-matrix-v2.xlsx

If approved, Ava will update ADR-013 with final language and we’ll proceed with merges for PR-29133 / PR-29140.

Thanks,
Kaitlyn
Authn/Authz & Tenant Isolation
Redwood Inference
