source.subject: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)
source.body:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 4, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Connor / Sahana — following up on yesterday’s release sync.

We need to lock a capacity plan for perf-canary + release-branch CI so LTS patch week doesn’t starve mainline CI (and vice versa). Goal is: gates are reliable enough to block an LTS publish, without causing a 24–48h backlog.

Proposal (happy to adjust):

1) Patch week windows
- Freeze window: Tue–Thu (release candidates), publish Thu EOD if green.
- Perf gate runs are prioritized during 9a–5p PT; best-effort outside.

2) Dedicated runners for LTS gates
- Reserve 2x H100 runners + 1x A100 runner pool for LTS perf gates only.
- Hard cap LTS benchmark concurrency at 2 (to avoid noisy neighbor / variance).

3) Rerun / flake policy
- If a regression is within threshold + looks like runner contention, allow 1 rerun.
- If still failing: treat as real regression unless Allison/Marcus can attribute to measurement noise.

4) CI contention
- Release-branch unit/integration tests should not share the same GPU queue as perf-canary.
- If we can’t split, we at least need a queue priority rule for runtime-*-lts.

Can Sahana confirm what’s feasible with current GitHub Actions / self-hosted runner setup, and Connor confirm the gating expectations for ENG-4825?

I opened INT-3368 for runner capacity and INT-3302 for secrets/permissions on release branches.

Attachments:
- LTS_PerfGates_CapacityPlan_v3.pdf
- runner-allocation-proposal.csv

Neha Kapoor
Infra / SRE Program Management
Redwood Inference


---
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

This looks directionally right.

For ENG-4825: for LTS publish, we need a “gateable” artifact that is:
- deterministic to the extent possible (same commit, same runner type)
- includes p50/p95 latency + throughput deltas vs baseline
- covers at least 2 canonical workloads + one mixed-length stress case

Re flake policy: +1 on exactly one rerun. After that, release manager should not have to debate. If we need a waiver, it should be explicit + time-boxed.

One question: do we have enough runner headroom to also run scheduler regression suite (ENG-4831) during patch week? If not, I’d rather protect perf gates + minimal release tests, and run scheduler suite overnight.

Connor O’Brien
Release Engineering (Runtime LTS)


---
From: Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 4, 2025 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Feasibility notes:

- We can reserve runners by labeling + restricting which repos/workflows can target them. For perf-canary, we already do this, but it’s currently shared with some ad-hoc benchmarking.
- Splitting GPU queue (CI vs perf) is doable if we provision 1–2 additional runner groups. Otherwise we end up with priority hacks and people bypassing them.

Constraints:
- Today we effectively have 2 H100s and 2 A100s available “most of the time,” but they’re not truly dedicated. Contention spikes on Wed/Thu when a bunch of teams run perf validations.
- Variance correlates with runner contention (co-resident jobs, background pulls). We can reduce that by pinning jobs to bare runners and pre-pulling images.

Actions I propose:
1) Make the LTS perf gate runner group “exclusive” (no ad-hoc workflows). I’ll need approval from Sean to enforce.
2) Add preflight step: verify runner is idle + image layers present before starting the benchmark.
3) For scheduler regression suite: it can run CPU-only except the high-load case. We could do CPU suite always + GPU stress nightly.

INT-3302: I can take point on secrets/permissions. We need a minimal, scoped token for release/* branches to publish artifacts to the staging bucket.

Sahana
CI/Build Engineering


---
From: Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 4, 2025 at 1:02 PM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

+1 to making the perf gate runner group exclusive.

On rerun policy: we should explicitly define what “looks like contention” means, otherwise it turns into bikeshedding. Suggestion:
- If p95 regression is > threshold AND run logs show runner had concurrent jobs OR GPU util was pegged by non-benchmark process => rerun allowed.
- If clean runner + same regression => treat as real.

Also, baseline: we should pin a baseline artifact (commit + container digest) for runtime-1.18-lts so “baseline drift” doesn’t become a recurring argument. Marcus and I can own publishing the baseline digest + expected variance notes.

Allison
Observability / Perf Signals


---
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 5, 2025 at 9:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Approved to enforce exclusivity for LTS perf gate runners during patch week.

Two conditions:
1) Publish a short “how to request ad-hoc bench time” (so folks don’t just repurpose the LTS runners). Point them to a queue and we’ll schedule.
2) If we hit an incident (customer Sev1) we may need to reclaim runners temporarily. Rafael will be the escalation point.

For INT-3368: we should try to add at least one more H100 runner before pilot. Even if it’s only available during business hours, it will reduce the temptation to parallelize on the same machine and induce noise.

Sean Gallagher
SRE (Runtime)


---
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 5, 2025 at 12:07 PM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Thanks — we’ll do both.

I’ll draft the ad-hoc request process and send for review (simple: Slack channel + form; Sahana can route to available runners).

Next steps / owners:
- Runner exclusivity + labels + workflow restrictions: Sahana (ETA Fri)
- Baseline digest + variance notes for runtime-1.18-lts: Allison + Marcus (ETA Mon)
- INT-3368 (add 1 H100 runner / reserve hours): Neha + Sean (target: before pilot)
- INT-3302 (release branch secrets): Sahana (in progress)

Open question for Connor: do we want to enforce “no publish if perf gate missing” (i.e., runner outage blocks)? My recommendation is yes for LTS, but we should document an emergency exception path (Sean + Connor sign-off).

Neha


---
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 4:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Agree: for LTS, “missing perf gate” blocks publish.

Exception path (only for out-of-band security patch):
- Release manager documents reason gate is missing (runner outage / infra incident)
- Require explicit approval from Sean + me in-thread
- Must run a reduced benchmark set ASAP post-publish and be ready to roll back if it’s bad

If we align on this, I’ll add it to the release manager checklist and link INT-3368 / INT-3302 so it stays visible.

Connor

title: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)
preview:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 4, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Connor / Sahana — following up on yesterday’s release sync.

We need to lock a capacity plan for perf-canary + release-branch CI so LTS patch week doesn’t starve mainline CI (and vice versa). Goal is: gates are reliable enough to block an LTS publish, without causing a 24–48h backlog.

Proposal (happy to adjust):

1) Patch week windows
- Freeze window: Tue–Thu (release candidates), publish Thu EOD if green.
- Perf gate runs are prioritized during 9a–5p PT; best-effort outside.

2) Dedicated runners for LTS gates
- Reserve 2x H100 runners + 1x A100 runner pool for LTS perf gates only.
- Hard cap LTS benchmark concurrency at 2 (to avoid noisy neighbor / variance).

3) Rerun / flake policy
- If a regression is within threshold + looks like runner contention, allow 1 rerun.
- If still failing: treat as real regression unless Allison/Marcus can attribute to measurement noise.

4) CI contention
- Release-branch unit/integration tests should not share the same GPU queue as perf-canary.
- If we can’t split, we at least need a queue priority rule for runtime-*-lts.

Can Sahana confirm what’s feasible with current GitHub Actions / self-hosted runner setup, and Connor confirm the gating expectations for ENG-4825?

I opened INT-3368 for runner capacity and INT-3302 for secrets/permissions on release branches.

Attachments:
- LTS_PerfGates_CapacityPlan_v3.pdf
- runner-allocation-proposal.csv

Neha Kapoor
Infra / SRE Program Management
Redwood Inference


---
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

This looks directionally right.

For ENG-4825: for LTS publish, we need a “gateable” artifact that is:
- deterministic to the extent possible (same commit, same runner type)
- includes p50/p95 latency + throughput deltas vs baseline
- covers at least 2 canonical workloads + one mixed-length stress case

Re flake policy: +1 on exactly one rerun. After that, release manager should not have to debate. If we need a waiver, it should be explicit + time-boxed.

One question: do we have enough runner headroom to also run scheduler regression suite (ENG-4831) during patch week? If not, I’d rather protect perf gates + minimal release tests, and run scheduler suite overnight.

Connor O’Brien
Release Engineering (Runtime LTS)


---
From: Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 4, 2025 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Feasibility notes:

- We can reserve runners by labeling + restricting which repos/workflows can target them. For perf-canary, we already do this, but it’s currently shared with some ad-hoc benchmarking.
- Splitting GPU queue (CI vs perf) is doable if we provision 1–2 additional runner groups. Otherwise we end up with priority hacks and people bypassing them.

Constraints:
- Today we effectively have 2 H100s and 2 A100s available “most of the time,” but they’re not truly dedicated. Contention spikes on Wed/Thu when a bunch of teams run perf validations.
- Variance correlates with runner contention (co-resident jobs, background pulls). We can reduce that by pinning jobs to bare runners and pre-pulling images.

Actions I propose:
1) Make the LTS perf gate runner group “exclusive” (no ad-hoc workflows). I’ll need approval from Sean to enforce.
2) Add preflight step: verify runner is idle + image layers present before starting the benchmark.
3) For scheduler regression suite: it can run CPU-only except the high-load case. We could do CPU suite always + GPU stress nightly.

INT-3302: I can take point on secrets/permissions. We need a minimal, scoped token for release/* branches to publish artifacts to the staging bucket.

Sahana
CI/Build Engineering


---
From: Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 4, 2025 at 1:02 PM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

+1 to making the perf gate runner group exclusive.

On rerun policy: we should explicitly define what “looks like contention” means, otherwise it turns into bikeshedding. Suggestion:
- If p95 regression is > threshold AND run logs show runner had concurrent jobs OR GPU util was pegged by non-benchmark process => rerun allowed.
- If clean runner + same regression => treat as real.

Also, baseline: we should pin a baseline artifact (commit + container digest) for runtime-1.18-lts so “baseline drift” doesn’t become a recurring argument. Marcus and I can own publishing the baseline digest + expected variance notes.

Allison
Observability / Perf Signals


---
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 5, 2025 at 9:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Approved to enforce exclusivity for LTS perf gate runners during patch week.

Two conditions:
1) Publish a short “how to request ad-hoc bench time” (so folks don’t just repurpose the LTS runners). Point them to a queue and we’ll schedule.
2) If we hit an incident (customer Sev1) we may need to reclaim runners temporarily. Rafael will be the escalation point.

For INT-3368: we should try to add at least one more H100 runner before pilot. Even if it’s only available during business hours, it will reduce the temptation to parallelize on the same machine and induce noise.

Sean Gallagher
SRE (Runtime)


---
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Reed <marcus.reed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 5, 2025 at 12:07 PM PST
Subject: Re: LTS perf gates + CI capacity plan for patch week (runtime-1.18-lts)

Thanks — we’ll do both.

I’ll draft the ad-hoc request process and send for review (simple: Slack channel + form; Sahana can route to available runners).

Next steps / owners:
- Runner exclusivity + labels + workflow restrictions: Sahana (ETA Fri)
- Baseline digest + variance notes for runtime-1.18-lts: Allison + Marcus (ETA Mon)
- INT-3368 (add 1 H100 runner / reserve hours): Neha + Sean (target: before pilot)
- INT-3302 (release branch secrets): Sahana (in progress)

Open question for Connor: do we want to enforce “no publish if perf gate missing” (i.e., runner outage blocks)? My recommendation is yes for LTS, but we should document an emergency exception path (Sean + Connor sign-off).

Neha

source.subject: Dedicated staged rollouts: customer signals + MVP proposal (asks for default gates + constraints)
source.body:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Dedicated staged rollouts: customer signals + MVP proposal (asks for default gates + constraints)
Attachments: Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Customer_Signal_Summary_2025-02-03.pdf (application/pdf), SUP-28491-notes.txt (text/plain)

Hi all — sharing a quick customer-signal summary for Dedicated staged rollouts + a concrete MVP proposal so we can align this week.

Why now
- We’re seeing repeated escalations when we need to upgrade a Dedicated pool (model version, runtime build, routing policy, or config change). Customers want the same safety controls they have for app deploys: canary, objective gates, and clear rollback.
- Sales is also getting blocked on “how do you do safe upgrades without downtime?” (Lattice AI in particular).

Customer signal (themes)
1) “Canary” / phased rollout for Dedicated pool upgrades
- Lattice AI (HubSpot: company-lattice-ai): asked for staged upgrade of a reserved pool from Model vA → vB. They’re okay with limited exposure but want objective criteria and a stop button.
- EmberSearch (company-embersearch): embeddings-heavy, hates any throughput cliff during changes; wants a way to keep 100% capacity available while testing a small slice.

2) Objective gates + evidence, not “operator eyeballing”
- Customers explicitly called out: error rate, p95/p99 latency, 429/drop rate, GPU saturation/headroom, OOM rate.
- HarborBank (company-harborbank): change management requires an audit trail of who promoted/rolled back + why. They asked whether evidence can be exported.

3) Biggest pain: pool partitioning can reduce effective capacity
- We already saw an early version of this in SUP-28522 (“canary stage causes unexpected 429s”) where a small stage partition created queue depth spikes and backpressure.

4) Deterministic rollback expectations (esp. streaming)
- Multiple customers asked “if we rollback, what happens to in-flight requests?” (we need a clear, documented behavior).

References
- SUP-28491: Dedicated pool upgrade needs phased rollout (escalated via Support)
- SUP-28522: Canary stage causes unexpected 429s on Dedicated (beta issue)
- Folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1rwiD3dRolloutsSignals (internal)

Proposed MVP (to align with Console + Platform)
Goal: staged rollout plans for a Dedicated pool with explicit stages, promotion controls, and gate outcomes that are auditable.

MVP scope proposal:
- Stage model: default stages like 1% → 10% → 50% → 100% OR replica partitions where % is unsafe (small pools)
- Promotion: manual promote button + automatic abort on hard failures; ability to HOLD/freeze
- Gates (Redwood-defined in v1):
  - error_rate (5xx + model errors)
  - p95/p99 latency
  - 429/drop rate
  - saturation/headroom (GPU utilization + queue depth)
  - OOM / GPU health (if available reliably)
- Audit trail: create/update/promote/abort/hold + overrides

Asks (need answers this week so we can lock PRD + begin implementation)
1) Default gates + thresholds (SRE):
- What are the default thresholds we should ship for v1 (by tier, if needed)?
- What evaluation window do you want (5m vs 15m) and minimum sample size before we allow promotion?
- Missing data behavior: do we fail-closed (block promotion) vs fail-open (allow) and under what conditions?

2) Partitioning constraints (Platform/SRE):
- Minimum pool size / minimum headroom to allow a 1% or 1-replica stage without starving capacity?
- Guardrails to prevent fragmentation on small reservations (e.g., if fewer than N replicas, force replica-based stages only)?

3) Console UX assumptions (Marissa):
- OK to show “gate unknown” when telemetry is stale, with explicit callout and block promotion unless override?
- Do we need customer-facing vs admin-only UI for force-promote/hold in beta?

If you can reply with guidance (even rough) today, I’ll incorporate into the PRD draft and circulate.

Thanks,
Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Product, Dedicated
Redwood Inference


---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:04 AM PT
Subject: Re: Dedicated staged rollouts: customer signals + MVP proposal (asks for default gates + constraints)

Agree on urgency.

SRE POV on gates/thresholds (initial take; we can refine in the SLO alignment doc):

Promotion windows / sample size
- Prefer 15m window for p95/p99 + error rate to avoid promoting on a transient lull.
- BUT: for low-traffic pools, 15m may still be too few requests; we should require min request count (or min token count) before calling a gate “evaluated.” Otherwise gates sit “unknown” forever.

Missing telemetry behavior
- Strong preference: fail-closed for auto-promotion (i.e., do not promote if gate data is stale/absent), and allow a manual override with explicit reason + audit event.
- Exception: if only a non-critical gate is missing (e.g., a “nice to have” GPU health signal), we can treat it as “skipped” rather than hard-fail. We need to define which gates are critical.

Default gate suggestions (rough)
- Error rate: keep very strict; we should abort quickly on sustained 5xx / model load errors.
- 429/drop: treat as high-severity for Dedicated because customers interpret it as downtime.
- Latency: use relative regression rather than absolute if we can (baseline vs previous stage) — absolute thresholds vary a lot by model.

Capacity / partitioning constraints
- We need hard guardrails. The SUP-28522 pattern is real: a canary partition that’s too small can cause queue depth spikes.
- Suggest: do not allow a stage that maps to < 1 full replica in a region (or < X headroom). If a pool has only a few replicas, we should force replica-based stage splits (e.g., 1 replica canary) and disallow 1% traffic splits.

If you want, I can put together a one-pager with proposed defaults + “critical vs non-critical” gate list by EOD tomorrow.

Rafael
Rafael Mendes
SRE
Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 12:37 PM PT
Subject: Re: Dedicated staged rollouts: customer signals + MVP proposal (asks for default gates + constraints)

+1 to the MVP framing.

A few platform constraints to consider as we lock “default stages”:

- Percentage splits vs replica partitions: percent-based routing is easy to express, but for Dedicated we can’t pretend capacity is continuous. If a stage implies less than a replica worth of capacity, you get exactly the SUP-28522 symptom.

- Multi-region pools: for pools spanning regions, stages need to be defined per-region or we need a consistent rule like “promote stage only when all regions pass gates.” Otherwise we’ll have weird partial states and hard-to-explain customer experience.

- Determinism + auditability: every promote/abort/hold needs to be idempotent. We should use idempotency keys and optimistic concurrency so we don’t double-promote under retries.

- Missing data: I’m aligned with fail-closed for automated progression. For manual promote, we can allow override with a required reason string and emit that into audit logs.

Action items I propose:
1) Rafael: draft defaults (thresholds + windows + min sample size) and a “gate criticality” classification.
2) Lauren/Marissa: confirm how we want to represent “unknown” gates in Console and whether it blocks promotion by default.
3) Connor: sanity-check whether any of this conflicts with the beta enablement plan / migrations.

Priya
Priya Natarajan
Engineering, Platform (Sponsor)
Redwood Inference

title: Dedicated staged rollouts: customer signals + MVP proposal (asks for default gates + constraints)
preview:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Dedicated staged rollouts: customer signals + MVP proposal (asks for default gates + constraints)
Attachments: Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Customer_Signal_Summary_2025-02-03.pdf (application/pdf), SUP-28491-notes.txt (text/plain)

Hi all — sharing a quick customer-signal summary for Dedicated staged rollouts + a concrete MVP proposal so we can align this week.

Why now
- We’re seeing repeated escalations when we need to upgrade a Dedicated pool (model version, runtime build, routing policy, or config change). Customers want the same safety controls they have for app deploys: canary, objective gates, and clear rollback.
- Sales is also getting blocked on “how do you do safe upgrades without downtime?” (Lattice AI in particular).

Customer signal (themes)
1) “Canary” / phased rollout for Dedicated pool upgrades
- Lattice AI (HubSpot: company-lattice-ai): asked for staged upgrade of a reserved pool from Model vA → vB. They’re okay with limited exposure but want objective criteria and a stop button.
- EmberSearch (company-embersearch): embeddings-heavy, hates any throughput cliff during changes; wants a way to keep 100% capacity available while testing a small slice.

2) Objective gates + evidence, not “operator eyeballing”
- Customers explicitly called out: error rate, p95/p99 latency, 429/drop rate, GPU saturation/headroom, OOM rate.
- HarborBank (company-harborbank): change management requires an audit trail of who promoted/rolled back + why. They asked whether evidence can be exported.

3) Biggest pain: pool partitioning can reduce effective capacity
- We already saw an early version of this in SUP-28522 (“canary stage causes unexpected 429s”) where a small stage partition created queue depth spikes and backpressure.

4) Deterministic rollback expectations (esp. streaming)
- Multiple customers asked “if we rollback, what happens to in-flight requests?” (we need a clear, documented behavior).

References
- SUP-28491: Dedicated pool upgrade needs phased rollout (escalated via Support)
- SUP-28522: Canary stage causes unexpected 429s on Dedicated (beta issue)
- Folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1rwiD3dRolloutsSignals (internal)

Proposed MVP (to align with Console + Platform)
Goal: staged rollout plans for a Dedicated pool with explicit stages, promotion controls, and gate outcomes that are auditable.

MVP scope proposal:
- Stage model: default stages like 1% → 10% → 50% → 100% OR replica partitions where % is unsafe (small pools)
- Promotion: manual promote button + automatic abort on hard failures; ability to HOLD/freeze
- Gates (Redwood-defined in v1):
  - error_rate (5xx + model errors)
  - p95/p99 latency
  - 429/drop rate
  - saturation/headroom (GPU utilization + queue depth)
  - OOM / GPU health (if available reliably)
- Audit trail: create/update/promote/abort/hold + overrides

Asks (need answers this week so we can lock PRD + begin implementation)
1) Default gates + thresholds (SRE):
- What are the default thresholds we should ship for v1 (by tier, if needed)?
- What evaluation window do you want (5m vs 15m) and minimum sample size before we allow promotion?
- Missing data behavior: do we fail-closed (block promotion) vs fail-open (allow) and under what conditions?

2) Partitioning constraints (Platform/SRE):
- Minimum pool size / minimum headroom to allow a 1% or 1-replica stage without starving capacity?
- Guardrails to prevent fragmentation on small reservations (e.g., if fewer than N replicas, force replica-based stages only)?

3) Console UX assumptions (Marissa):
- OK to show “gate unknown” when telemetry is stale, with explicit callout and block promotion unless override?
- Do we need customer-facing vs admin-only UI for force-promote/hold in beta?

If you can reply with guidance (even rough) today, I’ll incorporate into the PRD draft and circulate.

Thanks,
Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Product, Dedicated
Redwood Inference


---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:04 AM PT
Subject: Re: Dedicated staged rollouts: customer signals + MVP proposal (asks for default gates + constraints)

Agree on urgency.

SRE POV on gates/thresholds (initial take; we can refine in the SLO alignment doc):

Promotion windows / sample size
- Prefer 15m window for p95/p99 + error rate to avoid promoting on a transient lull.
- BUT: for low-traffic pools, 15m may still be too few requests; we should require min request count (or min token count) before calling a gate “evaluated.” Otherwise gates sit “unknown” forever.

Missing telemetry behavior
- Strong preference: fail-closed for auto-promotion (i.e., do not promote if gate data is stale/absent), and allow a manual override with explicit reason + audit event.
- Exception: if only a non-critical gate is missing (e.g., a “nice to have” GPU health signal), we can treat it as “skipped” rather than hard-fail. We need to define which gates are critical.

Default gate suggestions (rough)
- Error rate: keep very strict; we should abort quickly on sustained 5xx / model load errors.
- 429/drop: treat as high-severity for Dedicated because customers interpret it as downtime.
- Latency: use relative regression rather than absolute if we can (baseline vs previous stage) — absolute thresholds vary a lot by model.

Capacity / partitioning constraints
- We need hard guardrails. The SUP-28522 pattern is real: a canary partition that’s too small can cause queue depth spikes.
- Suggest: do not allow a stage that maps to < 1 full replica in a region (or < X headroom). If a pool has only a few replicas, we should force replica-based stage splits (e.g., 1 replica canary) and disallow 1% traffic splits.

If you want, I can put together a one-pager with proposed defaults + “critical vs non-critical” gate list by EOD tomorrow.

Rafael
Rafael Mendes
SRE
Redwood Inference

- Determinism + auditability: every promote/abort/hold needs to be idempotent. We should use idempotency keys and optimistic concurrency so we don’t double-promote under retries.

- Missing data: I’m aligned with fail-closed for automated progression. For manual promote, we can allow override with a required reason string and emit that into audit logs.

2) Lauren/Marissa: confirm how we want to represent “unknown” gates in Console and whether it blocks promotion by default.
3) Connor: sanity-check whether any of this conflicts with the beta enablement plan / migrations.
